greenhouse_4231638009.title: Leasing Hub Associate - Sandy Spring, GA
greenhouse_4231638009.rewritten_description:
This isn't a traditional leasing consultant role. No property tours. No move-in paperwork. No resident calls about maintenance. The Leasing Hub Associate at Cortland sits in a centralized hub and works leads remotely, connecting prospective residents to the right communities across Cortland's portfolio before they ever set foot on a property.

The core of the job is volume and conversation. You're fielding and initiating 40 to 50 lead contacts per day through calls, emails, and texts sourced from RealPage. Your job is to qualify those prospects, understand what they actually need in an apartment home, and hand off warm, scheduled appointments to the on-site leasing teams. You're not closing leases yourself. You're filling the top of the funnel and making sure the on-site teams aren't working cold traffic.

That distinction matters. Strong candidates in this role tend to come from inside sales or high-volume customer service backgrounds where consistent outreach is the job, not the exception. If you're used to reactive service work and waiting for inbound volume, the daily outbound pace here will feel like a shift. People who thrive are the ones who treat every follow-up as a real sales touchpoint, not just a task to check off.

You'll need a genuine command of Cortland's communities, pricing, and amenities because prospects will ask specific questions and vague answers lose appointments. Cortland uses OneSite for property management operations, and familiarity with L2L (Lead to Lease) is a plus. Bilingual fluency is welcome given the diverse renter base across Cortland's markets.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • At least 2 years of customer service experience, with a preference for direct sales backgrounds
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills across phone, email, and text
  • Comfort with high outbound contact volume daily
  • Proficiency in OneSite; L2L experience preferred
  • English fluency required; bilingual candidates encouraged

Cortland is a vertically integrated multifamily operator founded in 2005, ranked among the top 10 multifamily owners in the US. They handle design, construction, and management in-house, which gives their leasing teams more product knowledge to work with than you'd find at a typical third-party management shop. The Sandy Springs, GA location puts you close to Cortland's Atlanta headquarters.

For someone building a career in multifamily, this role develops skills that transfer directly into leasing management, regional sales support, or corporate training. The exposure to lead management systems and portfolio-wide traffic patterns gives you a broader view of operations than a single-property leasing role typically allows.

greenhouse_4224586009.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant - Cortland Vesta
greenhouse_4224586009.rewritten_description:
Leasing consultants at Cortland aren't just showing floor plans and handing out applications. The expectation is closer to a sales rep with a pipeline to manage, conversion targets to hit, and a brand standard to uphold on every single tour. At Cortland Vesta in Birmingham, that means you're the first and most important impression a prospective resident gets of the community.

What the Role Actually Requires

Occupancy doesn't manage itself. You'll own the full leasing cycle: generating and nurturing leads, running tours, handling objections, and closing leases. CRM discipline matters here. Cortland tracks lead activity, conversion metrics, and pipeline health, so your follow-up habits show up in the numbers. A prospect who toured on Tuesday and hasn't heard from you by Thursday is a missed opportunity reflected in your traffic-to-lease ratio.

The consultative side of the role is real, not just a buzzword. You're trying to understand what a prospect actually needs, a shorter commute, a quieter unit, a specific move-in date, and match them to the right home rather than pitching the same script to everyone. That approach tends to produce better close rates and stronger resident retention downstream.

Beyond leasing, you'll support community marketing and social media presence and help plan resident events that keep occupancy sticky. Renewals start with the experience you create before someone even signs their first lease.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Cortland operates as a vertically integrated platform with in-house construction, design, and management. That means the product is consistent and the brand standard is specific. You're not selling a commodity. The candidates who do well in this environment tend to be organized and coachable in equal measure. Organized because pipeline management falls apart without disciplined follow-up. Coachable because Cortland tracks KPIs closely and will give you direct feedback on what's working and what isn't.

  • 6 or more months of sales, customer service, or performance-driven experience preferred
  • Comfortable being measured against leasing goals and conversion targets
  • CRM experience or a clear ability to learn and use one consistently
  • Strong follow-up habits and time management
  • Professional presentation that matches a Class A resident experience
  • Ability to handle objections without getting rattled

Where This Role Can Go

Leasing consultant positions at well-capitalized operators like Cortland are a legitimate path into assistant property management and eventually community management. The skills you build here, pipeline management, resident relationship-building, sales under pressure, transfer cleanly into NOI-focused management roles. Birmingham's multifamily market has seen steady development activity, which means there's room to grow within the portfolio if you perform.

Cortland was founded in 2005 and is recognized as one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country. It's an equal opportunity employer and participates in E-Verify.

greenhouse_4232283009.title: Maintenance Technician-Cortland Wren Northlake
greenhouse_4232283009.rewritten_description:
Cortland sits among the top ten multifamily owners and operators in the country, and their maintenance teams carry real weight in how that reputation holds up at the property level. This Maintenance Technician role at Cortland Wren Northlake in Charlotte puts you at the center of day-to-day operations for a well-resourced organization that builds, owns, and manages its own communities.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day moves fast and rarely follows a script. You might start the morning pulling HVAC preventative maintenance checks, shift to a plumbing diagnosis in a ground-floor unit by midday, and finish the afternoon closing out a backlog of work orders before an on-call rotation kicks in. The variety is real. Strong technicians here aren't just wrench-turners; they're the people who catch a failing gate latch or a flickering corridor light before it becomes a liability issue.

Core responsibilities include:

  • Diagnosing and repairing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliance, flooring, and general building system issues
  • Identifying and correcting unsafe conditions quickly, including broken lighting, damaged entry points, and structural hazards
  • Running scheduled preventative maintenance on HVAC units and building equipment
  • Monitoring building and security systems and reporting on their condition
  • Keeping parts and supply inventory stocked to avoid delays on work orders
  • Participating in on-call rotations and responding to after-hours emergencies
  • Documenting completed work and flagging recurring issues to the supervisor

Resident interactions happen constantly in this role. How you handle those moments, whether it's a quick hallway update or a tense conversation about a delayed repair, shapes how residents feel about the community overall. That's not a soft skill. It's a core part of the job.

What You'll Bring

  • EPA certification (Type I, II, or Universal) required
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • 1 to 3 or more years in maintenance, facilities, or multifamily housing
  • Solid technical skills across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and appliance repair
  • Ability to manage multiple work orders and shift priorities without dropping the ball
  • Comfort with physical work in varying conditions, including lifting, ladder work, and outdoor tasks
  • Willingness to participate in on-call schedules

Where This Role Can Take You

Maintenance Technician is one of the clearest career-building positions in multifamily. Technicians who develop strong HVAC and electrical depth, learn to lead make-ready turns efficiently, and build a track record of low delinquency on work orders are the candidates properties fight over for Lead Technician and Service Manager roles. At a vertically integrated company like Cortland, where construction and operations sit under the same roof, there's also exposure to capital improvement projects and renovation work that most third-party management shops simply don't offer. The skills you build here, technical range, resident communication, preventative maintenance discipline, compound across every future property you work on.

Charlotte's multifamily market has stayed active, and Cortland has a visible footprint in the region. For a technician looking to grow inside a structured, well-resourced organization rather than bounce between smaller operators, this is a practical step in the right direction.

Role Overview As a Maintenance Technician, you ensure the community remains safe, functional, and well‑maintained by diagnosing and repairing a wide range of building and apartment systems. You provide timely, high‑quality service to residents, support preventative maintenance programs, and help preserve the property’s physical assets through consistent care and attention. Diagnose, repair, and resolve maintenance issues involving electrical, plumbing, HVAC systems, appliances, flooring, stairs, railings, and other building components. Correct unsafe conditions such as broken lights, damaged gates, open holes, or other hazards, ensuring issues are addressed quickly and safely. Support asset preservation by monitoring and reporting on the condition of building and security systems such as automatic doors and surveillance cameras. Schedule and perform preventative maintenance on HVAC units, equipment, and building systems to improve performance and reduce downtime. Maintain appropriate inventory of supplies and spare parts to ensure timely completion of work orders. Respond to on ‑ call rotations and emergency maintenance situations, assisting with both resident and property needs. Provide friendly, professional service when interacting with residents and team members. Document completed work and report recurring issues or safety concerns to your supervisor. Qualifications EPA certification required (Type I, II, or Universal). High school diploma or equivalent required. 1–3+ years of experience in maintenance, facilities, or multi ‑ family housing. Strong technical skills in electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliance repair, and general building systems. Ability to prioritize tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple work orders. Strong attention to detail and problem ‑ solving abilities. Ability to safely operate tools and maintenance equipment. Good communication and customer ‑ service skills. Ability to perform physical work including lifting, climbing ladders, and working indoors/outdoors in various weather conditions. Willingness to participate in on ‑ call schedules and respond to emergencies. greenhouse_4231635009.title: Senior Leasing Hub Associate - Sandy Springs, GA
greenhouse_4231635009.rewritten_description:
This is a centralized, hub-based leasing role. You're not sitting at a property front desk greeting walk-ins. You're working remotely or from a central office, handling a high volume of inbound and outbound leads across multiple Cortland communities in the market.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day runs on lead volume. Expect to manage 40 to 50 leads daily through RealPage's OneSite platform, working calls, emails, and texts from prospects who are at various stages of their apartment search. Your job is to qualify those leads, match each prospect to the right Cortland community based on their needs, and hand off warm, appointment-ready prospects to the onsite leasing teams.

You need to know Cortland's communities well. Pricing, floor plans, amenities, and local positioning. Prospects will ask detailed questions, and you can't afford to stumble. Cortland provides training and site visits to build that knowledge, but you have to put in the work to retain it.

This is a sales role. Outbound activity is a real expectation, not an afterthought. If you're more comfortable waiting for inbound traffic than initiating contact, this isn't the right fit.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in customer service, with a clear sales component
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, bilingual candidates welcome
  • Comfort with OneSite (RealPage); familiarity with L2L is a plus
  • Fast, accurate typing and solid computer skills overall
  • Ability to sustain energy and quality across a high volume of daily contacts

Where This Role Can Take You

Hub leasing roles are relatively new in multifamily, and associates who excel here build a skill set that translates well. You'll develop deep knowledge of how leads move through a leasing funnel, how to handle objections at scale, and how to coordinate across multiple properties simultaneously. That cross-community exposure is something most traditional leasing consultants don't get. Strong performers here often move into senior leasing, leasing manager, or training roles within larger operators like Cortland.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator in the US, vertically integrated with in-house design and construction. The organization is large enough to offer real career movement, and this role puts you in front of the leasing function at the portfolio level from day one.

greenhouse_4231362009.title: Service Manager - Cortland Sun River
greenhouse_4231362009.rewritten_description:
Cortland runs a vertically integrated operation, which means a Service Manager here carries more weight than the same title at a typical third-party managed community. You're not just keeping the lights on. You're protecting an asset that Cortland owns, designs, and operates under one roof, and your team's performance feeds directly into resident retention, NOI, and the long-term value of the property.

What This Role Actually Involves

The core of this position is maintenance leadership at a multifamily community. You'll diagnose and resolve complex issues across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and structural systems, and you'll make sure your technicians can do the same. That means hands-on training, daily task assignment, work order oversight, and stepping in yourself when the situation calls for it. Make-readies, punch list inspections, preventive maintenance scheduling, and on-call coverage all fall within your scope.

Beyond the technical side, you're managing the full maintenance operation: vendor coordination, parts and supply inventory, budget tracking, and safety compliance. Cortland expects you to stay ahead of problems rather than react to them. If your team is consistently catching issues early, before a resident submits a request, you're doing the job right.

What Cortland Needs From You

  • 3 to 5 or more years of maintenance experience covering carpentry, plumbing, electrical, and HVAC. Multifamily background is strongly preferred.
  • Universal EPA certification and CPO certification, both required at hire.
  • Valid Class E driver's license.
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, ADA, EPA regulations, and Fair Housing requirements.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead, train, and mentor a maintenance team.
  • Comfort managing a high volume of details: open work orders, vendor schedules, budget line items, and preventive maintenance records simultaneously.
  • Availability for evenings, weekends, and on-call rotation.
  • Physical ability to lift, climb, and work in both indoor and outdoor conditions.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

A lot of applicants can show technical competence. Fewer can show they've built a team culture where technicians take ownership and flag problems proactively without being asked. At Cortland's scale, reactive maintenance is expensive. The Service Managers who advance here are the ones who run tight preventive maintenance programs, keep delinquent work orders from piling up, and communicate clearly with both their team and residents. If you've managed a maintenance crew through a heavy capex project or a lease-up push, that experience translates directly.

Cortland was founded in 2005 and has grown into one of the ten largest multifamily owners and operators in the country. The in-house structure means decisions move faster and resources are generally more accessible than in a typical fee management setup. For a Service Manager who wants visibility and room to grow into a regional or director-level maintenance role, that structure matters.

greenhouse_4226737009.title: Service Technician - Cortland Aertson
greenhouse_4226737009.rewritten_description:
Multifamily operators at scale run into a specific staffing challenge: they need technicians who can handle the full range of day-to-day maintenance competently, but who also fit into a structured, systems-driven operation. Cortland, one of the ten largest apartment owners and operators in the country, built its maintenance program around that reality. This role at Cortland Aertson in Nashville puts you inside that system.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day moves between scheduled work orders and whatever the property surfaces. That means diagnosing and repairing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, and fixtures, then correcting unsafe conditions before they become liability issues. You're also responsible for keeping preventive maintenance on schedule, which matters more than most technicians realize. Equipment that gets skipped on PM cycles fails faster, and emergency repairs always cost more than preventive ones.

Security systems, automatic doors, and surveillance cameras fall under your watch too. You'll manage your own parts inventory to keep work order completion times tight. On-call rotations are part of the role, so resident and property emergencies after hours are a real expectation, not a footnote.

  • EPA certification required (Cortland offers in-house certified proctors if you need CPO or CPR)
  • 1 to 3 years of experience in facilities or multifamily maintenance
  • Hands-on competency across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and general repairs
  • Comfortable identifying and correcting unsafe conditions independently
  • Able to manage parts inventory and coordinate with on-site staff on work completion

Why This Role Has Real Upside

Nashville's multifamily market has seen significant lease-up activity over the last several years, and that growth brought a wave of Class A properties that now need experienced maintenance staff to keep them performing. Working at a Cortland asset gives you exposure to a vertically integrated operation where design, construction, and property management sit under the same roof. That structure means decisions move faster, resources are more accessible, and the training programs are built in-house rather than outsourced.

Cortland offers a personalized growth plan alongside mentorship and leadership support, which is worth taking seriously if you're thinking about where this role leads. Technicians who build their certification portfolio and develop strong diagnostic skills typically move into lead or supervisor roles within a few years. Monthly commission is included in the compensation package alongside full benefits.

The candidates who stand out here aren't just technically solid. They communicate well with residents, close work orders without being chased, and treat preventive maintenance as seriously as emergency calls. If you approach the job that way, the path forward inside a company this size is straightforward.

greenhouse_5981357004.title: Regional Manager, Affordable Housing
greenhouse_5981357004.rewritten_description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Regional management in affordable housing sits at the intersection of regulatory precision, people leadership, and financial oversight. These aren't parallel tracks. They run together every single day. If you're someone who can hold a LIHTC compliance review in the morning, coach a struggling community manager through a performance conversation at noon, and then dig into a variance report before the day ends, this role was built around your skill set.

Asset Living is a third-party management firm ranked in the NMHC top 50, operating across multifamily, student, build-to-rent, and affordable housing portfolios nationwide. This Regional Manager position covers a portfolio of affordable communities in the Arlington, WA area, and the work is genuinely specialized. Affordable housing regional management isn't just conventional property oversight with a compliance checkbox added. The regulatory layer is substantial, and it shapes everything from how your teams process applications to how you interact with funding agencies and ownership groups.

What You'll Actually Be Managing

On the compliance side, you'll be responsible for ensuring your sites follow all LIHTC and Section 8 policies correctly. That means monitoring annual recertification processes, conducting file reviews, performing semi-annual physical inspections, and maintaining relationships with regulatory agencies. Fair housing and Equal Housing Opportunity requirements aren't background noise here. They're front and center.

Personnel management takes up a real portion of your time. You'll screen, hire, train, and develop on-site staff across your portfolio. You'll also handle the harder parts: performance documentation, terminations when necessary, salary review approvals, and timesheet oversight. Asset Living expects its regional leaders to handle these situations with consistency and clear communication up the chain to senior leadership and HR.

Financially, you're accountable for NOI across the portfolio. That means building annual operating budgets, monitoring income and expense variances, flagging trends, and making capital improvement recommendations. You'll review vendor contracts, approve invoices, and ensure month-end close and ownership reporting happen accurately and on time.

Leasing strategy is part of the picture too, including developing marketing plans, ensuring staff leasing techniques are effective, and building resident retention programs that reduce turnover. Every unnecessary turn costs money and disrupts residents who often have limited housing options. That context matters in affordable.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Affordable housing compliance knowledge is the real differentiator. Candidates who've managed conventional portfolios but have no LIHTC or Section 8 experience face a steep learning curve in this seat. Regulatory agencies don't grade on a curve, and neither do audits. If you've already worked through file reviews, corrective action plans from agencies, or managed a lease-up under affordable program requirements, that background is genuinely valuable here.

Beyond compliance, the strongest regional managers in affordable housing tend to be people who lead with clarity rather than authority. Your on-site teams deal with complex resident situations, income verification stress, and documentation demands that go well beyond what conventional teams typically handle. They need a regional who sets clear expectations, provides real coaching, and doesn't disappear when things get hard.

  • Demonstrated LIHTC and/or Section 8 compliance experience
  • Regional or multi-site property management background
  • Strong financial acumen including budget development and NOI management
  • Experience hiring, coaching, and managing on-site property staff
  • Familiarity with regulatory agency relationships and reporting
  • Solid understanding of fair housing law

Asset Living's AMO accreditation through IREM signals a commitment to professional standards that tends to attract operators who take the craft seriously. If you want a portfolio where the work is substantive and the compliance stakes are real, this is worth a closer look.
greenhouse_5981357004.description_text: Company Overview Asset Living is a third-party management firm and a proven partner in fostering thriving communities nationwide. Founded in 1986, Asset Living has decades of experience delivering exceptional value to our partners. Since the beginning, our undeniable passion has driven our organic growth from a small property management firm to one of the most trusted allies in real estate. Asset Living's growing portfolio includes a multitude of properties across the country that span the multifamily, single-family home rentals, affordable housing, build-to-rent, active adult, and student housing divisions. Asset Living is a fast-growing company made up of talented individuals from diverse backgrounds that differentiate us and help us drive innovation and results for our clients and communities across the US. At Asset Living, we wholeheartedly believe 'Together We Lead, Together We Succeed. We empower each other to lead by example, collaborate, and evolve – inspired by our belief that we can continually improve as individuals and as an organization. Together, we create an environment where every voice is heard, every idea is valued, and every individual experiences opportunities to grow as our company grows. When you join Asset Living, you become part of a dynamic team that thrives on unity, unique talents, and a universal culture of winning. Recognized as one of the nation's Best and Brightest Places to Work and ranked in the NMHC top 50 professionals, join Asset Living because of its reputation as the most trusted partner in real estate, workplace culture, and growth opportunities. Asset Living is a member of The Institute of Real Estate Management (IREM) and is recognized as an Accredited Management Organization (AMO). Together, we lead the way, and together, we achieve excellence in all that we do. Join a workplace where success is a collective journey and leadership is a shared responsibility. As a Regional Manager specializing in affordable housing you will manage all phases of the operations, including personnel, leasing, maintenance, financial, administration & risk management. As an offsite leader, you will supervise all aspects of the property and staff to ensure compliance with Asset’s company policies and procedures, safety and fair housing guidelines, and liability concerns. Essential Duties & Responsibilities Affordable Housing Ensure compliance with all Equal Housing Opportunity/Affirmative Fair Housing laws. Ensure proper LIHTC and/or Section 8 policies and procedures are followed. Monitor LIHTC Application and Annual Recertification processes. Regulatory Compliance – Ensures compliance with regulatory and funding requirements, contracts, and reporting Conduct files reviews, physical inspections, and interacts with regulatory agencies. Conduct semi-annual inspections as required. Personnel Management Use consistent techniques & company directives to screen, hire, train, coach, and develop on-site staff Ensure effectiveness of staff through ongoing training, coaching, counseling, and guidance in compliance with Asset training benchmarks Deal effectively and consistently with performance problems; document adequately, communicate with direct supervisor and HR and terminate appropriately when necessary Prepare request for a salary increase for Community Manager and assist with other team members if necessary; sign off on all requests from the site Ensure all counseling statements, written evaluations, and salary requests are provided for review to the Regional & Senior Vice President and delivered to the Human Resource department Approve all timesheets and ensure hours indicated are correct, vacation and sick time reported, and signatures available Deal effectively and consistently with performance problems; document adequately, communicate with Regional & Senior Vice President and HR and terminate appropriately when necessary Promote harmony and quality job performance of staff through support and effective leadership Ensure staff compliance and consistency with Company policies and procedures Financial Management Achieve the highest possible net operating income through the implementation of effective cost control and revenue & leasing improvement; identify trends and recommend strategies and adjustments Develop yearly operating budgets/forecasts Provide monthly written evaluation of income and expense line items that are significantly over budget Provide Capital improvement suggestions for the future of the site Monitor all proposals and contracts for large projects at sites and check work in progress Monitor & maintain the timely receipt and reconciliation of deposits, rent collections, and charges to ensure submitted on a timely basis Monitor & approve the timely receipt, reconciliation, and coding of all vendor invoices Ensure property closeout is completed on time and ownership financial reports are accurate Strategic Leasing Management Develop yearly marketing plan and utilize marketing strategies & systems Provide marketing strategy to generate rentals or for rent increases Ensure staff leasing techniques are effective in obtaining closure, follow up procedures met, signing/documentation of leases, and reporting systems are accurate and up to date Deal with resident complaints, concerns, and requests to ensure resident satisfaction Develop and implement resident retention programs (i.e., resident functions, special promotions, monthly newsletter, etc.) Effectively show, lease, and move in prospective residents Administrative & Maintenance Management Ensure all administrative & leasing reporting is accurate, complete, and submitted on a timely basis Assist with the emergency team for the property; ensures proper response and handling of all property emergencies with staff, residents, buildings, etc. within company guidelines to minimize liability Manage excellent customer service and monitor service request turnaround and responsiveness of maintenance staff Maintain property appearance and ensure repairs are noted and completed on a timely basis (this requires regular property inspections and tours) Plan for and utilize property resources, equipment and supplies economically (i.e., obtaining bids/pricing from vendors, suppliers, and contractors, dealing with inadequate service, etc.) Give direction to and monitor maintenance, construction, and rehabilitation activities to ensure quality and expediency (i.e., “walk” units to ensure make-ready and work orders are completed) Travel This position entails travel, estimated at 25-50% of work time annually. Travel may be required for property visits, conferences, training sessions, or other business-related activities. Education & Experience Affordable housing experience which includes LIHTC Prior experience as a Regional Manager High School Diploma or Equivalent; Bachelor’s degree preferred or four years experience in the housing industry; or one to two years related experience and/or training; or equivalent combination of education and experience Certified Apartment Manager (CAM) or Accredited Resident Manager (ARM) preferred Ability to understand and perform all on-site software functions; basic computer skills required Must have basic knowledge of Fair Housing Laws and OSHA requirements This job description should not be considered all-inclusive. It is merely a guide of expected duties. The employee understands that the job description is neither complete nor permanent and may be modified at any time. At the request of their supervisor, an employee may be asked to perform additional duties or take on additional responsibilities without notice. This is a safety-sensitive position and may be subject to additional compliance requirements. This compensation range is specific to the State of Washington and includes the range of factors considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; certifications; etc. We would not anticipate that the individual hired into this role would be near the top end of the compensation range, but that decision will be dependent on the facts associated with each specific individual’s relevant experience for the role.  A reasonable estimate of the range is below. Washington Pay Rate $95,000 — $120,000 USD

greenhouse_7713719003.title: Lead Maintenance Technician - Riata
greenhouse_7713719003.rewritten_description:
The Technical Range This Role Demands

Lead Maintenance Technician at Riata isn't a one-trade job. You're moving across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, carpentry, and mechanical repairs on any given day, and the depth of that range matters. A technician who's strong in one area but thin in the others will feel it fast. The residents at this Austin community notice response times, and the team you're leading will take cues from how you handle the tough calls: the compressor that fails on a Friday afternoon, the water heater that goes out over a holiday weekend.

Olympus Property has been recognized as a National Apartment Association Top Employer three years running (2023, 2024, 2025). That's not just a badge. It signals that the company is watching how people are treated at the team level, not just the resident level. For a lead tech, that matters because you're hiring, training, and evaluating the people under you. Your ability to develop the team directly affects how well the property runs.

What You're Actually Responsible For

This role carries supervisory weight alongside hands-on work. You're conducting routine inspections of the property's interior and exterior, coordinating exterior vendors like landscaping, managing proper trash removal procedures, and keeping the Community Compliance Manual current. You're also the person who makes sure tools, equipment, and materials are stored safely and that OSHA standards aren't just a poster on a wall.

  • Two to five years of multi-family maintenance experience, with prior supervisory time preferred
  • Working knowledge across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and carpentry
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or CPO certifications may be required based on property needs
  • Comfort with operations technology platforms and basic computer communication
  • Flexibility for days, evenings, weekdays, and weekends
  • High school diploma or equivalent required

The compensation range sits between $80,000 and $90,000, with monthly and quarterly bonuses available. That's a real number for Austin's market and reflects the scope of what this role covers.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Austin's rental market runs hot. Properties in this city deal with high turn volume and residents who have options. A lead tech who treats make-readies as just a checklist will produce units that feel like it. The best candidates here think about the punch list differently: they know which items a prospective resident will notice during a tour and they sequence the work accordingly.

The supervisory piece is where many technicians hit a ceiling. Doing the work well and teaching others to do it well are different skills. If you've coached a junior tech through a difficult repair, run a team through a safety protocol, or held a performance conversation, those experiences translate directly to what Olympus is looking for here. The culture emphasis on teamwork and respect isn't just language. It shows up in how leads are expected to run their teams day to day.

Respect for our employees. Courtesy for our partners. We pride ourselves on being #OlympusProud, and to us, culture is everything. greenhouse_7851131.title: Property Manager
greenhouse_7851131.rewritten_description:
Weinstein Properties has been family-owned for over 70 years, and that history shows up in how they actually operate. This isn't a company where a Property Manager gets a key, a login, and a "figure it out." Corporate teams stay close, processes are clearly defined, and the expectation is that you'll run your community with both independence and accountability. That combination is harder to find than it sounds.

The open role is at Bexley Duluth, a Class A apartment community in Duluth, GA. Weinstein manages more than 60 communities across Virginia, North Carolina, Texas, Tennessee, and Georgia, so the infrastructure behind this position is real. You're not operating in isolation.

What the role actually demands day to day: you're the first person residents reach when something has escalated beyond the leasing staff's ability to resolve it. You're coordinating with your Maintenance Supervisor on project timelines and community standards. You're reviewing your key performance metrics, managing delinquency, watching occupancy trends, and making sure your team has the direction they need to perform. The administrative side is genuine. So is the people side. You'll need to hold both at once without letting either slip.

This role builds something specific. Property Managers who come through highly structured, operations-driven companies tend to develop a sharper understanding of process design and accountability systems than those who work in more autonomous environments. That translates directly if you ever move into a regional or multi-site role, because you'll already know how to think about consistency at scale, not just execution at a single property.

What separates strong candidates here from average ones isn't just experience. It's the ability to work within a defined structure without feeling constrained by it. Weinstein's model is high-touch by design. Managers who chafe under frequent corporate communication or who prefer to build their own systems from scratch will likely find this environment frustrating. Managers who appreciate clear expectations and see structured support as an asset rather than a leash will thrive.

What you'll need to be considered

  • Prior property management experience, required
  • Class A multifamily background, strongly preferred
  • Demonstrated ability to lead and develop on-site teams
  • Strong communication skills and a genuine sense of urgency
  • Comfort managing high request volume from both residents and corporate simultaneously
  • Detail-oriented, organized, and solutions-focused by default

Compensation is competitive and tied to experience. Quarterly and annual bonus opportunities are part of the package. If you've put in the work to understand how a well-run apartment community actually operates, and you want to do that work inside a company that will back you up with real support, this is worth a close look.

greenhouse_8522679002.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_8522679002.location: The Republic at Lubbock, Lubbock, Texas, United States
greenhouse_8522679002.rewritten_description:
Student housing runs on a different clock than conventional multifamily. Lease-up seasons are intense, turn periods are compressed and chaotic, and the residents you're serving are often renting their first apartment without a parent holding their hand. The Experience Team Member role at The Republic at Lubbock sits right in the middle of all that energy, and it's genuinely a different animal than a standard leasing consultant position at a garden-style or Class A conventional community.

Scion is one of the larger operators in the student housing space, and this property serves the Texas Tech market. The ETM role blends leasing, marketing, resident engagement, and light property administration into a single position. That's not a criticism. It's just the reality of how student housing communities tend to staff their front-line teams. You'll be giving tours, handling package retrieval, jumping on social media to push engagement, helping plan resident events, and occasionally covering after-hours lockout calls. Some days you'll do all of that before lunch.

What makes someone genuinely good at this role isn't just friendliness, though that matters. It's the ability to shift gears without losing your footing. A prospective resident and their parent walk in right as you're logging packages and a maintenance request just came through the queue. You handle all three without any of them feeling like an afterthought. That composure under layered demands is what separates strong ETMs from ones who burn out by November.

The on-call rotation is worth taking seriously before you apply. After-hours lockout duty is part of the job, not an occasional exception. If that's a dealbreaker, it's better to know now than to find out mid-semester.

What Scion is looking for

  • A genuine customer-first mindset, not just a friendly face at the front desk
  • Strong communication skills across a diverse resident population, including international students
  • Flexibility to work weekends, holidays, and through turn periods when the property flips hundreds of units in a matter of days
  • Self-discipline and reliability, because this role involves a lot of independent judgment in real time
  • Comfort with social media and digital communication as actual leasing and engagement tools

For someone early in a property management career, this role builds a real foundation. The combination of leasing, marketing, and operations exposure you get in student housing is hard to replicate elsewhere. ETMs who develop their sales instincts and learn to read traffic patterns and objections often move into assistant manager or leasing director tracks faster than their peers at conventional properties. The pace of student housing accelerates that learning curve considerably.

This is a part-time position based on-site at The Republic at Lubbock. Work authorization in the United States is required. Visa sponsorship is not available.

greenhouse_8523140002.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_8523140002.location: Redpoint Wilmington, Wilmington, North Carolina, United States
greenhouse_8523140002.rewritten_description:
Student housing runs on energy, consistency, and genuine connection. This leasing consultant role at Redpoint Wilmington puts all three of those skills to work every single day. The Scion Group calls this position an Experience Team Member, and that title is more accurate than "leasing consultant" in a meaningful way: closing a lease is only part of the job.

Skills This Role Requires on Day One

You'll need real communication ability before you walk in the door. Not just friendliness, but the capacity to read a prospective resident's hesitation, understand the objection underneath it, and respond without sounding scripted. Student housing prospects often bring parents into the conversation, which means you're sometimes selling to two different audiences at once. You'll also need to stay organized under pressure. Package logging, maintenance follow-ups, office opening and closing procedures, and social media engagement can all land on your plate in the same afternoon shift.

  • Comfortable giving community and amenity tours to prospects with varied expectations
  • Able to document and communicate sales objections clearly to management
  • Familiar with social media platforms and capable of engaging authentically, not just posting
  • Available during Turn periods, summers, and holidays, which are the highest-traffic seasons in student housing
  • Willing to participate in after-hours on-call rotation for lockout situations

Skills This Role Builds Over Time

If you're early in a property management career, this position is one of the better training grounds available. Student housing lease-ups are fast and high-volume. You'll learn how to manage traffic, track objections, and contribute to local marketing strategy in ways that a conventional multifamily leasing role might not expose you to as quickly. The planning and execution of resident engagement events builds event coordination skills that carry into assistant manager and community manager roles later. Working inside a structured system like Scion's also gives you exposure to how national operators standardize culture and process across a large portfolio, which is a transferable skill regardless of where your career goes next.

Student housing has its own rhythm. The academic calendar drives nearly everything: move-in, move-out, occupancy pressure, and renewal urgency all cluster around predictable windows. Understanding that rhythm early in your career gives you a framework for thinking about leasing strategy that's genuinely useful across property types.

What Makes Strong Candidates Stand Out Here

The candidates who thrive in this role aren't necessarily the most polished. They're the ones who stay consistent when the environment shifts quickly, who follow up without being reminded, and who treat every tour like it matters even on a slow Tuesday. Reliability counts more here than charisma. Scion operates at scale in the student living sector, and they're building teams that can execute a process well, then improve it. If you come in curious about why things work the way they do, and you're willing to share that feedback upward, you'll get noticed.

This position is based at Redpoint Wilmington. Hours include standard business hours plus an on-call rotation. Work authorization in the United States is required; visa sponsorship is not available.

greenhouse_8523191002.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_8523191002.location: The Grove at San Marcos, San Marcos, Texas, United States
greenhouse_8523191002.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

The Grove at San Marcos sits in a college town, which means this role moves at a pace that's genuinely different from conventional multifamily leasing. You're not just showing apartments. On any given day you might be walking a prospective resident through a unit, troubleshooting a package pickup situation, responding to a maintenance follow-up, and then pivoting to help plan a resident engagement event before the afternoon is over. The variety is real. So is the pressure during peak periods.

Scion calls this position an Experience Team Member, and the name is intentional. Your core job is to make sure people feel taken care of at every touchpoint, from the first tour to the moment they sign and beyond. You'll handle leasing conversations, manage social media content, assist with community events, and cover after-hours lockout calls on a rotating basis. That last part matters. It's not a hypothetical. You need to be comfortable with an on-call component built into the schedule.

You'll also be a direct line of feedback to the management team. When you're hearing the same objection from three different prospects in a week, your job is to surface that clearly. That kind of loop between the leasing floor and leadership is how Scion adjusts its approach, so your observations carry weight.

What You Bring

  • A customer-first instinct that holds up under pressure, not just when things are easy
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills across a diverse resident and prospect base
  • Flexibility to work weekends, holidays, and Turn periods (student housing Turn is an all-hands season, no exceptions)
  • Self-discipline and reliability without needing constant supervision
  • Comfort with social media as a professional communication tool
  • Work authorization in the United States (visa sponsorship is not available)

Context Worth Knowing

Student housing leasing runs on a different rhythm than conventional multifamily. Instead of rolling month-to-month traffic, you're often working toward a specific lease-up deadline tied to the academic calendar. Turn, the period when hundreds of residents move out and a new group moves in within days, is one of the most logistically intense stretches in this business. Candidates who have lived through a Turn at a student property, or who clearly understand what that means operationally, tend to hit the ground faster than those coming from conventional communities.

What separates a strong ETM from an average one isn't just friendliness. It's the ability to stay organized and professional when the office is loud, the phone keeps ringing, and three people are waiting at the desk at the same time. That's the version of this job you should be ready for.

greenhouse_8523267002.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_8523267002.location: Alight Stillwater, Stillwater, Oklahoma, United States
greenhouse_8523267002.rewritten_description:
Student housing leasing moves differently than conventional multifamily. Renewals happen months in advance, traffic spikes around academic calendars, and Turn is a real operational event, not just a figure of speech. The Experience Team Member role at Scion's Alight Stillwater property sits right at the center of that cycle.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Most of your time goes toward prospect and resident interaction. Tours, follow-ups, answering questions about the community, and helping convert interest into signed leases. You'll also assist with social media content and local marketing efforts, so comfort with digital communication matters here.

On the administrative side, you're logging packages, keeping the office and common areas organized, scheduling maintenance requests, and following up to make sure work orders get closed out. After-hours lockout coverage is part of the rotation. That's not a footnote; it's a real part of the job.

You'll also support planning and executing resident engagement events and other initiatives management puts in place. The scope shifts depending on the season, and that's by design.

What the Role Requires

  • Strong interpersonal skills across a diverse, college-age population
  • Availability during summers, holidays, and Turn periods (non-negotiable in student housing)
  • Reliability and self-discipline without constant supervision
  • Comfort with a workload that changes week to week
  • Eligibility to work in the United States (no visa sponsorship available)

Where This Role Can Take You

ETM positions at student housing operators like Scion are a practical entry point into property management careers. People who do this job well tend to move into leasing lead, assistant manager, or community manager roles within a few years. The skills you build here, including sales technique, resident relations, and operational awareness, transfer cleanly to conventional multifamily or purpose-built student housing at any scale.

One honest note: student housing Turn is intense. Moving hundreds of residents out and back in over a compressed summer window is demanding work. Candidates who've worked retail, hospitality, or event coordination during high-volume periods tend to handle it well.

greenhouse_8523207002.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_8523207002.location: Alight 12th Avenue, Norman, Oklahoma, United States
greenhouse_8523207002.rewritten_description:
It's 7:30 a.m. on a Monday after a long weekend, and the work order queue already has six requests waiting. That's the reality of maintenance in student housing. Residents expect fast turnarounds, and at a property like Alight 12th Avenue, keeping up means being organized, skilled, and genuinely comfortable with variety in your day.

What This Role Actually Involves

The Scion Group operates in the student living sector, and this Facilities Technician position at their Norman, Oklahoma property covers the full range of general maintenance work. You're responding to service requests, completing turns to get units ready for incoming residents, and running preventive maintenance on equipment and systems. That last part matters more than people think. PM work keeps warranty coverage intact and extends the useful life of HVAC units, water heaters, and other capital equipment that's expensive to replace.

Day-to-day tasks include:

  • Diagnosing and completing minor repairs across plumbing, electrical, carpentry, appliances, and painting
  • Logging all work in Entrata and a CMMS platform, with accurate notes on parts used and follow-up needed
  • Inspecting vacated units and common areas, flagging issues for the Facilities Supervisor
  • Maintaining pool chemistry and cleanliness (CPO certification required or obtainable within six months)
  • Completing PM checklists including filter changes, smoke and CO2 alarm checks, coil cleaning, and equipment lubrication
  • Pressure washing, grounds upkeep, snow removal, and assisting with resident event setups
  • Serving on-call for after-hours emergencies outside of approved PTO

You'll work under a Facilities Supervisor and coordinate daily assignments with them. The documentation side of this job is real. Sloppy or incomplete work orders cause problems downstream, so attention to detail in your paperwork matters as much as your wrench skills.

What Scion Is Looking For

Candidates need a high school diploma or GED (trade school is a plus), plus one to three years of hands-on general maintenance experience. EPA 608 Type I certification is required on day one. A valid driver's license, the physical ability to stand for a full shift and lift up to 100 pounds, and a flexible schedule that includes weekends and emergency calls are all non-negotiable.

Comfort with technology matters here. Scion uses Entrata for property management and a CMMS for work order tracking. If you've used either before, great. If not, you need to be willing to learn both quickly and use them consistently.

Scion cannot sponsor work visas, so candidates must already be authorized to work in the U.S.

One thing worth being honest about: student housing runs on tight turn schedules, especially heading into a new academic year. The pressure to get units punched and ready on time is real, and it hits hardest in summer. Technicians who thrive here tend to be people who can prioritize under pressure without cutting corners on documentation or safety. That combination is rarer than it sounds, and it's what separates solid performers from the ones who wash out by August.

Benefits include health, dental, and vision insurance, 401k with matching, paid time off, paid parental and maternal leave, a discretionary annual bonus, and learning reimbursement. This is a non-exempt, hourly position.

greenhouse_8523589002.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_8523589002.location: West Edge, Colorado Springs, Colorado, Untied States
greenhouse_8523589002.rewritten_description:
Student housing maintenance is its own discipline. The rhythms are different from conventional multifamily: you're working against academic calendars, managing high-volume turns in a compressed summer window, and serving residents who may have never lived away from home before. At West Edge in Colorado Springs, The Scion Group is looking for a Facilities Technician who understands that the work goes beyond fixing things. It's about keeping a community functional for students who depend on it.

The core of this role is hands-on general maintenance. You'll respond to service requests, complete preventive maintenance on building systems, and prepare units for turnovers. That means basic plumbing, appliance repair, carpentry, painting, electrical and lighting work, and HVAC filter changes and coil cleaning. You'll document everything in Entrata and a CMMS platform, which matters more than it sounds. Clean, timely records protect the property, support warranty compliance, and give your supervisor visibility into what's done and what still needs attention. Sloppy documentation creates problems downstream.

Common area upkeep is part of the daily rotation, not an afterthought. Light fixture checks, pool chemistry, pressure washing, snow removal, drainage maintenance, and escorting vendors through the property all show up on the list. Student housing properties see heavy use. Things wear out faster, get reported more frequently, and need faster turnaround than a typical apartment community. If you've worked in student housing before, you already know this. If you haven't, it's worth knowing going in.

This is an on-call position. After-hours emergencies happen, and you'll be expected to respond when directed. That's a real part of the job, not a footnote.

What You'll Need

  • High school diploma, GED, or trade school credential plus at least one to three years of general maintenance experience
  • EPA 608 Type I certification
  • Certified Pool Operator (CPO) certification, or a genuine plan to obtain it within six months
  • Valid driver's license and the ability to travel periodically
  • Physical stamina to stand eight or more hours per day and lift up to 100 pounds
  • Comfort with computer systems, including maintenance management software
  • Flexible availability for weekends and emergency response

What separates a strong candidate here from an average one isn't just technical range, though that matters. It's follow-through. Student residents notice when a request goes unanswered. They notice when a common area looks neglected. The technicians who build real trust in this kind of environment are the ones who close the loop, communicate clearly, and treat every interaction with a resident like it represents the property's reputation. Because it does.

Technicians who perform well in student housing often build skills that transfer directly into facilities supervisor roles or portfolio-level maintenance positions. The variety of work here, HVAC, pool systems, carpentry, software documentation, vendor coordination, gives you a broader foundation than a single-discipline trade would. If you're early in your maintenance career and want to grow, this kind of environment accelerates that development faster than most.

greenhouse_8522903002.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_8522903002.location: Redpoint Raleigh, Raleigh, North Carolina, United States
greenhouse_8522903002.rewritten_description:
The Craft Behind This Role

Maintenance work at a student housing property runs on a specific set of skills, and Scion's Redpoint Raleigh community needs someone who's genuinely good at all of them. Basic appliance repair, plumbing and fixture work, carpentry, painting, HVAC filter maintenance, coil and condenser cleaning. Not just familiar with these things. Actually competent. The turns here move fast, and a make-ready that drags because one tech can't handle a leaking faucet or a tripped breaker creates a ripple effect across the whole schedule.

Documentation is a real part of this job, not an afterthought. You'll be expected to submit daily work logs that are legible, specific, and complete, including what was done, what parts were used, and what still needs follow-up. Sloppy paperwork in maintenance creates liability and budget headaches. If that kind of discipline isn't already a habit for you, it needs to become one quickly.

You'll also be carrying out preventive maintenance tasks, which is where a lot of technicians either shine or fall short. Anyone can respond to a broken thing. The techs who build a real career in this industry are the ones who catch problems before they become work orders.

What Student Housing Actually Demands

Student housing has its own rhythm. Turns are concentrated and intense, typically timed to academic calendars, which means the pressure to complete punch list items and get units rent-ready isn't spread out across the year the way it might be at a conventional apartment community. You'll need a flexible schedule and genuine willingness to handle after-hours and weekend calls when they come up. That's not a maybe. It's part of the job.

The resident population here is largely 18 to 22 year olds, many of whom have never lived outside their parents' home. Customer service matters. A lot. How you show up at a door, how you explain what you're doing, how you follow through on a request affects how residents feel about the community. Scion takes that seriously.

A Certified Pool Operator credential is required here, or at least a commitment to obtain one within six months. If you don't have it yet, get moving on it early. It's not a heavy lift, but it does take some lead time.

What Sets This Opportunity Apart

Scion has built a national footprint specifically in the student housing sector, which means their SOPs, training programs, and operational expectations are calibrated for this asset class rather than borrowed from conventional multifamily. For a technician who wants to grow, that structure matters. Techs who demonstrate reliability and skill at the property level have a clear path toward lead or supervisor roles, particularly as companies like Scion continue to expand their portfolios.

  • High school diploma or GED required
  • CPO certification required (or obtained within six months)
  • Valid driver's license
  • Ability to lift up to 100 lbs. and stand for extended periods
  • Basic computer proficiency for work order and documentation systems
  • On-call availability outside normal business hours
  • Work authorization in the United States (visa sponsorship not available)

greenhouse_7713830003.title: Business Manager - Olympus at Memorial
greenhouse_7713830003.rewritten_description:
Olympus Property has earned the National Apartment Association's Top Employer recognition three years running (2023, 2024, and 2025). That's not a marketing footnote. It reflects something real about how this company operates, and it matters for anyone evaluating where to take their career next.

The Business Manager role at Olympus at Memorial is, in plain terms, the property manager position. You're running the asset. That means owning the financial performance, the team, the resident experience, and the vendor relationships all at once. You'll work directly with Regional Leadership to set strategy, build annual business plans, and analyze monthly performance against budget projections. If you're the kind of person who reads a T-12 the way other people read a news feed, you'll be comfortable here.

Day to day, this role pulls you in a lot of directions. Morning might be a budget conversation with your Regional VP. Afternoon could be a property inspection, a vendor negotiation on a service contract, or coaching a leasing consultant through a difficult prospect interaction. The financial side and the people side don't take turns. They happen simultaneously, and the best candidates for this role don't just tolerate that reality. They're wired for it.

This is a Class A community, and Olympus has a preference for candidates who've managed at that level before. Luxury assets carry specific expectations around curb appeal, amenity presentation, and resident communication. The bar for "market-ready condition" is higher, and so is the resident's willingness to escalate when it isn't met. That's not a reason to be discouraged. It's just the honest context for what you're walking into.

The team you'll lead includes both office and maintenance staff. That cross-functional dynamic is worth thinking about. Strong candidates here don't just manage their leasing team and hand maintenance off to a supervisor. They understand enough about make-ready timelines and punch list quality to have real conversations with their maintenance lead, hold the standard, and back their team up when vendors push back on scope or pricing.

What You'll Need

  • At least two years of direct multifamily property management experience, with Class A or luxury background preferred
  • Familiarity with RealPage, Knock, or Onesite platforms (strongly preferred, not just a nice-to-have)
  • Comfort with financial reporting, budget forecasting, and performance analysis
  • Full-time availability Monday through Friday, with flexibility for resident events and after-hours situations when they arise
  • Experience supervising and developing both leasing and maintenance staff

The base pay range is $70,000 to $80,000 annually, with monthly commissions and quarterly and annual bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401(k) with employer matching, tuition reimbursement, an apartment rental allowance, and a sabbatical program, among others. Olympus also invests in leadership development through their Budget Camp and Leadership Conferences, which matters if you're thinking about where this role leads. Business Managers at companies like Olympus are a natural pipeline for Regional Manager positions, particularly for candidates who demonstrate strong financial acumen alongside team development skills.

If the culture-first language in property management job postings usually makes you skeptical, that's fair. But Olympus builds recognition programs, career pathing, and team events into the actual structure of the job, not just the recruiting copy. Whether that fits how you work is worth thinking through before you apply.

The Business Manager (Property Manager) manages all aspects and operations of the property, including creating budgets and financials, ensuring exceptional resident satisfaction, and fostering successful relationships with vendors, partners, and investors. greenhouse_7714060003.title: Leasing Consultant - The Preserve at Greenway Park
greenhouse_7714060003.rewritten_description:
Leasing Consultant roles at apartment communities are part sales, part customer service, part administrative grind. At The Preserve at Greenway Park in Casper, WY, you're the first face a prospective resident sees, and the last person who makes sure their move-in paperwork is actually ready before they show up with a moving truck.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

Most of your time splits between working the leasing floor and managing what happens around it. You're greeting walk-ins, taking calls, and guiding prospects through unit tours while explaining floor plan differences, pricing, and availability. You're closing leases, yes, but you're also entering data, preparing lease files, and coordinating move-in logistics so nothing falls through the cracks on key day.

Beyond the sales cycle, you're helping existing residents when concerns come up, which means some days feel more like problem-solving than selling. You'll also spend time outside the property visiting local businesses and apartment locators to build referral relationships and drive traffic back to the community. Resident events are part of the job too, including some after-hours availability on a monthly basis. It's not constant, but it's real, and you should expect it.

The schedule includes both Saturday and Sunday availability. That's non-negotiable here, and worth knowing upfront rather than finding out later.

What Olympus Is Looking For

  • At least one year of apartment leasing experience, or comparable time in a customer-facing role in retail, hospitality, or restaurants where you were genuinely selling, not just assisting
  • Comfort with a sales conversation, including closing without being pushy about it
  • Organized enough to handle lease paperwork, move-in prep, and database entry without things slipping
  • A genuine interest in helping people find a place to live, not just filling units

Pay starts between $15 and $18 per hour depending on experience, and the role includes monthly leasing commissions and resident renewal bonuses, so your actual take-home has real upside tied to your performance. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401(k) with employer matching, tuition reimbursement, pet insurance, an apartment rental allowance, and a sabbatical program, among others.

Where This Role Can Take You

Olympus has been recognized by the National Apartment Association as a top employer three years running (2023, 2024, 2025), and they're transparent about career pathing being part of how they operate. Leasing Consultant is a well-worn entry point into property management. Strong performers here typically move into Senior Leasing, Assistant Manager, or eventually Property Manager roles, especially at a company that promotes from within. The skills you build here, closing leases, reading residents, managing traffic and follow-up, translate directly into the operational side of running a property.

If you want a role where showing up with energy and follow-through actually shows up in your paycheck, this one is structured to reward that.

greenhouse_7851575.title: Pool Attendant
greenhouse_7851575.rewritten_description:
Seasonal, outdoor, and genuinely straightforward. Pool attendant roles at apartment communities like Bexley Westshore exist because residents expect a clean, safe, well-managed amenity experience on their days off, and that doesn't happen without someone present and paying attention.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

You're the person on the pool deck during the hours it matters most: weekend afternoons and Friday evenings when resident traffic peaks. Your job is to make sure only authorized residents and their guests are using the pool, that the deck and bathrooms stay clean and trash-free, and that community rules get enforced calmly and consistently. You'll also help close down the area at the end of each shift.

Occasionally, you may walk through other amenity spaces like the gym or business center to do a quick spot check. Nothing complicated, but it does require someone who stays alert and doesn't need to be told twice to handle something they notice on their own.

The schedule is fixed and part-time: Friday evenings and Saturday and Sunday afternoons, totaling around 20 hours per week. The role runs through September or October, with some flexibility if your schedule changes before then. This is a seasonal position. It's not designed to become full-time, and Weinstein Properties is upfront about that.

Who Fits This Role Well

  • Reliable. Residents count on amenities being properly staffed, and showing up consistently matters more than almost anything else here.
  • Comfortable working independently without a supervisor nearby at all times
  • Able to enforce rules with residents in a firm but non-confrontational way
  • Physically comfortable being outdoors for extended periods in warm weather
  • CPR or First Aid certified (a plus, not a requirement; this is not a lifeguard position)
  • Has reliable transportation to get to the property on time every shift

Pay runs $15 to $17 per hour. A background check is part of the process.

One Thing Worth Knowing

Enforcing pool rules is the part of this job that trips people up. Most residents are fine. Some aren't happy when told their guest count is over the limit or that the pool is closing. If you're the kind of person who can hold a boundary without making it a confrontation, you'll do well here. That skill, handling low-stakes but uncomfortable resident interactions, is something that transfers directly if you ever want to move into a leasing or assistant manager role at a residential community down the road.

Weinstein Properties manages a number of communities across the Southeast, so if you perform well and show genuine reliability, there's context for future opportunities within the company even if this specific role is seasonal.
greenhouse_7851575.description_text: Seasonal Pool Attendant(s) Needed! Looking for a fun summer job? This is a great seasonal opportunity if you enjoy spending time outside. Our pool attendants help monitor pool activity during the spring and summer during busy hours. Work now through September/October* at Bexley Westshore in Cumming, GA. Can be flexible if you need to end earlier. Job duties include : Monitor pool use for authorized residents and their guests Maintain cleanliness of area around pool deck Emptying trash around the pool deck and pool bathrooms Enforcing community pool rules Assist with closing down pool at the end of shift And may also include: Spot checking amenity areas such as the gym and business center Schedule: 20 hours per week available with the following schedule: Friday 6pm-10pm Saturday 2pm-10pm Sunday 2pm-10pm Pay: $15-17/hr Successful candidates will be responsible, reliable and able to work independently. CPR/First Aid certification a plus, but not required - this is not a lifeguard position. Reliable transportation required. Background check conducted. * We are only looking for a pool attendant for the busy months, this is a seasonal role and not year round.

greenhouse_5120302007.title: Leasing Consultant - Dylan Point Loma
greenhouse_5120302007.rewritten_description:
It's a Saturday afternoon, and a couple walks in without an appointment, having driven past Dylan Point Loma twice before finally stopping. You've got ten minutes to turn that curiosity into a signed lease. That's the job.

What This Role Actually Involves

MG Properties has been acquiring, developing, and managing apartment communities across the Western U.S. for over 30 years. Dylan Point Loma in San Diego is one of those communities, and this Leasing Consultant position sits at the front line of keeping occupancy where it needs to be. You're the first impression, the tour guide, the application processor, and the move-in coordinator, all at once.

Day to day, you're greeting and qualifying prospective residents, walking them through units, and converting traffic into signed leases. You'll prepare and execute lease agreements, maintain accurate rental files, and enter data into the property management system with enough precision that nothing falls through the cracks at month-end. You'll also coordinate with the maintenance team to make sure new residents move into a clean, punch-list-complete unit. When current residents come to you with complaints or maintenance concerns, you handle them professionally and follow through.

Saturdays are required. Point Loma sees weekend traffic, and that's when leasing decisions often get made.

What You Bring

  • At least one year of property management or leasing experience (preferred, not required if your sales background is strong)
  • Genuine sales ability and customer service instincts, not just a friendly personality
  • Comfort with leasing software and accurate data entry
  • Professional communication, written and verbal
  • Availability to work Saturdays consistently
  • Willingness to work within a team that expects follow-through, not just front-desk charm

Compensation runs $21 to $22 per hour, with monthly and quarterly bonuses tied to performance. MG Properties also offers company profit sharing, a 401(k) with employer match, medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid holidays, sick time, and rental discounts for employees.

One thing worth knowing about MG Properties specifically: they promote from within at a rate that's notable for a company their size. A Leasing Consultant who hits occupancy targets, keeps delinquency low, and handles resident relations well is exactly the profile that moves into an Assistant Manager seat. The administrative work you do here, organizing files, processing applications, closing leases cleanly, builds the operational foundation that makes that next step a realistic one, not just a talking point in an interview.

greenhouse_5120378007.title: Maintenance Technician - Sterling Village Apartments
greenhouse_5120378007.rewritten_description:
Maintenance Technicians at apartment communities wear a lot of hats. On any given day, you're the person a resident trusts to fix what's broken, the teammate keeping the property presentable, and the on-call responder when something goes wrong at 10pm. At a property like Sterling Village Apartments in Vallejo, that range of responsibility is exactly what the job looks like.

What the Work Actually Involves

MG Properties is a well-established regional operator with over 30 years managing apartment communities across the Western U.S. They're looking for a Maintenance Technician who can handle the full scope of property upkeep, not just one or two trades. That means service requests across plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and appliances, plus grounds and common area cleaning, painting, and apartment turns. Turns are worth calling out specifically: getting a unit make-ready between residents requires attention to detail, a solid punch list mindset, and the ability to move efficiently without cutting corners.

You'll also participate in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies. That's a real commitment, and it's worth knowing going in. It's not every night, but when it's your week, it's your week.

  • Complete resident maintenance service requests across all major trades
  • Perform apartment turnovers and make-ready work
  • Handle appliance repairs, painting, and general upkeep
  • Maintain grounds, offices, and common areas
  • Participate in on-call emergency rotation

What MG Properties Is Looking For

Two or more years of hands-on apartment maintenance experience is preferred, ideally on a property of 200 units or larger. That scale matters because larger communities generate more concurrent work orders, and you need to be able to triage and prioritize without constant direction. Multi-trade competency is required, not optional. Customer service attitude is listed as a key qualifier, and they mean it. Residents interact with maintenance staff more directly than almost anyone else on a property team, and how you show up at someone's door sets the tone for how they feel about where they live.

Reliable transportation is required. You'll need to get yourself to the property and, depending on the on-call situation, potentially respond from home.

Compensation and Team Culture

The pay range is $25 to $26 per hour depending on experience, with monthly bonuses and company profit sharing on top of base wages. The benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401(k) with employer match, paid time off, paid holidays, paid sick time, and rental discounts. That last one is meaningful if you're local to the Vallejo area and looking to reduce your own housing costs.

MG Properties talks about employee development as a real priority, not just a line in a job description. For a technician with broad trade skills, that can translate into a path toward lead maintenance or even a maintenance supervisor role as the company continues to grow its Western U.S. portfolio. The skills you build managing turns, handling complex service requests, and working across trades are directly transferable to higher-level maintenance leadership positions. Companies like this tend to promote from within when they find people who show up consistently and communicate well with both residents and management.

If you've got solid hands-on experience, a calm approach to resident interactions, and you're comfortable being part of an on-call rotation, this is a role worth taking seriously.
