greenhouse_5201997008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5201997008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has a reputation in this industry for running tight communities and investing in the people who keep them running. This Maintenance Technician opening in Fort Lauderdale is a ground-level role in the truest sense: you're the person residents call when something goes wrong, and you're the reason a turned unit looks ready for a new resident to sign a lease and move in with confidence.

The work is what you'd expect from a solid mid-size property management company. Service ticket resolution, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair. Turns and make-readies. Grounds upkeep. You'll also carry your share of the on-call rotation, which means the occasional evening or weekend call-out is part of the deal. That's not a small thing. If after-hours availability is a dealbreaker for you, this role isn't the right fit. If you're already used to it, you know it's just part of working in residential maintenance.

One thing worth noting: Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year. For a technician who doesn't yet have it, that's real money and a real credential. It opens doors to more complex HVAC work and, frankly, to higher-paying roles down the road. Companies that pay for certifications are showing you something about how they treat their maintenance staff.

What they're looking for

  • Some background in building maintenance or a trades education, beginner to intermediate skill level across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Ability to handle on-call emergencies and participate in the rotation
  • Physical capability to lift and exert force regularly throughout a shift (25 to 50 lbs frequently)
  • Reliable personal transportation for getting to the property and responding after hours
  • Comfort with basic software and mobile apps used for work orders and maintenance tracking
  • Clear written and verbal communication, professional presentation

The salary range sits between $51,000 and $53,000, plus bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates a strong candidate here isn't just technical skill. It's the combination of solid hands-on ability and genuine customer service instincts. In residential property management, a technician who fixes things correctly and leaves the resident feeling heard is worth considerably more to an operation than one who only does half of that. Bozzuto properties tend to skew toward quality communities where resident experience is taken seriously. That culture starts with who they put in front of residents every day.

greenhouse_5200104008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5200104008.rewritten_description:
It's 7 a.m. on a Monday. A resident submitted a ticket over the weekend: no hot water. You're the first person they see, and how you handle the next 30 minutes sets the tone for their entire week.

Bozzuto is hiring a Maintenance Technician for their community in Burke, VA. This is a hands-on, full-cycle maintenance role covering everything from daily service requests to unit turns and grounds upkeep.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most of your day runs on service tickets. HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair. You diagnose the problem, fix it correctly, and close it out. Residents notice when you do it fast and when you do it right. Both matter here.

Unit turns are a regular part of the job. When a resident moves out, you go in, assess the punch list, and get the apartment ready for the next move-in. You're not just patching walls. You're resetting the unit to a standard that justifies the rent.

General upkeep rounds out the role: building cleanliness, grounds maintenance, and snow removal when the season calls for it. You'll also rotate on-call for after-hours emergencies. That part's non-negotiable, and it's worth understanding upfront before you apply.

What You Need to Bring

  • Hands-on experience in building maintenance or a trades education background
  • Beginner to intermediate skills across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Ability to lift and carry 25 to 50 pounds frequently as part of daily work
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and on-call response
  • Basic computer skills for work order systems and communication
  • Strong communication, a professional appearance, and a genuine service mindset

You don't need to be a master of every trade on day one. Bozzuto is looking for someone with solid fundamentals and the willingness to keep learning. After your 12-month anniversary, they'll cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. That's a real credential that transfers anywhere in this industry.

The salary for this role is $50,000, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

One thing worth noting for anyone thinking about career trajectory: technicians who build strong diagnostic skills and a reliable on-call track record tend to move into Lead Tech or Service Manager roles faster than those who only handle routine tickets. If that path interests you, this is a reasonable starting point with a company that has the portfolio size to promote from within.

greenhouse_5201739008.title: Multi-Site Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5201739008.rewritten_description:
Multi-site maintenance roles exist because not every property generates enough volume to justify a dedicated full-time technician. Bozzuto fills that gap by deploying technicians across multiple communities, which means your day stays varied and your skills get tested across different building systems, resident needs, and property conditions. If you thrive on range rather than routine, this structure suits you well.

This position covers properties in the Santa Monica area. Your core work centers on service ticket resolution, turn work on vacant units, preventative maintenance, and general upkeep across the buildings you're assigned. On the technical side, you'll be working across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level. You don't need to be a licensed master tradesperson on day one, but you do need a real foundation to draw from. Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year, so if you don't have it yet, that's a concrete skill this role builds for you.

What This Role Requires vs. What It Builds

Coming in, you need reliable personal transportation (essential for multi-site coverage and on-call response), the physical capacity to handle moderate to heavy lifting regularly, and genuine comfort with software and mobile apps used to manage work orders. Communication matters here more than people expect. You're interfacing with residents directly, and how you handle a frustrated tenant mid-turn is as important as how you fix the faucet.

What the role builds is just as meaningful. Working across multiple properties accelerates your exposure to different building types, systems, and management styles faster than a single-site role would. Technicians who handle multi-site work tend to develop stronger diagnostic instincts because they can't rely on familiarity with one building's quirks. That breadth translates well if you eventually want to move into a lead tech, service manager, or property maintenance supervisor position.

What the Schedule Actually Looks Like

  • Weekends and evenings are part of the rotation, not occasional exceptions
  • On-call emergency response is required, so proximity and reliable transportation aren't optional
  • Make-ready work on vacant units runs alongside active service tickets, so prioritization is a daily skill
  • Grounds maintenance and general cleanliness are part of the job, not beneath it

The salary range sits between $51,000 and $55,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The EPA 608 certification sponsorship after 12 months is worth noting specifically because refrigerant certification opens doors in HVAC work that are otherwise closed.

Strong candidates for this role aren't necessarily the most experienced technicians in the pool. They're the ones who communicate clearly with residents, show up reliably for on-call situations, and treat a punch list on a vacant unit with the same care they'd give a complex repair. That service mentality, paired with solid technical fundamentals, is what separates a good fit here from someone who'll struggle with the multi-site pace.

greenhouse_5200008008.title: Property Manager
greenhouse_5200008008.rewritten_description:
What This Role Demands Daily

This is a full P&L ownership role. You're responsible for occupancy, NOI, client reporting, and team performance at a Bozzuto community in Sunnyvale. The job pulls on financial acumen, leadership, and sales skills in roughly equal measure. If any one of those three is weak, the other two won't compensate.

Day to day, you're reading your T-12, reviewing delinquency, tracking traffic and conversion, and making sure your make-ready pipeline stays clean. You're also the primary relationship owner with the client, which means your financial and operational reports need to be accurate and on time, not approximate and late. Bozzuto manages for third-party clients, so that client relationship carries real weight.

One weekend per month is required. You get two weekdays off in exchange. That's a fair trade, but it's worth knowing upfront.

Skills That Drive Performance Here

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience, plus five or more years in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • Demonstrated P&L management and budget accountability
  • Yardi proficiency preferred; comfort with financial reporting is non-negotiable
  • Strong written and verbal communication, particularly with clients and ownership groups
  • Active real estate license, or willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire
  • A proven record of developing and retaining on-site team members

What separates strong candidates from average ones here is the ability to hold the financial side and the culture side simultaneously. Some managers are sharp on budgets but checked out on their team. Others are beloved by residents but can't explain a variance report. Bozzuto places real emphasis on both, and their client-facing reporting expectations will expose gaps quickly.

What's Distinctive About This Role

Sunnyvale sits in the heart of Silicon Valley, where Class A apartment demand correlates tightly with tech hiring cycles. A property manager here needs solid competitive market intelligence because concession strategies and renewal pricing can shift fast when major employers announce layoffs or expansions. Knowing your submarket isn't optional.

Bozzuto's culture is well-documented in the industry. They invest in career development and tuition reimbursement, and they promote from within more consistently than many large operators. For a property manager who wants a path toward regional or portfolio oversight, the infrastructure is there. The salary range is $80,000 to $90,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that, plus a benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 20 days of PTO, and 401k with company match.

This isn't a role for someone who wants to delegate and disappear. Bozzuto's model expects managers to work alongside their teams, not above them. If that's how you already operate, you'll fit here quickly.

greenhouse_5202295008.title: Resident Experience Associate
greenhouse_5202295008.rewritten_description:
The Skills This Role Puts to Work Every Day

This is a front-line role where people skills aren't just helpful, they're the job. As a Resident Experience Associate with Bozzuto at their Columbia, MD community, you'll work Monday through Friday from 3PM to 11PM, which means you're the face residents see when they come home after work. The evening shift carries a specific rhythm: package deliveries pile up, residents decompress, and prospects who toured earlier in the day are following up with questions. Your ability to read a room, remember a name, and turn a routine interaction into something that feels personal is what this position runs on.

You'll convert inbound leads into scheduled tours by following up with prospects and communicating what makes the community worth living in. That's active sales work, even if the title doesn't say "leasing." You'll also coordinate directly with the leasing team to keep tour schedules organized and occupancy goals in sight. Resident retention is part of your accountability too. Planning and executing community events, monitoring the condition of shared spaces, managing front desk logs and package rooms, and encouraging satisfied residents to leave reviews all fall within your scope.

What This Role Builds

If you come from hospitality, retail, or hotel work, you already know how to handle high-volume customer interactions under pressure. This role takes that foundation and adds property management context. Within your first 90 days, you'll be expected to work toward obtaining a leasing license, which is a meaningful credential that opens doors to Leasing Consultant and eventually Assistant Manager roles. The combination of leasing knowledge, resident relations experience, and event programming you develop here maps directly onto upward movement in multifamily operations.

Strong candidates in this role don't just handle problems, they anticipate them. Knowing which residents have packages waiting before they ask, remembering that a particular resident has a dog and greeting both by name, noticing that a common area needs attention before a prospect tour starts. That proactive instinct separates good from exceptional here.

What You Bring and What Bozzuto Offers

Bozzuto is looking for someone with at least two years in a customer-facing environment, strong written and verbal communication, a polished appearance, and comfort with technology across various software and apps. You'll also need to be able to lift up to 25 pounds, which becomes relevant during busy package seasons.

  • 2+ years in hospitality, retail, travel, or hotel environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Proficiency with software and digital tools
  • Willingness to obtain a leasing license within the first 90 days
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds

The position pays $23.08 per hour. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. greenhouse_5192602008.title: Sales & Marketing Associate
greenhouse_5192602008.rewritten_description:
It's a Saturday afternoon, the leasing office is buzzing, and three different prospects just walked in within ten minutes of each other. That's a normal weekend at a well-run Bozzuto community, and it's exactly the environment this role is built for.

What This Role Actually Involves

Bozzuto is hiring a Sales and Marketing Associate at their Lake Worth, FL community, and the job sits squarely at the intersection of leasing and brand building. You're not just showing apartments. You're managing a pipeline, following up with leads, converting interest into tours, and converting tours into signed leases. That follow-up work matters more than most candidates expect. A lot of traffic comes in on weekends, which is why this position requires working three weekends per month with compensating weekdays off. That schedule isn't unusual in leasing, but it's worth knowing upfront.

Beyond the in-person leasing work, you'll carry some responsibility for the community's social media presence, specifically creating content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook that generates genuine interest in the property. This isn't a throwaway task. Bozzuto leans into brand consistency across their portfolio, so the content you produce reflects the community's identity to prospective residents who may never step foot in the office before deciding to tour.

Resident experience is part of the job too. Building loyalty means the work doesn't stop at lease signing. How residents feel about the community during their tenancy directly affects renewals, and you play a role in shaping that.

What You'll Need

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, the kind that hold up in both a quick text follow-up and a 45-minute tour conversation
  • Comfort with social media content creation, not just scrolling but actually producing material that performs
  • A professional presentation and genuine ease in one-on-one conversations with strangers
  • Competency with software, apps, and leasing platforms (specifics will vary, but tech-averse candidates will struggle)
  • A Florida real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire

The salary range is $48,000 to $50,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities tied to performance. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates in this specific role is the ability to genuinely read people during a tour and adjust the pitch accordingly, not recite a script. Bozzuto communities tend to attract residents who are making a real lifestyle decision, not just checking off square footage and price. If you can make a prospect feel understood rather than sold to, you'll close more leases than someone with a harder sales approach who treats every tour the same way.

greenhouse_5203030008.title: Senior Leasing Consultant ($5,000 Sign on Bonus)
greenhouse_5203030008.rewritten_description:
The Skills This Role Runs On

Senior Leasing Consultant at Bozzuto's Nashville community is, at its core, a sales role with a hospitality spine. You'll draw on lead conversion skills every single day. That means following up with prospects, reading where they are in their search, and delivering the right information at the right moment to move them from curious to committed. You won't just hand someone a floor plan and hope for the best. You'll conduct personalized tours that connect the physical space to what a prospect actually wants in a home.

Social media is a real part of the job here, not an afterthought. Bozzuto expects you to build community awareness through platforms like Instagram and Facebook, which means you'll need a genuine eye for content and some comfort in front of a camera or keyboard. If you've ever generated organic traffic for a property through social, that skill translates directly.

What You'll Be Doing Week to Week

Your week will look something like this: working leads in the CRM, following up on inquiries that went cold, preparing for weekend tours, and keeping resident touchpoints consistent enough that renewals feel natural rather than forced. Three weekends per month are part of the schedule. Two weekdays off balance that out. It's worth being honest about: weekend availability is non-negotiable in leasing, and this role is no different. The upside is that's when traffic is highest, so your conversion opportunities are real.

The "Senior" designation matters. Bozzuto isn't just looking for someone who can show an apartment. They want someone who can hold the leasing desk together, maintain brand consistency, and represent the community with polish. One year of leasing or sales experience is the floor, not the ceiling.

  • Lead follow-up and prospect communication across all channels
  • Personalized apartment tours that drive lease decisions
  • Social media content creation for community awareness
  • Resident experience touchpoints that support retention
  • Professional presentation and brand representation at all times

What This Role Builds

Strong leasing consultants who move up in property management typically do so because they learned to read a prospect quickly, close without pressure, and handle objections with real information rather than spin. This role builds exactly those muscles. The conversion and relationship skills you sharpen here transfer cleanly into a Leasing Manager or Assistant Property Manager path, especially within a company like Bozzuto that promotes from within.

Nashville's rental market has stayed competitive even as new supply has come online across the metro. That environment rewards leasing professionals who know how to sell value, not just availability. If you can close in a market with options, that skill carries anywhere.

Bozzuto offers a $5,000 sign-on bonus for this position, along with a salary in the $53,560 to $55,000 range, medical, dental and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The culture here has a reputation for being intentional about employee development, which matters more than it might sound when you're deciding between comparable offers.

greenhouse_4233791009.title: Leasing Hub Associate - Dallas, TX
greenhouse_4233791009.rewritten_description:
Most leasing roles plant you at one community, and you work the traffic that walks through your door. This role is different. Cortland's Leasing Hub sits upstream from all of that, fielding leads centrally and routing qualified prospects to communities across the portfolio. It's a sales-forward position, and if you've ever thought of yourself as a connector rather than just a door-opener, that framing matters here.

What This Role Actually Involves

You're not on-site. You're working a pipeline. On a typical day, you'll handle somewhere between 40 and 50 leads coming in through RealPage via calls, emails, and texts. Your job is to qualify those leads, build genuine interest in the right community, and book appointments for the on-site leasing teams. That means you need to know Cortland's portfolio well enough to match a prospect's needs to a specific community's pricing, floor plans, and amenities without hesitation.

The brand representation piece is real. You're often the first human voice a prospective resident hears, and the impression you leave shapes whether they show up for that appointment or ghost it entirely. The on-site teams are counting on you to send them warm, prepared traffic, not cold leads who barely remember why they called.

Cortland uses OneSite for property management functions, and familiarity with L2L (Lead2Lease) is a plus. If you're comfortable in both, you'll ramp faster. If you're only in one, expect a learning curve in the first few weeks.

Who Fits Well Here

  • At least two years of customer-facing experience, with a genuine sales component, not just service
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, since you're working across all three channels simultaneously
  • English fluency required; bilingual candidates are valued given the Dallas market
  • Comfort with volume. Fifty contacts a day is the expectation, not the exception
  • Proficiency in OneSite; L2L experience preferred

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't speed. It's the ability to have a real conversation while moving efficiently. Prospects can tell when they're being processed. The ones who convert appointments into signed leases are usually talking to someone who asked one or two genuine questions and actually listened to the answers. That skill, at volume, is rarer than it sounds.

This role also builds transferable instincts. Working a centralized hub gives you exposure to how multiple communities position themselves, how concessions shift by market conditions, and how lead sources perform differently across the portfolio. That kind of cross-community visibility is something most leasing consultants spend years trying to piece together from a single property. It's a real head start if you eventually want to move into a community-level or regional role.

Cortland is a large, vertically integrated operator with in-house design, construction, and management functions. That structure means internal mobility is a realistic conversation, not a recruiting line. But you'll need to perform in this seat first.

greenhouse_4233796009.title: Leasing Hub Associate - Dallas, TX
greenhouse_4233796009.rewritten_description:
Cortland's Leasing Hub model is a centralized approach to lead management. Instead of sitting at a single community, you're working a pipeline of prospects across multiple properties from one location. It's a different animal than traditional on-site leasing, and the posting even flags that directly. If you're someone who thrives on volume, phone sales rhythm, and the challenge of moving leads through a funnel without ever walking a prospect to a model unit, this setup might actually suit you better than conventional leasing ever did.

What This Role Actually Involves

The core of the job is lead nurturing at scale. You're fielding inbound calls, emails, and texts generated through RealPage, then working outbound to keep prospects engaged and push them toward appointments at Cortland communities across the market. Forty to fifty leads per day is the stated expectation. That's a real number, and it requires genuine comfort with high-contact sales cadences, not just a pleasant phone manner.

Beyond volume, you're expected to carry real product knowledge. Advising prospects on pricing, amenities, and community-specific features across a portfolio requires you to stay current on what each property is offering, including any concessions or promotions that shift with occupancy. You'll also be the first human voice many prospects hear before they ever set foot on a property, which means how you qualify and warm a lead directly affects traffic quality for the on-site teams.

Who Fits This Role

  • At least two years of customer service or sales experience, with a track record of working high-volume contact
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, English fluency required, bilingual a genuine plus in the Dallas market
  • Comfort with OneSite (RealPage's property management platform); L2L experience is preferred
  • Fast, accurate typing and solid general computer skills. You're moving between systems constantly.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just friendliness. It's the ability to stay organized and energetic across dozens of interactions a day without losing the quality of each conversation. Hub leasing can feel repetitive if you let it. The people who do well treat each lead as a real sales opportunity, not a checkbox.

Context Worth Knowing

Centralized leasing hubs have expanded across large operators over the past several years, partly as an efficiency play and partly because AI-assisted lead response has changed what on-site teams need to handle themselves. Cortland's hub model puts a human in that layer, which means your role sits at an interesting intersection of traditional sales and modern lead management. If you're early in a multifamily career, this is a legitimate way to build pipeline and conversion skills that transfer directly to a community-based leasing or assistant manager role down the road. Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with a large footprint, so internal mobility is a real option for people who perform.

Cortland is based in Atlanta and ranks among the top ten multifamily owners and operators in the country. They run their own design, construction, and management operations in-house, which tends to mean a more consistent product across the portfolio than you'd see at a third-party management shop.

greenhouse_4231478009.title: Leasing & Sales Consultant-Cortland Duluth
greenhouse_4231478009.rewritten_description:
Cortland runs one of the more disciplined leasing operations in multifamily, and this role at their Duluth community reflects that. You're not just showing apartments. You're managing a sales process with real metrics, real accountability, and real earning potential tied to how well you perform against occupancy and conversion goals.

What the Day-to-Day Actually Looks Like

A typical day blends inbound traffic, outbound follow-up, and pipeline management. You'll field inquiries, conduct tours, and then spend a meaningful chunk of time working your CRM to nurture prospects who didn't lease on the first visit. Cortland tracks conversion rates closely, so the follow-up discipline matters as much as the in-person presentation. You'll also coordinate resident events, manage social media content for the community, and handle objections from prospects who are cross-shopping nearby properties.

Duluth sits in the Gwinnett County submarket, which has seen consistent renter demand from the area's strong employment base and its growing international residential community. Competition among Class A communities in this corridor is real, which means your ability to articulate what makes a Cortland community worth the rent premium is a skill you'll develop and sharpen quickly.

Core Responsibilities

  • Hit leasing goals and conversion KPIs consistently, not just occasionally
  • Maintain accurate CRM records for every prospect interaction and follow up on a defined timeline
  • Use consultative selling to understand what a prospect actually needs before pitching anything
  • Guide prospects through the application and move-in process in a way that feels effortless to them
  • Plan and execute resident engagement events that build loyalty and generate referrals
  • Manage the community's social media presence as part of the overall marketing mix
  • Respond to coaching from leadership and adjust your approach based on what the data shows

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with in-house design, construction, and management, which means the brand standards are consistent and specific. The consultants who do well here aren't just personable. They're organized. They treat the CRM like a tool, not a chore. They follow up the same day, every time, without being reminded. If you've come from a retail or hospitality background where you were measured on conversion and customer experience simultaneously, that transfers directly to this kind of role.

Six months of sales or customer-facing experience is the baseline. What matters more is whether you're coachable, whether you actually like closing, and whether you can handle a stretch of slow traffic without losing your process. Leasing has quiet weeks. The consultants who stay consistent during those periods are the ones who build the strongest pipelines when traffic picks back up.

This role is a legitimate entry point into property management. Strong leasing consultants at companies like Cortland move into assistant manager roles with exposure to leasing administration, delinquency management, and financial reporting. The sales foundation you build here carries into every senior role in the industry.

greenhouse_4230098009.title: Maintenance Technician - Cortland Vesta
greenhouse_4230098009.rewritten_description:
Multifamily maintenance is the operational backbone of any apartment community. Residents tolerate a lot, but a slow work order or a broken HVAC in July will undo months of goodwill. Cortland understands this, which is why they staff their communities with technicians who can actually diagnose problems, not just swap parts and hope for the best. This opening at Cortland Vesta in Birmingham is a full-scope maintenance role inside a portfolio that's consistently ranked among the top 10 multifamily owners in the country.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day will move between resident work orders and property-level systems. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliances, flooring, railings: you're expected to handle all of it with real technical competence, not just entry-level familiarity. Preventative maintenance on HVAC units is a core responsibility here, which matters more than people realize. A well-run PM schedule directly reduces emergency calls, extends equipment life, and protects NOI. You'll also keep an eye on building systems like automatic doors and security cameras, flagging anything that needs attention before it becomes a liability.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. That's worth saying plainly. Emergency maintenance situations come with the territory in multifamily, and Cortland expects you to respond. If that's a dealbreaker, this isn't the right fit. If you're already used to it, you know the tradeoff: the variety keeps the work interesting, and the exposure to different failure scenarios builds your diagnostic skills faster than almost any other environment.

What You'll Bring

  • EPA certification (Type I, II, or Universal) is required before you start
  • 1 to 3 or more years of hands-on maintenance experience, ideally in multifamily or facilities
  • Solid working knowledge of electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and appliance repair
  • Ability to manage a queue of work orders without losing track of priorities
  • Physical capability for lifting, ladder work, and outdoor tasks across Alabama's seasonal extremes
  • Comfort with on-call schedules and genuine responsiveness when emergencies come up
  • A high school diploma or equivalent

The candidates who stand out in this type of role aren't necessarily the ones with the longest resume. They're the ones who communicate well with residents, document their work accurately, and flag recurring issues instead of just closing tickets. Cortland's vertical integration means there are actual eyes on asset condition across the portfolio. A technician who thinks about the property as a whole asset, not just a list of work orders, fits that culture well.

From a career standpoint, a role like this at a well-resourced operator is a real building block. Technicians who develop strong HVAC and electrical skills, get comfortable with preventative maintenance programs, and show reliability on on-call rotations are exactly the people who move into lead tech and maintenance supervisor positions. Cortland's scale means those opportunities exist internally, which isn't always true at smaller operators.

Role Overview As a Maintenance Technician, you ensure the community remains safe, functional, and well‑maintained by diagnosing and repairing a wide range of building and apartment systems. You provide timely, high‑quality service to residents, support preventative maintenance programs, and help preserve the property’s physical assets through consistent care and attention. greenhouse_4233304009.title: Maintenance Technician - Phoenix, AZ
greenhouse_4233304009.rewritten_description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Maintenance tech work at a multifamily community is genuinely broad. You're not specializing in one trade. On any given day you might be diagnosing a tripped breaker, troubleshooting a condenser unit, repairing a leaking supply line, and swapping out an appliance, all before lunch. The skill set here is wide rather than deep, and that's actually the point. Cortland is looking for someone who can move fluidly across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, appliance repair, and general building systems without needing a specialist called in for every ticket.

EPA certification (Type I, II, or Universal) is required, so HVAC competency isn't optional. Beyond that, the ability to read a situation quickly and prioritize matters more than people expect. A backlog of 15 work orders requires triage. Some are resident-facing and urgent. Some are safety issues that jump the line regardless. Some can wait. Knowing the difference without being told every time is what separates a solid tech from a frustrating one.

What You're Actually Responsible For

The core of the job is completing work orders accurately and efficiently, but there's more texture to it than that. Preventative maintenance on HVAC units keeps the emergency call volume lower over time, so it's worth doing right. Monitoring the condition of building systems, doors, cameras, lighting, and railings feeds into asset preservation in a way that directly affects the property's physical value. Cortland is a vertically integrated operator with a genuine focus on the physical product, so sloppy make-readies and deferred punch list items tend to get noticed.

On-call rotation is part of the job. That's standard in this industry and worth being clear-eyed about before you apply. Emergency calls happen after hours, and residents expect a response, not a voicemail. Customer interaction here is real. How you communicate when someone's toilet is overflowing at 10pm says a lot about your professionalism.

  • 1 to 3 or more years in maintenance, facilities, or multifamily housing
  • EPA certification required (Type I, II, or Universal)
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Hands-on skills across electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and appliance repair
  • Comfortable with on-call schedules and emergency response
  • Physical ability to lift, climb, and work in varying outdoor conditions (Phoenix summers are not abstract here)
  • Good communication skills with residents and colleagues

What's Distinctive About This Opportunity

Cortland sits in the top tier of multifamily ownership and operations nationally. They're not a scrappy third-party fee manager trying to do more with less. The vertical integration model means there's actual infrastructure behind the maintenance function: real inventory systems, defined PM schedules, and internal support rather than constant improvisation. For a tech who's worked at under-resourced communities where supplies are always backordered and nothing is documented, that difference is noticeable pretty fast.

Phoenix is also worth mentioning practically. The heat load on HVAC systems here is severe. Units run hard from April through October, and a tech who knows how to keep equipment performing under those conditions builds real, transferable expertise. If you're looking to grow toward a lead tech or maintenance supervisor role, high-volume HVAC work in a desert climate is solid experience to accumulate.

greenhouse_5121493007.title: General Manager
greenhouse_5121493007.rewritten_description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

General management in commercial property isn't a single discipline. It's four or five disciplines running simultaneously. The person who thrives here is equally comfortable reading a variance report at 8am, walking a vacant suite with a prospective tenant at 10am, coaching an underperforming team member at 2pm, and fielding an owner call about Q3 NOI projections before close of business. Lincoln Property Company is looking for someone who can hold all of that without dropping any of it.

The financial fluency required here goes beyond basic budgeting. You're preparing and presenting annual operating and capital budgets, managing CAM and OpEx reconciliations, tracking AR and reporting delinquency status to clients with actual recommendations, not just numbers. You need to understand what the T-12 is telling you and where the asset is headed. Yardi or MRI experience helps significantly. Kardin experience is a plus. Excel at an intermediate to advanced level isn't optional.

Communication is the other load-bearing skill. You're the primary contact between ownership and the property. That means translating operational complexity into clear, concise reporting that clients can act on. It also means knowing when a difficult conversation with a vendor, a tenant, or a direct report needs to happen and not waiting for someone else to have it.

What You're Actually Managing

This is a senior leadership role overseeing large, complex commercial assets, which may include office, retail, or industrial properties, or a combination. You may have property managers or senior property managers reporting directly to you, and you may carry oversight responsibility across multiple assets or portfolios. The scope is real.

  • Full P&L accountability: budgets, variance reporting, capex planning, and owner reporting
  • Lease administration oversight, including lease setup, changes, expirations, covenants, and encumbrances that affect asset value
  • Vendor sourcing, bid analysis, contract compliance, and service quality management
  • Tenant lifecycle management from onboarding through retention, including move-ins, move-outs, and proactive tenant care programs
  • Emergency preparedness: documented plans, resource readiness, and team drills
  • Team leadership: hiring, scheduling, performance evaluations, cross-training, and day-to-day coaching

Vacant spaces need to stay tour-ready. That sounds minor. On a large asset, it reflects the general standard of how the property is run, and owners notice.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart at This Level

Lincoln Property Company operates at institutional scale. The General Manager here isn't just keeping the lights on. You're functioning as an owner representative, which means your recommendations carry weight and your judgment is trusted. Candidates who succeed in this role tend to combine sharp financial instincts with genuine people management ability, two skills that don't always come in the same package.

A CPM or RPA designation signals that you've invested in the profession, and LPC recognizes it. Five to eight years of relevant commercial experience is the baseline. What separates good candidates from great ones is usually the ability to forecast, not just report. If you can look at a lease expiration schedule, model out the revenue risk, and bring a thoughtful recommendation to an owner before they ask for one, that's the profile this role rewards.

Austin's commercial market has seen real pressure on occupancy across certain asset classes in recent years. A GM who understands how to manage retention proactively, structure concessions thoughtfully, and keep NOI stable through a soft leasing period will find this environment genuinely interesting work.
greenhouse_5121493007.description_text: As a LPC Property Management General Manager, you will be responsible for leading the team responsible for all operational and financial activities of large/iconic/complex property or collection of assets, typically with PM or SPM direct reports. You may also supervise other managers with other assets/portfolios. Responsibilities: Manages team response to tenant needs, ensuring that administrative and building technical staff resolve problems promptly. Ensure that services are provided consistent with policies, procedures, regulations and contractual obligations and standards. Manage, review and approve payment of vendor invoices following the terms of the management agreement, LPC policies and property management approval authorization limits. Proactively collects rent and other charges in accordance with lease terms at assigned properties. Proactively reports status of AR to client(s) with recommendations for action as appropriate. Prepares client approved standard legal notices as required. Direct and control preparation and delivery of timely, accurate and insightful monthly reports according to client (and/or company) requirements. Develop, direct and control preparation and presentation of annual budgets for operating and capital expenses consistent with client and company requirements. Preparation and submittal of reconciliations of tenant rent and Common Area Maintenance (CAM) or Escalation (OpX) recovery charges to client per the client (and/or company) requirements. Primary responsibility for completion of lease administration activities in coordination with accounting and/or lease administration department (if applicable) including review of all lease set up, lease changes, reporting, etc. and provide final approval per client (and/or company) requirements. Maintain oversight of contracted terms that impact the financial performance of the asset/company. Report as required on expirations, covenants, restrictions and other encumbrances that drive decision making and asset value. Coordinates tenant move-ins and move-outs, and walk-through spaces with tenants and tenant improvement department. Maintain vacant spaces in "tour ready" condition Ensure teams assigned to tenant onboarding activities properly document and communicate with tenants timely and appropriately. Follow up as needed with tenants to ensure a successful transition. Respond to tenant needs, ensuring that administrative and building technical staff resolve problems promptly. Craft, deliver and communicate an effective tenant care program that maximizes likelihood of tenant retention. Direct sourcing and procurement activities to effect optimal operations at assigned properties. Deliver detailed bid analysis and recommendations to clients that drive value. Manage quality of vendor services delivery to ensure contract compliance and desired value. May conduct consolidated sourcing/procurement activities to achieve savings across portfolio. Ensures monthly or more frequent property inspection. Recommends and/or approves alterations, maintenance and reconditioning as the need arises. Contracts for and coordinates vendor services as required. Document plans and preparations for emergency response using company or client formats. Ensures resources, supplies and backups are in place to effectively recover from adverse events. Orchestrates drills and other training as required. Manage and lead the daily and long-term activities of the team. Establish work schedules, assign tasks, and cross-train staff. Establish and track staff and department deadlines to meet client and company objectives. Mentor and coach as needed. Elevate team performance through subject matter expertise and suggestions to improve systems, processes and procedures. Champion business cases for improvement as appropriate. Act as primary contact with property owners, serving as Owner Representative to ensure that objectives as being met. Prepare and deliver timely, accurate and complete reports as required. Provide formal supervision to assigned team. Oversee recruiting and hiring of new employees. Monitor the training and development of staff. Conduct performance evaluations and coaching. Requirements: The job requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions. Bachelor's Degree preferred with 5 - 8 years of relevant experience. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered. Real Estate License required where mandatory by local regulation. Professional designation such as RPA, CPM, CCIM preferred. Intermediate to advanced skills with Microsoft Office Suite, including Microsoft Excel. Knowledge of Yardi or MRI and Kardin is also preferred. Proficiency with AP and CMMS systems required. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to clearly and effectively convey complex topics with a positive tone to achieve operational and relationship objectives. Strong interpersonal and analytical skills. Ability to lead the exchange of sensitive, complicated, and difficult information, convey performance expectations and handle problems. Requires advanced knowledge of financial terms and principles. Ability to forecast outcomes, prepare budgets w/ long-term capital plans, and deliver insightful variance reports. Prepares and makes recommendations on financial/business analysis including cost/benefit propositions, ROI analysis and other complex reports. Strong organizational skills that deliver timely results and influence the productivity of the team. Ability to comprehend, analyze, and interpret complex documents and skill to solve problems involving several options in situations. Requires advanced analytical and quantitative skills. Experience with commercial office, retail, and industrial properties is required. Working knowledge of leases, contracts, financial instruments, property taxes, and construction is required. Proven leadership experience and skills to empower teams to achieve a broad range of client and company objectives. This position is 100% in-office . The role requires working in office during standard business hours. Remote work or telecommuting is not an option for this position. About Lincoln Property Company Lincoln Property Company (“Lincoln”) is one of the largest private real estate firms in the United States. Offering a fully integrated platform of real estate services and innovative solutions to owners, investors, lenders and occupiers, Lincoln supports the entire real estate lifecycle across asset types, including office, multifamily, life science, retail, industrial, data center, production studio, healthcare, government, universities, and mixed-use properties, throughout the United States, United Kingdom, and Europe. Lincoln’s combined management and leasing portfolio on behalf of institutional clients includes more than 680 million square feet of commercial space. For more information, visit: www.lpc.com . All job offers are contingent on completion of a background check and proof of eligibility to work in the United States. By submitting your information or resume in response to this opportunity, you acknowledge that your personal information will be handled in accordance with Lincoln Property Company's privacy policy. greenhouse_5121756007.title: Property Administrator
greenhouse_5121756007.rewritten_description:
Commercial property administration is one of those roles that holds a management office together, even though it rarely gets the credit. When purchase orders go untracked, invoices pile up uncoded, and delinquency reports don't land on the right desks, everything downstream suffers. Lincoln Property Company is looking for someone to keep that engine running at one of their Dallas properties.

Lincoln manages an enormous commercial portfolio across asset types, and their Dallas presence is substantial. This is a full-time, in-office role supporting on-site property management for what will almost certainly be a commercial office asset. If you're expecting flexibility on the in-office requirement, this isn't the right fit.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

A big part of this job lives in Workspace and MRI. You'll be coding invoices, issuing and tracking purchase orders, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks between receipt and payment. The Chief Engineer will lean on you to keep the electrical analysis worksheet current as utility invoices come in. That's a small but telling detail: this role sits at the intersection of accounting and operations, not just one or the other.

On the tenant side, you're handling incoming calls, responding to routine inquiries, applying rents and open credits, and compiling tenant billings. You'll run aged delinquency reports and send late letters when needed. There's also a softer side to the job: coordinating tenant events, blood drives, welcome lunches, that kind of thing. It sounds minor, but tenant relations in commercial management is a real part of retention, and someone has to own it.

  • Minimum two years of commercial property management experience
  • Hands-on experience with MRI, Workspace, or Yardi
  • Solid grounding in accounts payable, accounts receivable, and the monthly accounting cycle
  • Familiarity with commercial leases and how tenant billings actually work
  • Proficiency in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
  • High school diploma required; an associate's or bachelor's degree is preferred
  • Valid driver's license and reliable transportation

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

The difference between someone who does this job adequately and someone who excels at it usually comes down to one thing: proactive tracking. Purchase orders get issued constantly in commercial management, and the people who stand out are the ones who don't wait to be asked whether a PO has been matched to an invoice. They already know, and they've already flagged the problem. If your instinct is to build a system before things get disorganized rather than after, that matters a lot here.

Experience with tenant billings and an understanding of how commercial leases structure charges (base rent, operating expenses, miscellaneous charges) will also make you significantly more useful than a candidate who only has residential or general office admin experience. Lincoln operates at institutional scale, and their clients expect a certain level of sophistication in how their properties are run. This role is a meaningful entry point into that world, and for the right person, it's a realistic path toward a Property Manager position over time.

greenhouse_5983419004.title: Affordable Housing Compliance Specialist (LIHTC)
greenhouse_5983419004.location: Charlotte, NC; Columbia, SC; Greensboro, NC; Raleigh, NC
greenhouse_5983419004.rewritten_description:
Picture this: a new application lands in your queue at 9 a.m. You've got until 9 a.m. tomorrow to review it. The household income looks borderline, the asset documentation is incomplete, and the property's in its first year of the compliance period. That's a pretty normal Tuesday in affordable housing compliance.

Asset Living, one of the larger third-party management firms in the country and an NMHC top 50 operator, is adding a Compliance Specialist to support their affordable housing portfolio across North Carolina and South Carolina. This is a hybrid role, so you'll work remotely most of the time, but the travel is real. Roughly half your working hours annually will involve property visits, training sessions, and tax credit reviews by outside entities. If you're expecting a fully remote desk job, this one isn't it.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your primary function is reviewing affordable housing resident files for eligibility and regulatory compliance under LIHTC requirements. The 24-hour turnaround on file review is a hard expectation, not a guideline. You'll track changes in tax credit regulations and update procedures accordingly, participate in outside tax credit reviews, and attend TDHCA trainings on an annual basis.

Certification matters here. If you don't already hold the Housing Credit Certified Professional (HCCP) designation, you'll be required to complete the training and pass the exam within your first two years. Asset Living also considers HUD program knowledge a meaningful plus, since affordable portfolios often blend programs.

What You Bring

  • Hands-on experience reviewing LIHTC resident files and applying compliance standards
  • Working knowledge of RealPage OneSite Tax Credit module
  • Strong organizational habits and genuine attention to detail (this work punishes carelessness)
  • Clear written and verbal communication skills for interacting with site teams and outside auditors
  • HCCP designation, or the willingness to earn it within the required timeframe
  • Ability to travel approximately 50% of your time and residence within NC or SC

One honest note about this type of role: the compliance side of affordable housing has gotten more scrutinous over the past few years. State housing finance agencies are doing more frequent file audits, and the margin for documentation errors has shrunk. Strong candidates aren't just organized. They're people who understand why a missed verification or an incorrect income calculation can put a unit's tax credit status at risk, and they treat every file accordingly. That instinct is what separates a solid compliance specialist from someone who's just checking boxes.

This role is well-positioned for someone who wants to grow into a compliance manager or regional compliance director track within a large fee management organization. Asset Living's affordable portfolio spans multiple states, which means there's genuine upward movement for people who build expertise here.

greenhouse_7718664003.title: Maintenance Technician - The Palmer
greenhouse_7718664003.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a full-time maintenance technician role at The Palmer, a residential apartment community in Charlotte managed by Olympus Property. Your day runs Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 6 PM, with on-call availability for emergencies outside those hours. That part matters. Emergencies don't wait.

The work itself is broad. You're handling service requests across plumbing, electrical, carpentry, painting, and appliance repair. That means fixing a leaky faucet in the morning, swapping out a refrigerator in the afternoon, and patching drywall somewhere in between. You'll also run preventative maintenance on HVAC units: filter changes, coil cleaning, system diagnosis. An EPA or HVAC certification is a real asset here, not just a checkbox.

Beyond the unit-level work, you'll help maintain grounds, common areas, and pool facilities. You'll track your work orders through a software system, keep the maintenance shop organized, and coordinate daily priorities with the Lead Maintenance Technician. You're not working in isolation. The service team and leasing office depend on your turnaround speed.

What You Bring

  • At least 2 years of maintenance experience in a residential, apartment, or commercial setting
  • Working knowledge of HVAC systems. EPA certification preferred
  • Hands-on competency with plumbing, electrical, appliance repair, and basic carpentry
  • Comfort operating hand tools and power tools daily
  • Availability for emergency calls outside standard hours
  • Ability to track work orders and communicate clearly with team members

Context Worth Knowing

Olympus Property has been recognized by the National Apartment Association as a top employer three years running (2023, 2024, 2025). That kind of consistency usually reflects something real in how a company treats its maintenance staff, not just its corporate employees. Benefits here include medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401(k) with employer matching, an apartment rental allowance, tuition reimbursement, and monthly and quarterly bonus eligibility.

One thing that separates strong technicians in a role like this from average ones: follow-through on work order documentation. Managers and owners track completion rates, response times, and resident satisfaction scores closely. Technicians who close work orders accurately and communicate delays early make the entire operation run better. The ones who don't create noise that ripples up fast.

If you're solid technically and want a team with genuine career pathing built in, this is worth a close look.

Each of our team members contributes to continued success and is dedicated to ensuring we surpass every expectation so that we can commit to providing opportunities for growth, rewards, and recognition. The Maintenance Technician will work alongside the service team and office teams to ensure that all the repair and maintenance of property facilities and apartment homes meet or exceeds standards. Your Purpose & General Responsibilities: Embody the Olympus Property Core Values: Customer Care Teamwork Trust Family Fun Perform maintenance requests with knowledge of plumbing, carpentry, painting, appliance repair, etc. Preventative maintenance and diagnosis on HVAC units, replacing filters, and cleaning coils Repair and replace furnished kitchen appliances, refrigerators, stoves, washers and dryers, ovens, and microwaves Minor to moderate-level plumbing repairs, replacements, and installation Repair or replace sink/shower/toilet fixtures Repair or replace electrical fixtures and switches Lock changes, lockouts, and replace doors, doorknobs, and lock systems Operate various equipment, hand tools, and power tools Upkeep and organization of maintenance shop and inventory Track service requests and maintenance work performed through software systems Assist in maintaining the grounds, interior and exterior common areas, and pools Work closely with Lead Maintenance Technician on daily and weekly priorities Available as needed for emergency maintenance requests Essential Needs for The Palmer: Available Full-time, Monday – Friday from 9 AM – 6 PM (hours may vary slightly) Minimum of 2 years of commercial, residential, or apartment Maintenance experience Knowledge of HVAC systems and/or an EPA/HVAC license Available for emergencies as needed Benefits & Perks: Medical, Dental, & Vision Coverage Educational Assistance & Tuition Reimbursement* Pet Insurance 401(k) with Employer Matching* Short-term & Long-term Disability Critical Illness & Accidental Injury Coverage Life & AD&D Insurance Paid-Time Off Program Apartment Rental Allowance Sabbatical Career Pathing Team Building & Team Trips Opportunities for Growth Fun “Culture Days”! greenhouse_7858364.title: Groundskeeper
greenhouse_7858364.rewritten_description:
Physical skill and sharp attention to detail carry this role. A groundskeeper who can spot a cigarette butt from twenty feet, notice a cracked walkway before a resident trips on it, and keep a 200-plus unit community looking sharp on a Monday morning after a rainy weekend. That's the standard at Weinstein Properties, and it shows in how long their communities hold curb appeal and occupancy.

What This Work Actually Looks Like

Weinstein Properties has owned and managed apartment communities for over 70 years, still family-operated, with a portfolio across Virginia, North Carolina, Texas, Tennessee, and Georgia. This opening is at Bexley at Anderson Mill in Cedar Park. The schedule runs Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm, which is a real advantage in a trade where weekend call-ins are common.

The physical demands are real and worth taking seriously. You're outdoors year-round, lifting up to 50 pounds regularly, operating backpack blowers and power washers, running a golf cart across the property, weeding, planting, picking up pet waste, and trashing out units. Some of it is unglamorous. All of it matters. A well-kept exterior directly supports leasing traffic and resident retention, two metrics that drive NOI at any community. When grounds look sharp, prospects stay longer, and residents renew.

Daily and recurring tasks make up the bulk of the work. Litter patrol, pet station maintenance, and general upkeep happen every single day regardless of weather. The candidate who handles repetition well and still brings the same level of care on a Friday afternoon as they do on a Monday morning is the one who thrives here.

Skills This Role Builds and Uses

  • Independent task management without constant supervision
  • Equipment operation: backpack blowers, power washers, golf carts and trailers
  • Light painting and general housekeeping support
  • Proactive property inspection and issue identification
  • Cross-team coordination with maintenance and leasing staff

Weinstein specifically mentions a growth path into maintenance tech roles for groundskeepers who show initiative and a willingness to learn. That's not just filler. Companies that promote from within do it because internal candidates already understand the property, the residents, and the operational rhythm. Starting on grounds is a legitimate entry point into a skilled maintenance career.

Compensation and What Sets This Apart

Pay starts at $18 per hour with quarterly bonuses. There's also a $1,000 stay-on bonus available after 90 days in good standing. Benefits include health, dental, and vision coverage, a 401k with company match, rent discount, paid holidays, birthday time off, and vacation and sick leave.

What separates a strong candidate here from an average one isn't experience with a specific piece of equipment. It's consistency. Showing up on time every day, completing the same tasks thoroughly without being reminded, and communicating clearly with the maintenance team when something needs attention. Weinstein invests in training, so the technical side is teachable. The work ethic is not.

greenhouse_7859441.title: Maintenance Technician - Make Ready - Kings Crossing
greenhouse_7859441.rewritten_description:
Make-ready work is not glamorous. You're in vacant units back-to-back, patching walls, replacing hardware, touching up paint, checking appliances, and making sure every inch of the apartment is rent-ready before the next resident walks through the door. Some days the punch list is short. Other days you inherit a unit that looks like nobody cared for it in years. That's the honest version of this role at Kings Crossing in Richmond's Tuckahoe area, and Weinstein Properties is upfront about it.

What You'll Actually Do

The core of this job is the turn process. You'll move through vacant apartments systematically, completing make-readies so units can get back on the market quickly. Speed matters, but so does quality. A sloppy make-ready creates callbacks, resident complaints, and extra work for everyone. Beyond turns, you'll pick up work orders alongside the rest of the maintenance team and help keep the grounds, amenities, and pool areas in shape. The property is owned and managed in-house by Weinstein Properties, which matters: decisions get made locally, standards stay consistent, and you're not waiting on a third-party owner's approval chain for basic supplies or repairs.

The schedule is Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm. You'll rotate through an on-call schedule with the team for after-hours emergencies. On-call weeks come with a $150 flat payment regardless of whether your phone rings, plus additional pay for holiday coverage. That structure is worth noting because a lot of companies treat on-call as an invisible tax on your time. This one compensates for it directly.

What You Bring

  • At least one year of apartment or residential maintenance experience
  • Comfort with hand and power tools, basic plumbing, appliance checks, and unit prep work
  • EPA Type II or Universal certification is a plus, but not required if you're willing to work toward it
  • HVAC troubleshooting experience is valued, not mandatory
  • Reliable transportation and a valid driver's license
  • Ability to lift up to 75 lbs and work indoors and outdoors year-round, including climbing stairs and ladders
  • Comfort using work order software and basic digital tools on a phone or tablet

Strong candidates for this role tend to be self-directed. The maintenance supervisor isn't going to walk you through every unit. You need to look at a space, identify what it needs, sequence the work intelligently, and get it done. People who struggle here usually underestimate how much judgment goes into a good make-ready, even when the work itself looks straightforward.

The Bigger Picture

Weinstein Properties owns and manages its communities rather than working as a fee management shop. For technicians, that typically means better continuity: the leadership you build a relationship with stays involved, capital improvements get approved more directly, and the property isn't subject to ownership changes that shuffle priorities. Starting pay is $19 per hour, rising to $20 once you hold EPA certification. There's also a sign-on bonus, quarterly performance bonuses, a rent discount, and a full benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company match.

Make-ready experience transfers well across the industry. Technicians who develop speed and quality on turns often move into lead tech or assistant maintenance supervisor roles. If that's a direction you want to grow, in-house operators like Weinstein tend to promote from within more readily than companies managing properties they don't own.

greenhouse_7833401.title: Porter
greenhouse_7833401.rewritten_description:
Curb appeal isn't just a leasing tool. It's one of the fastest ways a property either wins or loses a prospect before they ever walk through the door. The Porter role exists to protect that first impression every single day, and at a community like Bexley Chamblee, that work is visible in everything from the parking lot at 8am to the pet stations by 5pm.

Weinstein Properties has been family-owned for over 70 years, managing more than 60 apartment communities across Georgia, Virginia, North Carolina, Texas, and Tennessee. That kind of longevity comes from taking daily operations seriously, and the Porter is a direct part of that equation.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm role, which is a real benefit in a field where weekend call-outs are common. Your day starts with a grounds walk. Trash pickup, pet waste stations, common areas, breezeways. Repetitive? Yes. But the properties that stay clean and tight are the ones where someone shows up consistently and doesn't cut corners on the unglamorous stuff.

Beyond daily rounds, you'll support the maintenance team on light tasks, help with make-ready turns when units need a quick cleanup before move-in, and handle light painting or housekeeping as needed. You'll operate equipment including backpack blowers and power washers, and move around the property using a golf cart. This role requires lifting up to 50 pounds regularly, working outdoors year-round in Georgia heat and humidity, and staying physically active for most of the shift.

Starting pay is $17 per hour, with quarterly bonuses on top of that. There's also a 30% rent discount if you live on-site, and a $1,000 stay-on bonus after 90 days in good standing.

Who Tends to Succeed Here

  • Reliable and punctual. The grounds don't wait. If you're not consistent, it shows immediately.
  • Self-directed. You won't have someone walking behind you with a checklist. You see something that needs attention, you handle it.
  • Comfortable with physical, repetitive work. Some days feel similar to the last. That's not a flaw in the role, it's the nature of maintaining a property at a high standard.
  • Open to growth. Weinstein promotes from within. Porters who demonstrate skill and attitude regularly move into maintenance technician tracks.

That last point is worth taking seriously. The Porter role is one of the more common entry points into a full maintenance career in multifamily. You learn how a property functions from the ground up, literally. Technicians who started as porters often have stronger site awareness than those who came in at a higher level because they've touched every corner of the property.

If you want a stable schedule, a team that actually invests in training, and a path toward more technical work in property maintenance, this position is worth a hard look.
greenhouse_7833401.description_text: We have a fantastic Porter opportunity available! Weinstein Properties, headquartered in Richmond, Virginia owns and manages over 60 apartment communities in Virginia, North Carolina, Texas, Tennessee, and Georgia. Still owned and operated by the Weinstein family after 70+ years, our continued success is attributed to our hands on approach to daily operations and our focus on the customer experience. Location: Bexley Chamblee Pay: Hourly rates are competitive (starting at $17/hour) + Quarterly Bonuses + 30% Rent Discount Bonus*: Currently eligible for a $1000 stay on bonus after 90 days and in good standing. (*For the stay on bonus - the recruiter can discuss the details. Stay with us and be awesome and you get this great bonus!) Schedule: Monday - Friday, 8am - 5pm Who should apply? This role is ideal for someone who is reliable, detail-oriented, self-motivated, and takes pride in their work. Porters are essential members of our team, keeping our communities clean, beautiful, and welcoming. At Weinstein Properties, we do things differently. We invest in people, not just resumes. We believe in training talent from the ground up. Ideal Characteristics of Successful Porters Reliability & Punctuality: Shows up on time, dependable, and trustworthy. Independence & Initiative: Works well independently, is a self-starter, and takes initiative to go beyond assigned tasks. Attention to Detail: Notices issues proactively and maintains a clean, well-kept appearance of the community. Strong Work Ethic: Keeps busy, completes tasks thoroughly, and doesn’t shy away from dirty or repetitive jobs. Team Player: Communicates and collaborates well with maintenance and leasing teams; helps out when needed without hesitation. Positive Attitude & Pride in Work: Takes pride in maintaining the property and contributes to community appearance. Adaptable & Versatile: Willing to take on various responsibilities such as light painting, housekeeping tasks, or supporting maintenance as needed. Growth Mindset: Open to learning new skills, with potential to grow into maintenance tech or other roles. Things Candidates Should Know Before Starting You will get dirty: Tasks include picking up trash, cleaning pet waste stations, trashing out apartments, and other potentially unpleasant (but necessary) duties. Repetition is part of the job: Many tasks (like picking up litter and pet waste) are daily, recurring responsibilities. Opportunity for Growth: Team members with a great attitude and willingness to learn can grow into more advanced roles in maintenance. This is a physically demanding role: . This position requires prolonged periods of standing and walking while performing tasks, frequently lifting and carrying items weighing up to 50 pounds or more, using hand tools and operating equipment (including backpack blowers, power washers), operating golf carts/trailers, frequently bending and stooping for planting, weeding and maintenance of outdoor spaces, and working in conditions with loud noise levels at times.  This position also requires working outdoors year-round in potentially inclement weather conditions (i.e. heat, cold, rain, humidity).  The person in this position must be able to complete all physical requirements of the job with or without a reasonable accommodation. Weinstein Properties is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.

greenhouse_7859585.title: Porter
greenhouse_7859585.rewritten_description:
Every apartment community runs on two things: the leasing team bringing people in and the maintenance team keeping things working. But the person who sets the first impression every single morning is the porter. At Bexley at Central Park in Grand Prairie, TX, Weinstein Properties is looking for someone to own that role.

Weinstein Properties is a family-owned company, headquartered in Richmond, Virginia, with more than 70 years of operating history and a portfolio of over 60 communities across five states. That longevity isn't an accident. They invest in the people doing the ground-level work, and that's exactly what this opportunity reflects.

What This Job Actually Looks Like Day to Day

You'll start each morning walking the property before most residents have had their coffee. Trash pickup, pet waste stations, blowing off breezeways and parking lots, checking that the grounds look the way they should. Some days you're pressure washing. Some days you're helping make-ready a vacant unit, whether that means a trash-out after a move, light cleaning, or touch-up painting. The work changes, but the standard doesn't.

This is a Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm schedule, which is a real quality-of-life advantage compared to on-call maintenance roles. Pay starts at $16.50 per hour, and there's a quarterly bonus structure in place. Weinstein also offers a $1,000 stay-on bonus after 90 days in good standing. Talk to the recruiter for specifics on that.

The honest part: this job involves repetitive tasks, outdoor work in Texas heat and cold, lifting up to 50 pounds regularly, and yes, picking up pet waste. Every day. If that kind of work doesn't bother you, you'll fit right in. If it does, this probably isn't the right match.

Who Tends to Succeed Here

  • Reliability above everything. Showing up on time, consistently, matters more than any other single quality.
  • Self-direction. Your supervisor isn't walking the property with you all morning. You need to see what needs doing and do it.
  • Attention to detail. Residents notice when something looks off. You should notice first.
  • Physical readiness. Standing, walking, bending, lifting, and operating equipment like backpack blowers and power washers throughout the day.
  • Openness to growth. Weinstein is known for developing porters into maintenance technicians. If you want to build a career in property maintenance, this is a real starting point, not just a placeholder job.

The candidates who stand out aren't necessarily the ones with the longest resumes. They're the ones who treat a clean property like it reflects on them personally, because at Weinstein, it does. A porter who takes initiative and communicates well with the maintenance and leasing teams tends to get noticed quickly, and that visibility opens doors to more skilled maintenance work over time.

Weinstein Properties is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
