greenhouse_4237029009.title: Grounds Person
greenhouse_4237029009.rewritten_description:
Curb appeal is often the first thing a prospective resident notices, and at a well-established community like Weymouth Commons, it's also what long-term residents expect to see maintained day after day. John M. Corcoran and Company, a New England real estate firm operating since 1951, is hiring a Grounds Person to keep that standard consistent across the property's exterior.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 4:30 pm position, which is a genuine plus in property maintenance work where weekend and on-call demands are common. Your day centers on the exterior of the community: mowing, trimming, pruning, and planting during the growing season, trash and pet waste removal as a daily constant, and exterior upkeep tasks like fence work, painting, and minor repairs as they come up. When winter hits the South Shore, you're shifting into snow removal and ice treatment. The pace changes with the seasons, but the expectation of a clean, safe property stays the same year-round.

You'll also keep an eye out for safety hazards and coordinate with vendors when issues go beyond the scope of your role. It's less about being handed a checklist and more about developing the habit of noticing what needs attention before someone else has to point it out.

What JMC Is Looking For

  • Hands-on experience with landscaping and grounds maintenance
  • Comfort operating landscaping equipment safely
  • The ability to work independently and follow through without close supervision
  • Clear communication with teammates, supervisors, and vendors

This role doesn't require a long resume. It requires reliability and a genuine attention to detail. The candidates who tend to stand out aren't necessarily those with the most credentials. They're the ones who take ownership of their section of the property, show up consistently, and take pride in how the grounds look at the end of each shift.

Compensation runs between $19.50 and $23.75 per hour, and the position comes with a $2,500 sign-on bonus. Benefits include medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, paid time off and holidays, and a 401k with company match.

JMC has a track record of promoting from within, and a grounds role like this is a real entry point into property operations. Maintenance technician paths, lead groundskeeper positions, and broader facilities roles are all natural progressions for someone who builds familiarity with a property and proves their work ethic early on.
greenhouse_4237029009.description_text: At JMC, you’ll be part of a legacy of excellence that dates back to 1951—yet we still maintain the close-knit, collaborative atmosphere of a small company. We’re proud to be one of New England’s leading real estate firms, known for our integrity, innovation, and commitment to building vibrant communities. Our culture is built on respect, inclusion, and opportunity. Many of our team members have grown their careers with us over decades, and we’re passionate about helping new talent thrive. Whether you're just starting out or looking to take the next step, JMC offers a supportive environment where your contributions truly matter. Join us in shaping the future of real estate—where your work builds more than properties; it builds community. We’re looking for a Grounds Person to maintain the overall appearance of the community and ensure the property’s exterior remains clean, safe, and visually appealing. In this role, you’ll support landscaping, grounds upkeep, and general property maintenance to uphold curb appeal standards. What you’ll do: • Maintain lawns and landscaping, including mowing, trimming, pruning, planting, and irrigation • Perform daily grounds upkeep, such as trash and pet waste removal • Assist with exterior repairs, painting, fence work, and installations • Support seasonal needs, including snow removal and ice treatment • Identify and address safety hazards and work with vendors as needed • Complete light maintenance and follow safety protocols What we’re looking for: • Experience with landscaping and grounds maintenance • Ability to operate landscaping equipment safely and effectively • Strong work ethic with ability to follow instructions and work independently • Ability to communicate clearly What we offer: • Competitive compensation and a $2,500 sign-on bonus • Health & Wellness: Medical, Dental, Vision, and Life Insurance • Paid Leave: We provide paid time off plus holidays • Retirement: We offer a 401k program with a company match • From collaborative team building offsites to spontaneous office fun, we’ve created an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and excited to come to work. Work Schedule: Monday through Friday, 8:00 am - 4:30 pm. Position pay range $19.50 — $23.75 USD Join us and take the next step in your career—apply today!

greenhouse_7866423.title: Leasing Consultant (Aldon at Keys Crossing)
greenhouse_7866423.rewritten_description:
Leasing is a people-first skill set, and this role at Aldon at Keys Crossing in Greenville, SC leans into that hard. Van Metre is hiring a Leasing Consultant who can build genuine relationships, stay organized under pressure, and represent a residential community with professionalism and warmth. If your background is in sales, hospitality, or customer service, you already have most of the foundation this role needs.

The Skills This Role Draws On Most

You're going to spend a significant portion of your day communicating. Phone follow-up with prospects, email outreach, tour conversations, lease renewal check-ins, resident event coordination, and service request updates all run through you. Strong verbal and written communication isn't a nice-to-have here; it's the actual job. Sales instincts matter too. You'll be expected to engage in grassroots marketing to drive traffic and occupancy, which means thinking beyond the leasing office and finding ways to connect with the broader Greenville community.

Time management is the quieter skill that separates good leasing consultants from great ones. On any given day you might be conducting a tour, processing a rental application, completing exterior property inspections, and preparing a market survey report. The job doesn't always sequence itself neatly, so the ability to prioritize without dropping anything is real and necessary.

Weekend availability is required, which is worth stating plainly. Saturday and Sunday are typically high-traffic days for apartment tours, so that's when your presence matters most.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Your primary responsibility is converting interest into leases and keeping current residents happy enough to renew. That means greeting every visitor, following up consistently with prospects, conducting apartment tours, and handling the administrative side of the leasing process correctly. You'll also do three exterior inspections per week, report deficiencies to the service team, inspect vacant units for make-ready readiness, and help coordinate resident events with your colleagues.

  • Previous experience in customer service, sales, or hospitality
  • Comfortable conducting indoor and outdoor property inspections in various weather conditions
  • Weekend availability
  • Bilingual ability is preferred
  • Social media skills are part of the role

Pay runs $18 to $20 per hour based on experience, with an annual bonus potential of $5,000 paid quarterly, plus leasing and renewal commissions. Van Metre also offers a meaningful PTO package, 401(k) matching, up to $900 in annual wellness reimbursement, tuition reimbursement, and company-paid certifications. There's also a rental discount for employees living on-site.

What Makes This Role Worth Taking Seriously

Van Metre has been in real estate development and property management since 1955. That's not a throwaway detail. It means the professional development infrastructure, the mentorship programs, and the growth paths they mention aren't just recruiting language. For someone early in a property management career, leasing is the right entry point. It builds the resident relations, sales, and operational awareness that translate directly into Assistant Manager and eventually Property Manager roles. The commission structure here also means your effort has a direct line to your paycheck, which isn't always the case in leasing roles.

Greenville is a growing market with steady apartment demand, so occupancy work here is active rather than static. You won't be managing a property that runs itself. The community needs someone who shows up ready to work the phones, walk the property, and genuinely care about the experience residents have from inquiry to renewal.
greenhouse_7866423.description_text: Van Metre is seeking a Leasing Consultant to work at one of our properties located in Greenville, SC called Aldon at Keys Crossing Apartments . Our Leasing Consultants take pride in their work, recognizing the value of teamwork and ownership of their responsibilities and impact. They are not only the face of our residential properties, but the heart of the community. Leasing Consultants are key contributors to the company’s success and we’re here to support you in your journey with professional development, career mentorship and growth opportunities, and great benefits and perks. As a Leasing Consultant , your primary focus will be to develop productive and positive relationships with residents (both current and prospective) that maximize client satisfaction, occupancy, and retention, while remaining true to Van Metre’s mission, vision, and values. Responsibilities: Greet every visitor to the community with a warm welcome, making yourself available to answer questions and assist as needed Engage in sales activities to increase traffic and occupancy (i.e., grassroots marketing) Maintain thorough knowledge of the property and its amenities, and act as a resource for current and prospective residents Build relationships with prospective residents by conducting phone and email follow-up, scheduling and conducting property tours, and processing rental applications Provide excellent customer service by checking in with residents, addressing concerns, and conducting lease renewals Monitor service requests and communicate with the service team and residents to ensure concerns are resolved quickly and adequately In conjunction with the rest of the team, plan, organize, and conduct resident events Complete three exterior community inspections weekly and report any deficiencies found (i.e., landscaping/paint needs, property damage, etc.) Inspect vacant apartments and report needed repairs to service team in order to maintain superior product quality Prepare and complete all sales administrative tasks and reports (i.e., market surveys, etc.) Other sales, customer service, and property-related tasks, as needed Requirements: Previous experience in customer service, sales, hospitality, or related fields Ability to conduct community and apartment inspections (inside and outside) in varying weather conditions Weekend work is required Bilingual preferred Competencies: Aptitude for working with people Professional demeanor and attitude Customer service skills Verbal and written communication skills Sales and goal orientation Time management and prioritization skills Social media skills Compensation and Benefits information: Van Metre offers excellent benefits, and a work environment that fosters and rewards excellence. We also promote work/life balance, including a robust Employee Wellness Program.  We encourage employees to make full use of vacation and other company-paid leave to refresh their bodies and minds. Salary: $18.00-$20.00 per hour (Pay is commensurate with experience, education, training, and skills) Annual Bonus Potential: $5,000 annually, paid quarterly Renewal and Leasing Commissions Paid Time Off: 11 paid holidays; 10 vacation days, 10 sick days, Two Volunteer Days off, Two Personal Days, Winter Break, Day off for Annual Physical, Day off for your Birthday; comprehensive parental and bonding leave Retirement Planning: Company Matched 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan; access to retirement planning coaches Wellness: Annual Wellness Reimbursement of up to $900 Professional Development: tuition reimbursement; company paid certifications, memberships, and licensures; mentoring programs; growth paths; regular performance evaluations and training opportunities Discounts: Rental and Van Metre Home purchase discounts; employee discount through Walker Title Company, Employee discount through Walker Title Company Van Metre Core Values Integrity; Ownership; Respect and Dignity; Diversity and Inclusion; Teamwork; Community; Work/Life Balance Van Metre Mission Statement The mission of Van Metre Companies is to build and continually improve upon the storied reputation, earned over the years since the Company’s founding in 1955, of providing top quality homes, investment properties and services to our customers and clients. The Company’s emphasis on integrity, professionalism and philanthropy ingrained by our founder Albert G. Van Metre, Sr. has garnered trust and respect in the Washington, D.C. area and beyond. It is critical to our future that we maintain that reputation and assure that Van Metre Companies continues to excel in real estate development and construction and in the management of apartment, retail and office investment properties, as well as in the provision of mortgage and related services. It is the task of our dedicated, energetic, and involved professionals to advance our ability to set the benchmark for luxury living and refined commercial development. The capstone of our mission is to make the “Van Metre” signature an icon for elegant residential communities, and industry leading condominium and apartment communities, as well as Class A neighborhood retail centers and office developments.

greenhouse_8531685002.title: Assistant Community Manager
greenhouse_8531685002.rewritten_description:
The ACM role exists because a Community Manager simply can't be in two places at once. Rent collection, delinquency follow-up, make-ready tracking, leasing team oversight, renewals, compliance. It's a lot. Waypoint Management Services is looking for someone at their Daytona Beach property who can carry a real share of that load, not just answer phones and file paperwork.

This is a working operations role. You'll sit alongside the Community Manager and touch nearly every function: financial reporting, lease administration, rent roll accuracy, training the leasing staff, and helping execute pricing strategy based on market surveys and occupancy trends. Weekend availability is required, which is worth saying plainly up front rather than burying it.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

Mornings often start with delinquency. Who owes, what's the status, what's the next step. From there it might be a make-ready walk, a conversation with a leasing consultant about their traffic conversion, or pulling occupancy numbers for a report. Afternoons can shift fast. A resident escalation, a renewal push, a compliance question. The role rewards people who can context-switch without losing their place. Yardi experience helps here because you'll be in it constantly, and learning it on the fly while managing daily operations is harder than it sounds.

The financial piece matters more than most ACM postings let on. You're not just collecting rent. You're monitoring performance, flagging variances, and making recommendations. If you've never read a basic P&L or tracked NOI movement, this role will stretch you. That's not a bad thing, but it's honest.

What Waypoint Is Looking For

  • At least two years in leasing, sales, or hospitality. Multifamily experience is the clearest path in, but they'll consider strong candidates from adjacent service industries.
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and social media tools, plus Yardi experience if you have it
  • Financial awareness: delinquency management, rent collection, basic reporting
  • Weekend rotation availability, no exceptions
  • Valid driver's license
  • High school diploma or equivalent required

Compensation includes a base wage plus monthly leasing and renewal bonuses, an employee housing discount, 120 hours of PTO in year one, two floating days, and medical, dental, and vision coverage starting on day one. There's also a 401k with company match and an employee referral program.

The ACM seat is one of the better training grounds in this industry. People who do it well typically move into Community Manager roles within two to four years. You learn the full operational picture at a scale that's manageable enough to actually understand it, which doesn't always happen when you jump straight into a top seat. If you want to run your own community eventually, this is a reasonable way to get there.
greenhouse_8531685002.description_text: Overview: We are looking for top talent to help us deliver legendary service experiences for our residents. We have an exciting opportunity for an Assistant Community Manager to join our team of passionate property professionals. In this role, you’ll help lead daily operations, build strong resident and team relationships, and support leasing, financial, and administrative functions at our premier multifamily community. Key Responsibilities: Availability to work weekends Partner with the Community Manager to drive daily operations and ensure an outstanding resident experience. Support leasing team performance, training, and operational excellence. Maintain compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local regulations. Help execute sales and marketing strategies to attract and retain residents. Build community relationships and stay current on market trends and competitor activity. Recommend pricing adjustments based on market surveys and occupancy goals. Assist with financial management, including rent collection, delinquencies, evictions, and reporting. Monitor financial performance and recommend solutions to improve results. Maintain accurate lease, rent roll, and resident records. Track renewals, make-ready schedules, and occupancy reports. Support administrative tasks and reporting with attention to detail. Perform other job-related duties as assigned. What You’ll Bring: Strong computer skills (Microsoft Office, internet, and social media). Financial, organizational, and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication and customer service abilities. Yardi software experience preferred. Ability to work weekend rotation. Education & Experience: High school diploma or equivalent required. Minimum 2 years of leasing, sales, or hospitality experience preferred. Valid driver’s license required. Apply today and grow your career with Waypoint. We are an equal opportunity employer. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. greenhouse_4235609009.title: Sales & Resident Experience Consultant
greenhouse_4235609009.rewritten_description:
Leasing is a numbers game, and the math is straightforward: traffic in, leases signed, occupancy up, NOI protected. Where it gets interesting is the human side of that equation. At Berkshire Residential's Park at Bayside community, the Sales and Resident Experience Consultant role exists precisely because closing ratios, renewal rates, and resident satisfaction scores don't improve on their own. Someone has to own that relationship from the first tour to the lease renewal conversation.

What This Role Actually Looks Like Day to Day

You're the first real impression a prospective resident gets of the property. That means your tour routes and model units need to be sharp every single time, not just on good days. Berkshire sets a clear performance benchmark here: a 20% closing ratio is the floor, not the ceiling. That's a concrete metric worth thinking about. If you're working 10 qualified prospects, you need to convert at least two. Strong consultants push that number higher by reading what each prospect actually needs, not by running the same canned pitch for everyone.

Beyond the sale itself, this role carries real administrative weight. Resident communications, lease documentation, and leasing office atmosphere all fall inside your scope. You're also expected to stay current on local market conditions, which means knowing what competing properties are offering in concessions, where your rents sit relative to the submarket, and what's driving traffic at any given time. That awareness shapes how you pitch the property and what feedback you bring to your property manager.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Two years of customer-facing experience is the baseline. But the consultants who actually move the needle in this kind of role do a few things differently:

  • They treat objections as information. A prospect who says the price is too high is telling you something about their priority set. Strong consultants use that to reframe value, not to discount reflexively.
  • They maintain consistency in slow traffic periods. Anyone can close when the phones are ringing. Keeping energy and presentation standards high during a slow Tuesday is where the separation happens.
  • They're comfortable with technology. Property management platforms, CRM tools, and digital communication channels are part of the job. Adaptability here isn't optional.
  • They write clearly. Resident communications reflect the community's brand. Sloppy follow-up emails cost leases.

Berkshire operates as both owner and manager of its properties, which means decisions move faster and accountability sits closer to the asset. For a leasing consultant, that's actually a better environment than third-party management. Your performance connects directly to a property that someone owns and cares about, and that tends to produce clearer feedback and better career development.

Berkshire offers three weeks of vacation, a 25% rent discount, personal development planning, and solid insurance coverage. The team culture leans heavily on individual ownership and high performance, so if you want a role where you can see the direct impact of your work on occupancy and resident satisfaction, this is a reasonable place to build that track record.

greenhouse_4235584009.title: Sales & Resident Experience Consultant (Future Opportunity)
greenhouse_4235584009.rewritten_description:
Leasing is a skill-heavy role that most people underestimate. It asks you to read people quickly, build genuine rapport in 20-minute increments, manage a pipeline of prospects, and close deals without coming across as pushy. At 20 Midtown, a Berkshire Residential property, that's exactly what this role demands every single day.

What This Role Actually Runs On

The core skill here is sales, but not the transactional kind. You're helping someone decide where they're going to live. That requires listening more than talking, asking the right questions early, and knowing when to push forward and when to give a prospect space. Berkshire sets a 20% closing ratio as a baseline expectation, which means you need to be tracking your traffic, following up consistently, and understanding why prospects don't convert just as much as why they do.

Beyond the close, you're also responsible for the day-to-day experience of current residents. That means professional communication, both written and verbal, and showing up with the same energy for a lease renewal conversation as you do for a brand-new prospect tour. Administrative accuracy matters here too. Lease files, guest card documentation, follow-up logs. The paperwork load is real, and the detail work doesn't stop just because the leasing office is busy.

Your tour routes and model units are your sales floor. You're expected to keep them in showcase condition and to notice when something is off before a prospect does. Think of yourself as part salesperson, part inspector.

Skills That Build Toward More

Leasing consultant roles are one of the most direct paths into property management. The skills you build here, closing sales, reading market conditions, managing resident relationships, handling administrative detail under pressure, translate directly into an assistant manager or property manager role. Berkshire operates its own portfolio rather than doing third-party fee management, which means there's a connected team structure and real room to grow internally if you perform.

Strong candidates for this role typically come in with some background in customer-facing sales or hospitality, not necessarily property management specifically. What separates the good ones from the average ones isn't energy alone. It's the combination of genuine curiosity about people, disciplined follow-through on leads, and the ability to stay composed when a frustrated resident walks into the leasing office mid-tour.

What Berkshire Brings to the Table

Berkshire owns and manages its own properties, so you're working within one organization rather than bouncing between ownership groups and management companies. The culture they describe emphasizes entrepreneurial thinking and individual development, including personal development plans for employees. They offer three weeks of vacation, a 25% rent discount, and health insurance. The tone internally leans toward high-energy and team-oriented, which fits the nature of the role well.

  • At least two years of transferable customer service or sales experience
  • Comfortable adapting to new software and leasing technology
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to maintain a minimum 20% closing ratio on leasing traffic
  • Detail-oriented with solid administrative follow-through

This is listed as a future opportunity, meaning Berkshire is building their pipeline for this location. If you're currently employed and keeping an eye out, that's exactly the kind of candidate they're looking for.

greenhouse_5208233008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager - $3,500 Sign-On Bonus
greenhouse_5208233008.rewritten_description:
It's 11 PM on a Tuesday and your phone rings. A boiler issue in one of the units. You're not surprised, and you're not rattled. You already know the system well enough to troubleshoot it before you even arrive on site. That's the kind of person Bozzuto is looking for in this role.

What This Job Actually Is

Bozzuto manages high-quality residential communities, and this Assistant Maintenance Manager position sits at the intersection of hands-on trades work and frontline leadership. The property is in Boston's 02115 zip code, which puts you in a dense, urban environment with the kind of complex mechanical infrastructure that goes with it. Think central plant, boilers, chillers, and split systems rather than simple package units. If you've only worked garden-style properties with straightforward HVAC setups, this one will stretch you.

Day to day, you're splitting your time between working the trades yourself and keeping the maintenance team moving. You'll resolve HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance issues. You'll own the turn process, making sure make-readies are completed thoroughly and on schedule so units get back online and leased. You'll run preventative maintenance programs that protect the building's long-term value, and you'll keep the grounds, common areas, and building systems operating cleanly and safely. Weekends are part of the schedule, and the on-call rotation is real.

Bozzuto places genuine weight on resident follow-through. Closing out a service ticket isn't just a paperwork task here. Residents notice when someone circles back to confirm the repair held, and that's the standard the team is held to.

What You Need to Bring

  • At least 5 years of experience in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA standards, risk management, and MSDS protocols
  • Demonstrated proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Familiarity with complex mechanical systems (boilers, chillers, central plant) is a real plus
  • Supervisory or leadership experience, even informal team lead experience counts
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently, climb ladders, and work in varying weather

The candidates who stand out here aren't just technically sharp. They're the ones who can walk a junior tech through a repair without taking over, who document their work cleanly, and who treat a resident's service request with the same urgency they'd want if it were their own home. That combination of trade competence and interpersonal steadiness is harder to find than either skill alone.

The salary range runs from $65,000 to $70,000, and the role comes with a $3,500 sign-on bonus. Bozzuto offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Additional bonus opportunities are also on the table.
greenhouse_5208233008.description_text: At Bozzuto, every team member shares a deep commitment to doing good for those around us . We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. Our people are dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences to everyone we serve including each other. This shared purpose drives us to excel in every role and fosters a welcoming, inclusive workplace where teamwork thrives. Guided by our core values of concern, creativity, passion and the pursuit of perfection, we are inspired and empowered to reach higher, unlock our full potential and be truly extraordinary. Primary Responsibilities: Your mantra is preserve and protect. You bring your passion for people and the trades to work with you every day. You’re a doer but you can also motivate others. As a jack of all trades and an advocate for the community’s asset preservation, you instill in everyone you interact with a sense of Bozzuto pride. As an Assistant Building Maintenance Manager, your primary responsibilities include: Leading by example and being a role model for the standards and behaviors consistent with the core values and culture of Bozzuto Showing care and concern for our residents through timely follow ups and meticulous completion of their apartment service tickets Resolving maintenance issues in the area of HVAC, electrical, plumbing and appliance repair Managing the make-ready and apartment turnover process with attention to detail and timeliness so apartments can be leased and new residents can move in Preserving the value of the building by consistently executing on preventative maintenance programs Preserving the beauty of the building and the safety of the community through general upkeep and cleanliness, snow removal and grounds maintenance Sustaining peak efficiency operation levels for all property components Adhering to applicable building, county and safety codes/standards, while running an accident-free operation Working weekends and participating in the emergency on-call rotation What You Bring to Us: 5+ years of experience in the trades, facilities and/or residential building maintenance Strong leadership aptitude and supervisory experience preferred An HVAC, EPA and/or NAPE certification Knowledge of risk management, OSHA and MSDS Proficiency with HVAC, plumbing, electrical trades and appliance repair Basic carpentry, drywall and painting skills Exposure to complex mechanical systems such as central plant, split systems, boilers and chillers desired A safety mindset and an eye for detail A builder’s mentality (know it like you built it!) Excellent communication skills both written and verbal A sharp professional appearance Basic proficiency with the Microsoft Office suite and comfort using various technology platforms, apps and computer programs Availability for emergency situations at the property, including an on-call rotation The ability to exert 25 to 50 pounds of force frequently and/or 10 to 20 pounds of force constantly, climb stairs, use ladders and work in varying weather conditions This position is eligible for additional bonus opportunities. Salary Range $65,000 — $70,000 USD When you join Bozzuto, you're not just accepting a job; you are becoming part of a community that cares about your overall well-being and professional growth. We recognize that our team members are the core of our business, and we are committed to taking care of them so they can deliver outstanding experiences to our residents. In addition to an award-winning culture, our benefits take into consideration everything from career development, retirement, family matters and health and wellness. Working on our team and in this position, you can expect: Competitive compensation. Health & wellness benefits. We offer coverage for medical, dental, and vision, including access to the best doctors at an affordable price. Paid leave. We provide 20 days of paid time off plus holidays. Retirement planning. We offer a 401k program with a company match. Tuition reimbursement. Plus, many other programs to support career development and growth. A place you belong. We proudly offer a diverse range of employee resource groups, providing opportunities for our employees to connect over shared experiences, backgrounds, and cultures. Bozzuto is proudly an Equal Opportunity Employer.

greenhouse_5206401008.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_5206401008.rewritten_description:
A prospective resident fills out an online form at 11pm. By the time they're checking email the next morning, you've already followed up with a personalized note about the community. That's the rhythm of this role.

What This Job Actually Involves

Bozzuto is hiring a Leasing Consultant at their Santa Clara, CA community. The core of the job is converting interest into signed leases. That means following up with every lead, conducting tours that feel personal rather than scripted, and staying consistent enough with the resident experience that people tell their friends.

You'll also manage social media presence for the community. Think Instagram and Facebook content that reflects the property's personality, not just stock photos and generic captions. This is a sales role with a marketing component built in.

The schedule runs three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. Weekends are when traffic peaks, so that's when the role earns its value.

Base salary runs $48,000 to $52,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What Bozzuto Expects From You

  • Strong written and verbal communication. You're often the first human voice a prospect hears.
  • Comfort with social media platforms, specifically Facebook and Instagram, and an ability to create content that actually gets attention
  • A sales instinct. You can read what a prospect needs and adjust your approach accordingly.
  • Professional presentation. Bozzuto operates in a premium space; appearance and demeanor reflect the brand.
  • Competency with software, apps, and general tech tools used in leasing operations

What separates strong candidates here is follow-through. Average leasing consultants give a good tour. Strong ones follow up the same day, answer questions before the prospect thinks to ask them, and keep the relationship warm through the application process. Santa Clara's rental market is competitive, and prospects are often comparing several communities at once. The consultants who close are the ones who stay in the conversation longest.

This role is a common entry point into property management careers. Leasing consultants who perform well often move into Assistant Community Manager roles, which brings exposure to financials, delinquency, and operations. It's a practical foundation for anyone who wants to manage a property rather than just lease it.

greenhouse_4237676009.title: Front Door Waste Technician-Cortland at the Village
greenhouse_4237676009.rewritten_description:
Cortland is one of the largest vertically integrated multifamily operators in the country, and they run their communities with a level of operational discipline that filters all the way down to the site level. This Front Door Waste Technician role at Cortland at the Village is a ground-level position in the most literal sense, and it's also one of the more resident-facing jobs on any property team.

The core of the work is valet trash service. Each shift, you'll walk assigned routes through the community, collecting waste bags left outside resident doors, loading them into leak-proof carts, and transferring everything to onsite compactors or containers. You're also keeping common areas clean: dog waste stations, pool decks, mail kiosks, parking lots, and compactor pads all fall within your scope. Routes include stairs, and the job requires consistent physical output, so this isn't a role where pace varies much from day to day.

Beyond the physical work, there's a reporting component that matters. You'll log start and end times, document your route with photos, and submit everything through a mobile app. Cortland tracks productivity at the community level, so completing your route efficiently and accurately isn't optional. If you spot improper waste or safety hazards, you're expected to flag them and take appropriate action, not just walk past.

Resident interaction is part of the job too. You're on the property during evening and early morning hours when residents are home, and how you carry yourself in those moments reflects on the community. Professionalism and consistent communication with both residents and site staff are genuine expectations here, not filler language.

What this role requires

  • High school diploma or equivalent (preferred)
  • At least one year in waste services or a customer-facing role
  • Working knowledge of EPA and OSHA safety standards related to waste handling
  • Physical ability to climb stairs, walk extended distances, and lift up to 50 lbs
  • Comfort working outdoors in all weather conditions, including exposure to strong odors
  • Valid Class E driver's license for local driving as needed
  • Personal smartphone (Android 2.2 or higher, or iPhone iOS 4.3 or higher) with an active data plan for route documentation
  • Bilingual ability is a plus

What separates strong candidates in this role isn't just physical capability. It's reliability and attention to detail. Valet trash services live and die on consistency. Residents notice immediately when a bag is skipped or a compactor area is left messy. If you show up on time, complete your route thoroughly, and communicate clearly when something is off, you'll stand out on any Cortland property. That kind of dependability also opens doors to broader porter and maintenance roles within the company over time.

As a recognized leader in the multifamily sector, our focus on performance, innovation, and disciplined execution continues to drive strong growth and market leadership. We are committed to building a best-in-class organization by empowering top talent with the resources, autonomy, and support needed to deliver results and advance their careers in a high-performance environment. greenhouse_4237665009.title: Front Door Waste Technician-Cortland Duluth
greenhouse_4237665009.rewritten_description:
It's 8 p.m. on a Wednesday and you're working a valet trash route, moving from building to building, collecting bagged waste from resident doorsteps before it becomes anyone's problem. That's the core of this role. It's physical, it's consistent, and it matters more to resident satisfaction than most people expect.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Cortland is hiring a Front Door Waste Technician for their Duluth community. This is a valet trash position, which means you're running scheduled collection routes through the property, picking up resident waste bags from doorsteps, loading them into leak-proof carts and hampers, and transferring everything to onsite compactors or containers. You'll also keep common areas clean: pool decks, dog waste stations, mail kiosks, parking lots, and compactor pads all fall within your scope.

Beyond the physical collection work, you're expected to flag improper or prohibited waste, clean spills as you find them, and interact professionally with residents and staff throughout your shift. Documentation matters here. You'll log start and end times and submit photo-supported reports through a mobile app, so comfort with a smartphone is a real requirement, not an afterthought.

The role also requires a valid Class E driver's license, the ability to lift up to 50 lbs, climb stairs, and work outdoors in all weather. If strong odors are a dealbreaker, this one isn't the right fit. That's worth saying plainly.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • High school diploma or equivalent (preferred)
  • At least one year in waste services or a customer-facing role
  • Working knowledge of EPA and OSHA regulations around waste handling
  • Clear, professional communication with residents and on-site staff
  • Bilingual ability is a plus
  • Ability to follow written instructions and operate basic equipment
  • Personal smartphone (Android 2.2+ or iPhone iOS 4.3+) with an active data plan and email
  • Valid Class E driver's license

Cortland is a vertically integrated multifamily owner-operator, one of the larger ones in the country, which means their properties tend to run with more structure and more accountability than you'd find at a smaller management shop. Routes have productivity standards. Reporting is tracked. That's not a complaint, just context. If you do the job well and consistently, there's a real organization behind this role.

One thing worth noting for candidates coming from general labor or service backgrounds: valet trash on a Class A multifamily property is resident-facing work. You're the person residents see on their doorstep several nights a week. How you carry yourself on the route directly affects how the community feels to the people living there. The best candidates in this type of role treat it like a service job, not just a labor job.

greenhouse_5126425007.title: Accounting Assistant
greenhouse_5126425007.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role sits at the intersection of property accounting and building administration, which means your day pulls in two directions at once. On the accounting side, you're coding and entering invoices in MRI or Yardi Voyager, posting tenant receipts, verifying monthly rents, distributing rent statements, and compiling the closing reports that keep the management team honest about where the numbers stand. New vendor setups, cash verifications, monthly reporting packages. It's detail work, and the margin for error is small.

On the administrative side, you're the connective tissue of the building office. You answer phones, manage lease and building files, maintain tenant and emergency contact lists, track certificates of insurance for both tenants and vendors (including renewal dates and coverage requirements), and handle amenity requests like fitness center memberships and bike room access. You'll also coordinate after-hours vendor access, which means confirming insurance is current and making sure building staff have everything they need before anyone shows up at an odd hour.

This is a fully in-office position in Boston. No hybrid schedule, no remote option. That's worth knowing up front.

What You Bring

  • High school diploma required; some accounting coursework or experience is a genuine advantage
  • Familiarity with MRI, Yardi Voyager, or comparable property management software is helpful, though not required
  • Solid Microsoft Office skills, particularly Word and Excel
  • Strong written and verbal communication. You'll be in contact with tenants, vendors, and internal staff regularly
  • Organized enough to track COI expirations, manage multiple file systems, and juggle accounting tasks without things slipping
  • Comfortable occasionally working beyond standard hours when the building or reporting cycle demands it

A Few Things Worth Knowing

The COI tracking piece deserves more attention than most postings give it. Keeping current certificates on file for every active vendor and tenant, and following up when coverage lapses or expires, is one of those tasks that feels administrative until something goes wrong. If you're meticulous about that kind of detail, you'll be genuinely useful here.

For someone early in a property management career, this type of role builds a practical foundation that transfers well. You'll touch accounts payable workflows, tenant billing, vendor management, and lease file administration all at once. Those are skills that show up on the job description for property accountant, assistant property manager, and building administrator roles alike. Lincoln Property Company operates across a large commercial portfolio, so internal mobility is a realistic consideration for someone who performs well and wants to grow.

Pay is $31 to $36 per hour. Lincoln Property Company is one of the larger private real estate operators in the country, with a portfolio that spans commercial, office, life science, and mixed-use assets.
greenhouse_5126425007.description_text: In cooperation with the Property Management Staff, the Accounting Administrator works to meet the accounting and administrative demands of the building management office. Duties and Responsibilities: Accounting Duties Code and Input invoices in MRI, Yardi Voyager or similar Property Management Software Input of Tenant receipts in MRI, Yardi Voyager or similar Property Management Software Verify monthly rents and distribute rent statements Prepare monthly closing reports, including cash verifications Complete new vendor requests and set-ups Compile monthly reports. Administrative Answer phone in a professional manner providing answers when possible and transferring calls or taking messages as needed Open, stamp and distribute mail Maintain tenant contacts lists Maintain emergency contact lists Maintain certificate of insurance files for tenants and vendors, tracking dates and coverages Maintain lease and building files, create new files as needed Process tenant amenity requests including fitness center membership, interior bike room use and exterior bike parking. Prepare and send all outgoing mail and packages Coordinating vendor after-hours access, verifying proper insurance coverages and communicating all information to building staff Education and Experience High school diploma required Basic Accounting experience a plus Some experience with MRI, Yardi Voyager or other property management software a plus Qualifications: Advanced communication skills both oral and written Strong organizational skills and attention to detail Self-starter, able to manage multiple projects under pressure Ability to work efficiently as a team member, dealing with coworkers and clients at all levels and disciplines Ability to work independently Ability to occasionally work flexible hours which may exceed 8 hours per day or 40 hours per week Personable and friendly demeanor in person, on phone and in email Proficient knowledge of Microsoft Office, including Word, Excel and Adobe Ability to gain proficient knowledge of other software programs as required This position is 100% in-office . The role requires working in office during standard business hours. Remote work or telecommuting is not an option for this position. Pay Range $31 — $36 USD About Lincoln Property Company Lincoln Property Company (“Lincoln”) is one of the largest private real estate firms in the United States. Offering a fully integrated platform of real estate services and innovative solutions to owners, investors, lenders and occupiers, Lincoln supports the entire real estate lifecycle across asset types, including office, multifamily, life science, retail, industrial, data center, production studio, healthcare, government, universities, and mixed-use properties, throughout the United States, United Kingdom, and Europe. Lincoln’s combined management and leasing portfolio on behalf of institutional clients includes more than 680 million square feet of commercial space. For more information, visit: www.lpc.com . All job offers are contingent on completion of a background check and proof of eligibility to work in the United States. greenhouse_5986742004.title: Regional Manager
greenhouse_5986742004.rewritten_description:
What This Role Demands From Day One

Regional management is fundamentally a coordination role. You're not turning units yourself or sitting at a leasing desk, but you're accountable for everything that happens at both. The skills this position draws on most heavily are financial analysis, personnel development, and the ability to hold multiple sites to a consistent standard without being physically present at any of them on a given day. If you're someone who can read a T-12, spot a delinquency trend before it compounds, and also have a direct conversation with a Community Manager who's struggling with team morale, this role is built for that range.

Asset Living is a third-party management firm, which shapes this role in a specific way. You're not managing assets on behalf of an internal owner. You're the face of the company to external ownership groups, which means your financial reporting, your property inspections, and your staff decisions all carry client relationship weight. That's a different kind of pressure than in-house management, and it's worth understanding before you step into it.

What You'll Actually Be Managing

The portfolio spans multifamily, single-family rentals, affordable housing, build-to-rent, and student housing, so the operational mix across your sites may not be uniform. That breadth is genuinely interesting if you've worked across asset classes, but it also means you can't rely on one playbook for every property.

  • Personnel: recruiting, training, coaching, and when necessary, terminating on-site staff. You'll approve timesheets, sign off on salary requests, and work closely with HR to document performance issues correctly.
  • Financial oversight: building and monitoring operating budgets, reviewing monthly income and expense variances, flagging capex needs, and ensuring ownership reports go out accurately and on time.
  • Leasing strategy: developing annual marketing plans, evaluating staff leasing effectiveness, and stepping in on resident satisfaction issues that escalate beyond the site level.
  • Administrative and maintenance accountability: regular property inspections, service request turnaround monitoring, vendor contract oversight, and emergency response coordination.

NOI improvement is a core expectation here. You'll be expected to identify where revenue is being left on the table and where expenses are running above budget, then recommend concrete adjustments, not just flag the variance.

What Separates Strong Candidates From Average Ones

The Regional Managers who thrive in third-party management tend to be people who can compartmentalize well. You might spend the morning reviewing a budget forecast for a Class B garden-style community and the afternoon walking a punch list at a build-to-rent site with a completely different ownership expectation. The ability to shift context without losing rigor is underrated in this role.

Strong candidates also know how to develop Community Managers rather than just manage around them. The on-site team is your primary execution layer. If you're consistently filling gaps yourself instead of building capability in the people below you, the role becomes unsustainable. The best Regional Managers are coaches first and operators second.

Asset Living holds IREM's Accredited Management Organization designation and has ranked in the NMHC top 50. That reputation matters to ownership groups when they're choosing a management partner, and it creates a real standard you'll be expected to uphold across your portfolio. This isn't a role where average performance stays quiet for long.
greenhouse_5986742004.description_text: Company Overview Asset Living is a third-party management firm and a proven partner in fostering thriving communities nationwide. Founded in 1986, Asset Living has decades of experience delivering exceptional value to our partners. Since the beginning, our undeniable passion has driven our organic growth from a small property management firm to one of the most trusted allies in real estate. Asset Living's growing portfolio includes a multitude of properties across the country that span the multifamily, single-family home rentals, affordable housing, build-to-rent, active adult, and student housing divisions. Asset Living is a fast-growing company made up of talented individuals from diverse backgrounds that differentiate us and help us drive innovation and results for our clients and communities across the US. At Asset Living, we wholeheartedly believe 'Together We Lead, Together We Succeed. We empower each other to lead by example, collaborate, and evolve – inspired by our belief that we can continually improve as individuals and as an organization. Together, we create an environment where every voice is heard, every idea is valued, and every individual experiences opportunities to grow as our company grows. When you join Asset Living, you become part of a dynamic team that thrives on unity, unique talents, and a universal culture of winning. Recognized as one of the nation's Best and Brightest Places to Work and ranked in the NMHC top 50 professionals, join Asset Living because of its reputation as the most trusted partner in real estate, workplace culture, and growth opportunities. Asset Living is a member of The Institute of Real Estate Management (IREM) and is recognized as an Accredited Management Organization (AMO). Together, we lead the way, and together, we achieve excellence in all that we do. Join a workplace where success is a collective journey and leadership is a shared responsibility. Regional Manager The Regional Manager is responsible for overseeing the entire operations of a housing community portfolio. As a Regional Manager, you will manage all phases of the operations, including personnel, leasing, maintenance, financial, administration & risk management. As an offsite leader, you will supervise all aspects of the property and staff to ensure compliance with Asset’s company policies and procedures, safety and fair housing guidelines, and liability concerns. Essential Duties & Responsibilities Personnel Management Use consistent techniques & company directives to screen, hire, train, coach, and develop on-site staff Ensure effectiveness of staff through ongoing training, coaching, counseling, and guidance in compliance with Asset training benchmarks Deal effectively and consistently with performance problems; document adequately, communicate with direct supervisor and HR and terminate appropriately when necessary Prepare request for a salary increase for Community Manager and assist with other team members if necessary; sign off on all requests from the site Ensure all counseling statements, written evaluations, and salary requests are provided for review to the Regional & Senior Vice President and delivered to the Human Resource department Approve all timesheets and ensure hours indicated are correct, vacation and sick time reported, and signatures available Deal effectively and consistently with performance problems; document adequately, communicate with Regional & Senior Vice President and HR and terminate appropriately when necessary Promote harmony and quality job performance of staff through support and effective leadership Ensure staff compliance and consistency with Company policies and procedures Financial Management Achieve the highest possible net operating income through the implementation of effective cost control and revenue & leasing improvement; identify trends and recommend strategies and adjustments Develop yearly operating budgets/forecasts Provide monthly written evaluation of income and expense line items that are significantly over budget Provide Capital improvement suggestions for the future of the site Monitor all proposals and contracts for large projects at sites and check work in progress Monitor & maintain the timely receipt and reconciliation of deposits, rent collections, and charges to ensure submitted on a timely basis Monitor & approve the timely receipt, reconciliation, and coding of all vendor invoices Ensure property closeout is completed on time and ownership financial reports are accurate Strategic Leasing Management Develop yearly marketing plan and utilize marketing strategies & systems Provide marketing strategy to generate rentals or for rent increases Ensure staff leasing techniques are effective in obtaining closure, follow up procedures met, signing/documentation of leases, and reporting systems are accurate and up to date Deal with resident complaints, concerns, and requests to ensure resident satisfaction Develop and implement resident retention programs (i.e., resident functions, special promotions, monthly newsletter, etc.) Effectively show, lease, and move in prospective residents Administrative & Maintenance Management Ensure all administrative & leasing reporting is accurate, complete, and submitted on a timely basis Assist with the emergency team for the property; ensures proper response and handling of all property emergencies with staff, residents, buildings, etc. within company guidelines to minimize liability Manage excellent customer service and monitor service request turnaround and responsiveness of maintenance staff Maintain property appearance and ensure repairs are noted and completed on a timely basis (this requires regular property inspections and tours) Plan for and utilize property resources, equipment and supplies economically (i.e., obtaining bids/pricing from vendors, suppliers, and contractors, dealing with inadequate service, etc.) Give direction to and monitor maintenance, construction, and rehabilitation activities to ensure quality and expediency (i.e., “walk” units to ensure make-ready and work orders are completed) Travel This position entails travel, estimated at 25-50% of work time annually. Travel may be required for property visits, conferences, training sessions, or other business-related activities. It is merely a guide of expected duties. The employee understands that the job description is neither complete nor permanent and may be modified at any time. At the request of their supervisor, an employee may be asked to perform additional duties or take on additional responsibilities without notice. This is a safety-sensitive position and may be subject to additional compliance requirements.

greenhouse_7721867003.title: Assistant Manager - Olympus at The Woodlands
greenhouse_7721867003.rewritten_description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Assistant Manager work is really two jobs running in parallel. You're keeping the financial side clean, collections current, and records accurate while also staying visible to residents and backing up your leasing team when traffic picks up. The skills this position uses hardest are financial accountability, resident communication, and operational follow-through. If any one of those three is weak, the property feels it quickly.

At Olympus at The Woodlands, you'll be the person who makes sure rent is collected and deposited before bank close each day, that move-out account statements go out promptly, and that the office files stay organized and confidential. On the delinquency side, you're not just running reports. You're actively managing the process and escalating appropriately when legal or liability issues surface. The Business Manager leans on you to hold things together during absences, so you need to be comfortable stepping into that decision-making seat without a lot of notice.

The leasing piece is real here, not just a checkbox. You'll jump in to show apartments and support resident retention, which means you need to read a prospect quickly and adjust your approach. Community events and the first weekend of each month require schedule flexibility, so if your availability is rigid, this probably isn't the right fit.

What the Day Actually Looks Like

A typical morning might start with pulling delinquency numbers, following up on outstanding balances, and checking whether make-ready units are actually ready for their move-in dates. Punch list items have a way of appearing at inconvenient times. By midday you could be handling a resident complaint that needs to be resolved before it becomes a formal issue, and by afternoon you're helping a leasing consultant close a prospect who came in without an appointment. Knowing the submarket matters here. The Woodlands is a competitive corridor, and staying current on new construction activity and nearby hiring trends gives you real context when you're talking to prospects about why your community makes sense for them.

RealPage and Onsite experience will make your first few weeks considerably less painful. Olympus uses these platforms for operations, so if you've worked in them before, you can focus on learning the property rather than learning the software at the same time.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

We've seen plenty of leasing consultants cross-train into assistant manager roles and struggle with the financial discipline side. The candidates who do well in this role tend to be the ones who are already comfortable with collections conversations, can read a T-12 without needing it explained, and don't treat the accounting functions as someone else's problem. Prior onsite experience as an Assistant Manager or a cross-trained leasing consultant is required, and that background genuinely shows in how quickly someone gets productive here.

  • Onsite apartment community experience as an Assistant Manager or cross-trained Leasing Consultant
  • RealPage, Knock, or Onsite software experience (strongly preferred)
  • Comfort managing collections, deposits, and move-out dispositions
  • Ability to cover Business Manager responsibilities when needed
  • Schedule flexibility for the first weekend of each month and periodic resident events

Olympus has held a spot on the National Apartment Association's Top Employer list three years running, which tells you something about how they manage internal culture. Monthly renewal incentives and quarterly bonuses are part of the compensation structure, along with a benefits package that includes an apartment rental allowance and a sabbatical program. Growth paths here are real. Assistant Manager is a well-worn stepping stone to Business Manager and eventually Regional roles for people who perform.

You are comfortable in an office environment with first-hand conversations at a moment's notice. You are also ready and willing to assist your Leasing team with showing apartments, providing resident service support, and participating in resident retention efforts and community events. greenhouse_7721860003.title: Assistant Manager - Olympus Creekside Park
greenhouse_7721860003.rewritten_description:
Skills That Drive This Role

The assistant manager position at Olympus Creekside Park in The Woodlands calls on a specific mix of financial discipline, interpersonal awareness, and operational follow-through. You're not purely a leasing consultant, and you're not yet a property manager. You sit in the middle, which means your day pulls you in multiple directions at once. The skill that separates people who thrive here from those who struggle is the ability to shift between a detailed, numbers-focused task and a face-to-face resident conversation without losing momentum on either one.

Financial accuracy is central to this work. Collecting rent, posting payments, reconciling receipts, and making sure every dollar is deposited before bank close each day requires consistency and a low tolerance for cutting corners. If you've worked with RealPage or Onsite before, you'll have a real head start here. If you've used Knock for lead tracking on top of that, even better.

What You'll Actually Be Doing Day to Day

Expect mornings that start with reviewing delinquency, flagging outstanding balances, and touching base with the property manager before the office opens to traffic. From there, the day can shift quickly. A prospective resident walks in and the leasing team is occupied. You step in, show a unit, and handle the conversation. A resident has a complaint about a neighbor. You address it directly and document it properly. A make-ready unit needs a final inspection before a move-in tomorrow. You walk it, catch what maintenance missed, and make sure the punch list gets closed out before the keys transfer.

When the property manager is out, you're running the site. That's not ceremonial. It means making real decisions about operations, escalating liability issues to the regional manager when appropriate, and keeping the team focused. The expectation is that you already understand how a community runs, which is why Olympus is looking for someone with onsite apartment experience, either as an assistant manager or a cross-trained leasing consultant.

The Woodlands is a competitive submarket. Knowing what's being built nearby, what concessions competitors are offering, and how hiring trends in the area affect renter demand is part of doing this job well. That market awareness feeds directly into retention conversations and renewal strategy.

Fit and Culture at Olympus Property

Olympus has been recognized as a top employer by the National Apartment Association three consecutive years running, 2023 through 2025. That kind of recognition doesn't happen by accident, and it also creates a real expectation: the culture they've built requires people who actually want to be part of it, not just people who list "team player" on a resume.

The schedule is Monday through Friday, 9 to 6, with some flexibility for after-hours resident events and the first weekend of each month. That's a reasonable ask for a community-facing role, but it's worth knowing upfront. Resident events aren't optional extras here. They're part of how this team maintains occupancy and builds the kind of retention that shows up in renewal numbers.

  • Onsite apartment experience required (assistant manager or cross-trained leasing)
  • RealPage, Onsite, or Knock experience strongly preferred
  • Comfortable stepping into the property manager role during absences
  • Detail-oriented with financial processes and resident file management
  • Available for occasional weekend and after-hours commitments

Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401(k) with employer matching, educational assistance, pet insurance, short and long-term disability, life insurance, paid time off, an apartment rental allowance, and a sabbatical program. Monthly renewal incentives and quarterly bonuses round out the compensation structure.

greenhouse_7721857003.title: Business Manager - Olympus at The Woodlands
greenhouse_7721857003.rewritten_description:
Running a Luxury Asset in The Woodlands

The Woodlands is one of the stronger submarkets in the Houston metro, with a renter base that expects Class A finishes and service to match. Olympus at The Woodlands sits squarely in that competitive space, and the Business Manager role here is a full-ownership position. You're not just enforcing policy. You're running the asset.

Olympus Property uses the title "Business Manager" deliberately. This is a property manager role in scope, but the expectation is that you think like an operator. That means owning the financial performance of the property from top to bottom: reviewing monthly performance against budget, building annual business plans, and having informed conversations with your Regional Manager and Regional VP about where the numbers are headed and why. You'll manage rents, occupancy, and expenditures with enough fluency to spot variance before it becomes a problem.

What the Day-to-Day Actually Looks Like

On any given week, you might be reviewing a vendor bid for a capital project in the morning, walking units with maintenance to confirm make-ready quality in the afternoon, and hosting a resident event that evening. The role is genuinely varied. You'll oversee both office and maintenance staff, which means your leadership skills get tested across two very different team cultures simultaneously.

Resident retention and renewal programs fall under your watch, as does leasing performance. You're responsible for marketing strategy and execution, not just approving what someone else builds. Regular property inspections, vendor contract negotiations, and compliance oversight are all part of the standard workload. Olympus uses RealPage, Knock, and OneSite, so familiarity with that stack will matter from day one.

  • Minimum two years of multifamily property manager experience required
  • Luxury Class A experience is strongly preferred
  • RealPage, Knock, and OneSite proficiency preferred
  • Full-time Monday through Friday with schedule flexibility for events and emergencies

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Plenty of property managers can handle occupancy and leasing traffic. The candidates who stand out at a company like Olympus are the ones who can read a T-12 with the same comfort they bring to a resident conversation. Class A residents in a master-planned community like The Woodlands have high expectations and they're not shy about voicing them. Operators who stay calm under that pressure, coach their teams well, and still hit NOI targets are the ones who build long careers here.

Olympus earned the National Apartment Association's Top Employer designation three consecutive years (2023, 2024, 2025), which signals something real about how the company invests in its people. They offer career pathing support, leadership conferences, and educational assistance alongside the standard benefits package. For an experienced property manager ready to run a premium asset with genuine company backing, this is a serious opportunity.
