greenhouse_5128120007.title: Leasing Consultant - Valdok Apartments
greenhouse_5128120007.rewritten_description:
MG Properties has been acquiring and managing apartment communities across the Western U.S. for over 30 years. They run a sizable portfolio and have a real track record of promoting leasing staff into senior roles. This opening at Valdok Apartments in Seattle puts you on the front line of that operation.

What the Job Looks Like Day to Day

You're the first person a prospective resident meets. That first impression drives traffic conversion, and conversion drives occupancy. Most of your time splits between touring apartments, qualifying applicants, and preparing leases. You'll coordinate with maintenance to make sure move-ins go smoothly, which means staying on top of make-ready timelines and following up before a new resident walks through the door.

The administrative side is real. Rental files need to stay current. Data entry into the property management system needs to be accurate. Monthly paperwork doesn't manage itself. Expect to handle resident complaints and rent collection conversations too, not just the fun parts of leasing.

What You Bring

  • At least one year of property management or leasing experience (preferred)
  • Strong sales instincts and genuine customer service ability
  • Professional communication, written and verbal
  • Comfort working in a team environment and sharing accountability for occupancy goals
  • Ability to stay organized under the competing demands of tours, applications, and admin tasks

Compensation and What Separates Strong Candidates

Pay runs $24 to $25 per hour depending on experience, with monthly and quarterly bonuses tied to performance. The package includes profit sharing, a 401(k) with employer match, medical/dental/vision, paid holidays, sick time, and a rental discount.

The candidates who do well here aren't just likable. They close. They follow up. They treat the application process with the same energy they bring to a tour. Seattle's rental market has stayed competitive, which means traffic quality varies and your ability to convert qualified prospects quickly matters. MG Properties promotes from within consistently, so this role is a reasonable entry point toward an Assistant Manager or Property Manager track if you execute.

greenhouse_5127701007.title: Maintenance Technician - Marina Village
greenhouse_5127701007.rewritten_description:
A resident puts in a service request at 8 a.m. for a leaking kitchen faucet. By noon, you've also handled a clogged drain in 204, touched up paint in a freshly turned unit, and walked the grounds to clear debris before the afternoon leasing traffic picks up. That's a normal Tuesday at Marina Village.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

MG Properties has been acquiring and managing apartment communities across the Western U.S. for more than 30 years. Marina Village in Sparks, NV is one of those communities, and they're adding a Maintenance Technician to the team.

Day to day, you're covering the full range of maintenance work: completing resident service requests, handling appliance repairs, painting, plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and apartment turns. You'll also keep common areas and grounds clean and take a rotation of on-call emergency calls. No single trade dominates. You need to be comfortable moving across all of them.

Turns are a real part of this job. Getting a unit make-ready on schedule requires attention to detail on the punch list and the ability to work efficiently without cutting corners. Residents notice the difference.

What You'll Need

  • At least 2 years of apartment maintenance experience, ideally on a property of 200 units or more
  • Working knowledge across trades: plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and appliance repair
  • Reliable transportation (this is required, not preferred)
  • Availability for on-call rotation
  • A customer service mindset. Residents are your internal customers, and how you communicate matters as much as how fast you fix things

MG Properties offers monthly bonuses, company profit sharing, medical, dental, and vision coverage, a 401k with employer match, paid holidays, paid sick time, and rental discounts. Background check and drug screening are required for the role.

One thing that separates strong candidates here: technicians who can self-prioritize a work order queue without constant supervision tend to thrive in multi-trade roles like this. If you're used to waiting to be told what's next, the pace will feel uncomfortable. If you're used to owning your day, it won't.

greenhouse_5124557007.title: Senior Leasing Consultant - San Diego
greenhouse_5124557007.rewritten_description:
The Role in Brief

Senior Leasing Consultants at mid-to-large apartment operators do more than show units. They own the front-end resident experience, from the first phone call through lease execution, while keeping occupancy numbers where they need to be. At MG Properties, that means working primarily at their Domain community in San Diego, with the possibility of supporting other properties in the market.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Most of your day centers on leasing activity. You're greeting prospects, qualifying applicants, and walking people through the leasing process start to finish. You prepare leases and sit with residents during signing. You coordinate with maintenance to make sure move-ins go smoothly, because a rough move-in is the first impression no one recovers from.

Beyond traffic and tours, there's real administrative work here. Rental files need to stay current. Data entry into the property management system has to be accurate. Monthly reporting tasks and paperwork require organization and follow-through. You're also a point of contact for current residents handling complaints, maintenance requests, and rent collection issues.

  • Greet and qualify prospective residents promptly and professionally
  • Prepare and execute lease agreements with new residents
  • Coordinate with maintenance for smooth move-ins
  • Maintain accurate and current rental files and system data
  • Handle resident concerns including maintenance follow-ups and payment issues
  • Support monthly administrative and reporting tasks

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

One year of property management experience is preferred. The candidates who stand out aren't just good at tours. They understand the full leasing cycle, keep their files tight, and don't let follow-ups fall through the cracks. San Diego is a competitive rental market with high renter expectations, especially at the Class A level. Prospects here have options, and the difference between a lease and a lost lead often comes down to response time and how well you present the community.

MG Properties promotes from within, and this role is a realistic path toward an Assistant Manager or Property Manager position. If you're looking to build a career in multifamily rather than just work a leasing desk, the structure here supports that. Pay runs $23 to $25 per hour depending on experience, with monthly and quarterly bonuses, profit sharing, and a full benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401k with employer match, and rental discounts.

greenhouse_8534858002.title: Assistant Community Manager
greenhouse_8534858002.location: Canvas Townhomes,3217 Old 63 S, Columbia, MO 65201
greenhouse_8534858002.rewritten_description:
Student Housing Operations at Canvas Townhomes

The Assistant Community Manager role at Canvas Townhomes in Columbia, MO sits squarely at the intersection of leasing performance and day-to-day operations. Student housing runs on a different clock than conventional multifamily. Annual turns are compressed, delinquency tolerance is low, and occupancy targets are tied to academic calendars rather than rolling traffic. This role touches all of it.

Scion is one of the larger operators in the student housing space, and this position supports the General Manager across the full operational picture: financial performance, staff development, facilities, resident accounts, and customer experience. You'll step in as acting GM when needed, which means you genuinely need to understand every moving part of the asset, not just the pieces directly assigned to you.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

On the financial side, you're keeping delinquency under 2% at month end, reconciling accounts receivable, posting damage charges from quarterly inspections, and helping prepare month-end reports. That's not just data entry. It requires consistent follow-through on collections policy, NSF enforcement, and final billing at move-out.

Leasing and customer experience are equally front-and-center. You'll manage sales interactions directly and direct team members to do the same. Weekly market surveys, concession budget management, and local marketing recommendations all fall within your scope. You're expected to use KPIs to spot trends before they become problems, whether that's slipping traffic-to-lease conversion or a spike in escalated resident concerns.

The annual turn process is a defining moment in student housing. Every bed needs to be inspected, charged if necessary, and made ready for the incoming wave of residents, often within a tight summer window. Your coordination of that process directly affects both the resident experience and the property's financial performance heading into the new lease year.

  • Staff coaching, scheduling, and Fair Housing compliance oversight
  • Resident account management and utility billing coordination
  • Routine inspections of grounds, amenities, and vacant units
  • Work order coordination with maintenance staff and vendors
  • Move-in, transfer, and move-out process execution
  • Escalated resident concern resolution

Who Performs Well Here

Candidates who thrive in this role typically come in with at least a year of on-site property management experience and a real comfort with property management software. Entrata experience is a plus given Scion's platform preference. Strong written communication matters because you're drafting legal notices, resident correspondence, and account documentation on a regular basis.

What separates strong candidates from average ones is the ability to hold both the relational and the operational sides of the job at the same time. Student residents have high service expectations and often low tolerance for administrative friction. The best ACMs here are the ones who can resolve a billing dispute and immediately pivot to a leasing conversation without losing composure in either direction.

Scion offers a benefits package that includes health, dental, and vision coverage, 401k matching, paid parental and maternal leave, a discretionary annual bonus, and learning reimbursement. This role is based on-site at Canvas Townhomes and requires availability for after-hours on-call coverage during peak periods. Work authorization in the United States is required. Visa sponsorship is not available.

greenhouse_8534584002.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_8534584002.location: The Verge at Grand Forks, Grand Forks, North Dakota, United States
greenhouse_8534584002.rewritten_description:
Student housing runs on a different rhythm than conventional multifamily. Residents move in waves, turns happen fast and hard, and the community around you is full of people who have never lived independently before. If you're a maintenance tech who can keep pace with that environment and still treat a work order like it matters, this role at The Verge at Grand Forks is worth a close look.

Scion has built its reputation specifically in the student living space, and the Facilities Technician role sits at the center of day-to-day operations. You're responding to service requests, knocking out preventive maintenance, and making sure units are ready for incoming residents. That last part carries real weight in student housing. Turn season is compressed and unforgiving. Punch lists don't wait, and a unit that isn't ready on move-in day is a much bigger problem here than in conventional apartment communities.

On a typical day, you might handle a plumbing fixture repair in the morning, replace a lighting element in a common area, document everything in the system before end of shift, and field an urgent call that afternoon about an appliance that stopped working. The documentation piece isn't optional. Clear, complete notes on work performed, parts used, and any follow-up needed are part of how the team stays coordinated and how the property protects itself. You'll also be maintaining HVAC components, cleaning coils and condensers, swapping filters, and lubricating equipment as part of a scheduled PM program.

On-call responsibility comes with the position. That's a real commitment, not a footnote. After-hours and weekend emergencies happen, and the expectation is that you're reachable and responsive outside of standard hours when it's your rotation. That's the honest reality of this work, and it's worth knowing upfront.

What separates a strong candidate here isn't just technical range, though that matters. It's the ability to work well with young residents who may not communicate maintenance issues clearly, who sometimes cause the problems they're reporting, and who still deserve to be treated with patience and professionalism. People skills and wrenches go together in this role.

What Scion is Looking for

  • High school diploma or GED
  • Certified Pool Operator (CPO) credential, or willingness to obtain within six months of hire
  • Valid driver's license
  • Demonstrated experience across basic plumbing, electrical, carpentry, painting, and appliance repair
  • Comfort with basic computer use for work order documentation and communication
  • Physical ability to stand for extended periods and lift up to 100 lbs.
  • Flexible availability, including on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies

Work authorization in the United States is required. Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.

greenhouse_8535196002.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_8535196002.location: Catherine Commons, Ithaca, New York, United States
greenhouse_8535196002.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a hands-on maintenance role at Catherine Commons, a student housing property in Ithaca, New York. You're responding to service requests, completing make-readies before residents move in, and keeping the property's systems running day to day. That means basic plumbing, appliance repair, painting, carpentry, and electrical work. It also means preventive maintenance: cleaning coils and condensers, swapping air filters, lubricating equipment, and catching small issues before they become expensive ones.

Documentation matters here. Every completed work order gets logged with notes on what you did, parts used, and any follow-up needed. You're also responsible for flagging supply needs before they run out. Student housing runs on tight turn schedules, and the team needs accurate, timely information to stay on track.

You'll carry an on-call rotation. After-hours and weekend calls are part of the deal. Ithaca winters are real, and the property expects you to show up when things go wrong off the clock.

What You Bring

  • High school diploma or GED
  • Certified Pool Operator (CPO) credential, or willingness to earn it within six months
  • Valid driver's license
  • Demonstrated experience with plumbing, basic electrical, appliance repair, painting, and carpentry
  • Comfort with basic computer use for work order systems and documentation
  • Ability to stand for extended periods and lift up to 100 lbs.
  • Flexible availability for emergencies and weekend coverage

Context Worth Knowing

Student housing has a distinct rhythm compared to conventional multifamily. Lease terms are often tied to the academic calendar, which means the summer turn window is compressed and high-volume. Every unit needs to be cleaned, repaired, and ready before move-in day. Strong technicians in this environment are the ones who can work efficiently through a punch list without cutting corners, and who communicate clearly when a unit isn't going to be ready on time.

The pay range for this role in New York is $24 to $26 per hour, with a discretionary annual bonus and a full benefits package including health, dental, vision, 401k matching, paid parental leave, and learning reimbursement. Scion does not sponsor work visas.

greenhouse_4238500009.title: Assistant Community Manager (Oroysom Village)
greenhouse_4238500009.rewritten_description:
Skills That Drive This Role

This position runs on two core skill sets: compliance precision and people fluency. At Oroysom Village, you're working within affordable housing regulations that leave little room for error. Tax Credit Allocation Committee (TCAC) reviews, Management and Occupancy Reviews (MOR), income verifications, and re-certifications all require someone who can follow a process completely, document it correctly, and meet a deadline without being reminded. That's the compliance half.

The people half is just as demanding. Residents come from diverse economic and social backgrounds, and many are navigating real instability in their lives. You'll handle rent collection, issue notices, coordinate resident meetings, and communicate emergency preparedness procedures. That requires someone who can be direct and consistent while still being genuinely decent to people who may be having a hard time. Bilingual fluency in English and Mandarin is required for this role, which reflects the resident population at Oroysom Village and is a real operational need, not a checkbox.

What You're Actually Managing Day to Day

On any given day you might process a lease agreement in the morning, walk the property to flag a maintenance issue before noon, and follow up on an overdue work order by afternoon. Turns are part of the rhythm. You'll schedule vacancies, prepare turnover forms, and keep the process moving so units don't sit idle. You'll also support bank deposit preparation and log petty cash, which means the property's fiscal records run through your hands regularly.

File maintenance is another constant. Resident files in affordable housing carry serious compliance weight. One missing document in a TCAC file can create audit exposure. Keeping records complete, current, and confidential isn't background work here. It's core to the job.

You'll report directly to the Community Manager and work closely with the Resident Services team. That collaboration matters. Property management and resident services don't always operate in sync at every organization, but MidPen's model puts them in close contact by design. Understanding where one team's work ends and the other's begins makes you more effective in both directions.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

Affordable housing compliance is its own discipline. Candidates who've worked at market-rate properties will find some of the regulatory layers unfamiliar. Experience with RealPage, income certifications, or any HUD or tax credit program gives you a head start. That said, strong administrative instincts and genuine comfort with detail can carry someone who's willing to learn the regulatory side quickly.

  • High school diploma or GED with at least one year of residential property management, customer service, or administrative experience
  • Proficiency in MS Office; RealPage experience is a plus
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Bilingual in English and Mandarin (required)
  • Organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable working independently
  • Alignment with MidPen's mission around affordable, dignified housing

Pay runs from $21.15 to $23.80 per hour based on experience. MidPen carries a full benefits package including health, dental, vision, 403(b) retirement, paid parental leave, and an education reimbursement program, among others.

For someone who wants a career path in property management, the ACM role at an affordable housing nonprofit builds a compliance foundation that transfers directly into Community Manager roles, regional positions, or even compliance specialist tracks. The regulatory knowledge you build here is genuinely hard to find and consistently in demand across the sector.
greenhouse_4238500009.description_text: About MidPen At MidPen, we build communities that change lives. Since 1970, we have been committed to our mission: to provide safe, affordable housing of high quality to those in need; establish stability and opportunity in the lives of residents; and foster diverse communities that allow people from all ethnic, social, and economic backgrounds to live in dignity, harmony, and mutual respect. We develop affordable housing, provide professional property management for all our communities, and coordinate robust onsite support programs that help residents thrive. We are active in 12 Northern California counties and have earned recognition as one of the nation’s leading non-profit developers and stewards of affordable housing. Our current portfolio of more than 130 properties is home to 20,000 working families, seniors, and people with special needs. We take pride in delivering affordable housing solutions, and we invite you to join us on a journey toward social, economic, and racial equity. MidPen is, once again, proud to be certified as an official Great Place to Work® in 2025. Property Management works closely with our residents, our community partners, and their fellow MidPenners, including close collaboration with our Resident Services team. As the Assistant Community Manager, you will report to the Community Manager. You’ll be responsible for supporting the daily operations of the property, including key areas of responsibility like fiscal management, compliance, facilities, risk management, and resident relations. The requirements listed below are representative of the education, knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Proficient in MS Office and/or RealPage software. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Excellent customer service with measurable outcomes. Able to work independently and collaboratively with minimal supervision. Highly organized and detail-oriented. Committed to the mission and values of MidPen Services and MidPen Housing This role primarily involves desk-based computer work with frequent sitting. Occasional physical activity includes standing, walking, reaching overhead, filing, and lifting or moving items up to 15 pounds. Rarely, the position may require bending, squatting, or kneeling. Education and Experience: High School diploma or GED and one year of residential property management, customer service or administrative experience, or an equivalent combination of education and relevant experience Pay Range: $21.15 - $23.80 Hourly - Pay based on applicable experience and qualifications Bilingual in English and Mandarin required Benefits and Compensation (see here for full details ) Health Insurance Dental, Vision, Life & Disability Insurance 403(b) Retirement Investment Employee Education Reimbursement Program Paid Parental Leave FSA for Childcare, Medical, and Commuter Benefits EAP Program Pet Insurance Paid Time Off Company Holidays Wellness Days EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER MidPen Housing Corp. provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. greenhouse_4239342009.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_4239342009.rewritten_description:
Leasing is a skill set, not just a job title. At Hawthorne Residential Partners, the Leasing Consultant role is built around three disciplines that experienced multifamily professionals recognize immediately: consultative sales, relationship management, and community representation. If you've developed any of these skills in retail, hospitality, or apartment leasing, this role gives you a place to use all three at once.

Skills This Role Puts to Work Every Day

The core of this position is converting prospect traffic into signed leases. That means you'll draw on active listening to understand what a prospect actually needs, not just what they say they're looking for. Tours aren't walkthroughs; they're guided conversations. You'll field inquiries by phone, email, and web, and your ability to follow up consistently and persuasively is what separates a full apartment from a partially occupied one.

You'll also handle the administrative side of leasing accurately: applications, verification paperwork, and move-in documentation. Attention to detail here protects the community's compliance record and sets new residents up for a smooth start. Occupancy and renewal goals are real benchmarks you'll contribute to, and monthly leasing and renewal commissions mean your results show up directly in your paycheck.

Skills This Role Builds

Hawthorne offers a defined career path program, and this role is a common entry point into assistant community manager responsibilities. The skills you develop here, including objection handling, market comp awareness, resident communication, and event coordination, transfer directly into operations-level roles. Many property management professionals who move into management trace their foundation back to time spent on the leasing floor.

You'll also build familiarity with local market positioning. Savannah's rental market has seen sustained demand driven by population growth, a strong tourism and hospitality economy, and continued interest from out-of-state renters. Knowing how to position your community against competitors in that context is a practical skill that carries into any leasing or management role you take on later.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

  • Coachability: Hawthorne has a defined service culture they call Live It. Candidates who learn it and apply it consistently outperform those who rely on instinct alone.
  • Follow-through: Most lost leases aren't lost on the tour. They're lost in the follow-up. Disciplined lead management is a real differentiator.
  • Comfort with a sales schedule: The office runs Monday through Friday with Saturday hours, and resident events may occasionally extend your week. That's standard in leasing, but worth knowing upfront.
  • Communication range: You'll need to be equally effective in person, over the phone, and in writing. One weak channel limits your conversion rate.

A high school diploma or GED is the minimum education requirement. One year of customer service or sales experience is preferred, and a valid driver's license is required. A CALP accreditation is a plus if you already have it, but it's not expected at entry. Hawthorne provides learning and development support to help you grow from where you are now.

greenhouse_4237928009.title: Maintenance Technician II
greenhouse_4237928009.location: Hawthorne at Pine Forest - 1045 Woodsia Way - Oak Island, NC - 28461
greenhouse_4237928009.rewritten_description:
It's 7:45 AM on a Tuesday and a resident just submitted a work order for a tripped breaker and a running toilet. By 9:00, you've knocked out both, logged them, and you're already pulling a punch list on a vacant unit that needs to be make-ready before Friday's move-in. That's the rhythm of this role at Hawthorne Residential Partners' Oak Island community, Hawthorne at Pine Forest.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

As a Maintenance Technician II, you'll carry your own work order queue and handle repairs independently. That means diagnosing HVAC issues, plumbing repairs, appliance fixes, and general unit maintenance without needing a supervisor walking you through every step. Make-ready turns are a core part of the job. When a resident vacates, you're restoring that unit to Hawthorne's standards so it's ready for the next resident on time. That work directly connects to occupancy, and occupancy drives everything.

Day-to-day responsibilities also include preventative maintenance on schedule, building and common area upkeep, grounds cleanliness, and pool maintenance. You'll keep the shop organized well enough that anyone can do a quick inventory scan without digging through boxes. On-call emergency rotation is part of the deal, which is honest work but worth knowing upfront before you apply.

What Hawthorne Is Looking for

  • High school diploma or GED
  • Two years of apartment maintenance experience (preferred)
  • Valid driver's license
  • EPA/HVAC certification (required)
  • CPO certification is a plus

The technical baseline matters, but so does reliability. A technician who shows up, closes work orders clean, and treats residents with genuine professionalism will outperform someone with more credentials but spotty follow-through. Hawthorne tracks resident satisfaction, and your work feeds directly into those scores.

Strong candidates at this level already know the difference between a warranty repair and a deferred maintenance item, and they don't wait to be told which is which. If you've worked at a garden-style or coastal property before, you'll recognize the grind of keeping exterior common areas and pool equipment in shape through a humid Carolina summer. That experience translates here.

Compensation includes hourly pay plus a monthly renewal bonus, quarterly performance bonuses, and quarterly resident satisfaction bonuses. Benefits include free dental, medical and vision coverage, 401k match, paid time off (including your birthday), pet insurance, paid parental and adoption leave, telehealth access, and company-paid life insurance. Hawthorne also offers a formal career path program with a clear progression from Technician II to Technician III, plus education reimbursement for maintenance-related training.

Standard schedule is Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies.

greenhouse_4692176005.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_4692176005.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role puts you at the center of everything happening at Dover Hills Apartments. On any given day, you're fielding phone inquiries from prospects, walking tours, processing applications, and following up with a new resident to make sure their move-in went smoothly. The job blends sales, customer service, and administrative work in roughly equal measure, and no two days look the same.

You'll handle the full leasing cycle: from that first phone call or walk-in through application processing, move-in paperwork, and renewal conversations. You'll also inspect vacant units and common areas to make sure they're showing well, assist with local outreach and marketing efforts, and help plan resident events. The role reports to the property manager, who reviews and approves the paperwork you process.

One honest note: leasing work requires you to be "on" with people consistently, even on slow traffic days or when a difficult prospect walks through the door. If you find genuine energy in turning a skeptical prospect into a signed lease, this role will suit you well.

What You Bring

  • At least one year in a customer-facing sales or service role; six or more months of on-site leasing experience is preferred
  • High school diploma or equivalent required; some college coursework is a plus
  • Comfort with MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and ideally some exposure to Yardi
  • Working knowledge of Fair Housing regulations, or a genuine willingness to learn them quickly
  • Strong attention to detail, particularly with lease documents and application paperwork
  • A communication style that works equally well in person, on the phone, and in writing

Context Worth Knowing

Paragon Properties is a Michigan-based company with a clear focus on long-term resident relationships and team development. They offer a structured training program, an employee recognition program, a leasing move-in bonus, and a rent discount alongside a full benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with employer contribution, and company-paid life and disability insurance. Starting pay is $18.50/hour, negotiable based on experience.

What separates strong candidates in this specific role isn't just sales energy. It's the ability to stay organized through a lease-up push while still delivering a personal, attentive experience to every prospect. Leasing consultants who track their own follow-up diligently, know their unit inventory cold, and treat every renewal conversation with the same care as a new lease tend to advance into assistant manager and property manager roles faster than those who focus purely on closing new traffic.

Kalamazoo has a steady rental market supported by Western Michigan University and a growing healthcare and manufacturing base, which means consistent prospect traffic across seasons. Dover Hills is a garden-style community, which typically means a mix of resident profiles and a pace that rewards relationship-building over high-volume churn tactics.

Pre-employment background check, drug screening, and physical are required before your start date.
greenhouse_4692176005.description_text: Are you a people-person with a knack for creating connections and a passion for providing exceptional customer service? At Paragon Properties, we are searching for an enthusiastic and personable Leasing Consultant to join the Dover Hills Apartments team! As a Leasing Consultant, you’ll be the face of our community, guiding prospective residents through the leasing process and ensuring current residents feel at home. With your ability to build relationships and attention to detail, you’ll play a vital role in shaping a positive living experience. If you thrive in a dynamic environment and are ready to make a difference, we’d love to have you on our team! greenhouse_4692178005.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_4692178005.rewritten_description:
The Skills This Role Runs On

Maintenance technician work at a residential community is about breadth. On any given day you might be diagnosing a tripped breaker, pulling a faucet cartridge, patching drywall before a turn, and checking pool chemistry before noon. The technician who thrives here isn't a specialist in one trade. They're competent across plumbing, electrical, HVAC, appliances, and basic carpentry, and they move between those disciplines without losing momentum. Customer service matters too. Residents notice how you show up just as much as whether you fixed the problem.

Dover Hills Apartments is a Paragon Properties community in Kalamazoo. The role is a standard full-time, 40-hour-per-week position with on-call rotation included. That means after-hours calls will happen, including weekends and holidays. Paragon does pay an on-call bonus for that, which is worth noting since many operators don't structure it that way.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Make-readies will be part of your regular rhythm. Preparing units for occupancy means working through a punch list that typically includes painting, basic drywall repair, and rough carpentry alongside the mechanical checks. You'll also handle resident service requests, which at a community this size means volume and variety. Troubleshooting HVAC equipment, evaluating appliance failures, and maintaining pool and spa equipment all fall within scope.

Curb appeal and property condition reporting are part of the job too. You're expected to flag potential liabilities and propose improvements when you spot them. That kind of proactive eye is something the better technicians in this industry develop over time, and it's genuinely valued in this role.

  • 1 or more years of hands-on maintenance experience, technical school, or vocational training
  • HVAC Type II or Universal certification strongly preferred
  • Working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, appliances, and general carpentry
  • Valid driver's license and reliable transportation
  • High school diploma required; trade school or college-level coursework strongly preferred
  • Ability to work outdoors across all seasons
  • Pre-employment background check, drug screen, and physical required

What's Distinctive About This Opportunity

Paragon offers a pay range up to $26 per hour depending on experience and certifications, which is competitive for the Kalamazoo market. The benefits package includes paid time off, nine company holidays, a 401(k) with employer contribution, and medical, dental, and vision coverage with a short enrollment wait. There's also a rent discount, company-paid life and disability insurance, and a tool kit provided after your first 30 days. A uniform program and quarterly team bonus round it out.

One thing worth mentioning for candidates thinking about trajectory: technicians who build out their HVAC credentials and get comfortable with turn management often move into lead tech or maintenance supervisor roles within a few years. Paragon has a stated training and certification program, which means the path is at least structured rather than informal. If you're already certified and have a few years behind you, this is a straightforward opportunity to land somewhere stable with a benefits package that holds up. If you're earlier in your career, the certification support is a real asset.

greenhouse_5209614008.title: Floating Leasing Specialist
greenhouse_5209614008.rewritten_description:
This role runs on two core skills: adaptability and closing. You're not anchored to one property. You move where Hillpointe needs you, which means you have to get up to speed fast, read a new community's traffic patterns quickly, and start converting prospects without a warm-up period.

What This Role Actually Involves

Hillpointe is a Sun Belt workforce housing developer with a fully integrated model. They control everything from land acquisition through construction and asset management. That means their communities are purpose-built and consistently positioned, which helps when you're floating between sites. You're not learning a totally different product every time you move.

Day-to-day, you're covering leasing offices during lease-ups, team transitions, and high-traffic periods. You conduct tours, qualify prospects, follow up by phone, text, and email, and keep tour paths and model units presentation-ready. You'll also work weekends. That's not a footnote here. It's a regular part of the schedule.

Temporary or permanent relocation is on the table depending on where business needs land. Free rent at an assigned Hillpointe community is part of the compensation structure, subject to their internal policy.

Skills This Position Relies On

  • Leasing, sales, hospitality, or customer service background. The product changes by property; your ability to build rapport quickly doesn't.
  • Strong follow-up discipline. Leads go cold fast. Consistent outreach through multiple channels is what separates average leasing performance from strong occupancy numbers.
  • Comfort with ambiguity. You won't always have a full week to learn a new community before you're on the floor doing tours.
  • Valid driver's license and willingness to travel frequently between properties in the portfolio.
  • High school diploma or equivalent. Fair housing compliance is required and non-negotiable.

What Makes Strong Candidates Stand Out

The biggest differentiator in a floating role isn't leasing knowledge. It's how fast you can integrate. Someone who walks into a new site, reads the community's selling points, absorbs the current traffic situation, and starts producing without needing two weeks of hand-holding is the person this role is built for. Hospitality backgrounds transfer well here because that instinct to make someone feel welcome immediately is exactly what a lease-up or peak-traffic period demands.

Hillpointe's workforce housing focus also means your prospects are often practical buyers. They want to know what they're getting for the rent. Connecting features to real lifestyle value, not just reciting amenities, is a skill worth sharpening before you walk in the door.
greenhouse_5209614008.description_text: WHY HILLPOINTE? Hillpointe is a fully integrated real estate development and investment management firm focused on developing market-rate workforce housing across the Sun Belt. Ranked at the top of NMHC's list of Builders and Developers, our team ensures best-in-class execution. Built on its long and proven track record of real estate development, the firm’s investment approach is centered around its in-house general contracting expertise, enabling direct control of cost and delivery timeframe. For each project, Hillpointe directly controls land acquisition, land development, construction, procurement of building materials, asset management, and capital markets. Position requires extended periods of computer use. May involve outdoor work while conducting property tours in varying weather conditions. Typical work schedule may include weekends, evenings, or holidays based on leasing activity and business needs. Fast paced, customer focused environment requiring professional demeanor and problem solving skills. Occasional exposure to noise, interruptions, and multiple concurrent tasks. Requires compliance with company policies, fair housing laws, and confidentiality standards.

greenhouse_5987753004.title: Assistant Property Manager
greenhouse_5987753004.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role sits at the operational center of a residential apartment community in Centennial. Day to day, you're handling rent collection, processing payments, and managing delinquency. That means tracking late accounts, preparing notices, and following the legal timeline carefully. You're also maintaining resident files, coordinating with vendors, and supporting the leasing side of the business. When the Property Manager is out, the community runs through you.

Cottonwood Residential was an early mover on self-guided tours, which tells you something about how they approach operations. The leasing experience here isn't purely transactional. You'll be expected to think about how residents and prospects experience the property, not just process paperwork.

What You Bring

  • Background in leasing, sales, or customer service. The combination of people skills and process discipline matters here.
  • Comfort with basic accounting. You don't need to be a bookkeeper, but understanding charges, credits, and ledger entries is part of the daily rhythm.
  • Strong written and verbal communication. You're drafting formal notices and talking to residents in the same afternoon.
  • Familiarity with Fair Housing Laws. This isn't optional knowledge in a leasing environment.
  • Weekend availability. Residential communities don't pause on Saturdays.
  • Entrata Core experience is a plus. If you've worked in another property management software, the transition is manageable.

Where This Role Leads

The Assistant Property Manager title gets underestimated. In practice, it's where you build the full operational picture of a residential asset. Delinquency management, vendor coordination, resident retention, leasing support, and occasional community oversight all run through this position. People who do this job well tend to move into Property Manager roles with a real command of both the financial and human sides of a community. The accounting exposure here, even basic, compounds quickly once you're reading T-12 reports and working toward NOI goals at the manager level.

One thing worth knowing: the gap between a good APM and an average one usually comes down to follow-through on the administrative side. Resident files that stay current, delinquency that gets addressed early, vendor communication that doesn't fall through the cracks. The leasing part is visible. The backend discipline is what earns trust with a Property Manager and sets you up for the next step.

greenhouse_5977980004.title: Maintenance Supervisor
greenhouse_5977980004.rewritten_description:
It's 7:15 a.m. on a Monday and you've already got two HVAC calls from the weekend on-call log, a turn that needs to be punched by Wednesday, and a new tech who's still learning the ropes. That's a pretty average morning for a Maintenance Supervisor at The Orchards at Cherry Creek Park in Centennial, CO. Cottonwood Residential is looking for someone who doesn't flinch at that kind of start.

What This Role Actually Involves

You'll lead the maintenance team day to day, which means your job is equal parts hands-on technician and people manager. The make-ready process will sit squarely on your shoulders. Turns need to be tight, punch lists need to be thorough, and apartments need to be rent-ready on schedule so the leasing side can do its job. You'll also manage the full range of service tickets, HVAC troubleshooting, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair, with the expectation that residents get timely follow-up, not just a closed ticket.

Beyond the unit-level work, you're responsible for grounds upkeep, building systems operating at peak efficiency, and keeping everything compliant with local codes and safety standards. You'll build and enforce monthly staffing schedules that guarantee 24/7 emergency coverage. That last part is real: this role carries on-call accountability, and you'll need to staff it accordingly.

Cottonwood also expects you to invest in your team. Hiring, developing, and running both on-the-job and classroom training for techs and community care associates is part of the position, not an afterthought.

What You'll Need to Bring

  • EPA certification Type II or Universal (required, non-negotiable)
  • Considerable hands-on HVAC troubleshooting experience
  • Valid driver's license
  • Demonstrated experience leading a maintenance team, not just working alongside one
  • Attention to detail on make-readies and a track record of hitting turn timelines
  • Comfort running an accident-free operation with a real understanding of safety codes

Cottonwood offers competitive pay with a bonus structure, full health, vision, dental, life, and disability benefits available within the first 30 days, a 401k and HSA with company match, three weeks of PTO in your first year, and 10.5 paid holidays.

One thing worth noting for candidates coming from smaller operations: what separates strong supervisors in this role from average ones is usually the ability to coach without micromanaging. Your techs will handle a lot independently. Your job is to set the standard, check the work, and develop the people, not to be the one always holding the wrench. If you've been the best tech on a team and you're ready to grow into the leadership side of this trade, this is the kind of role that builds that foundation.

greenhouse_5987758004.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5987758004.rewritten_description:
Cottonwood Residential operates The Orchards at Cherry Creek Park in Centennial, and they're looking for a maintenance technician who can handle the full range of day-to-day work that keeps a residential community running well. This isn't a light-duty posting. They need someone with real hands-on experience across plumbing, electrical, and carpentry, plus solid HVAC troubleshooting skills and an EPA Type II or Universal certification already in hand.

The Centennial submarket sits in one of the Denver metro's more competitive corridors, where resident expectations tend to run high. Communities here compete hard on condition and responsiveness, which means your work order turnaround and make-ready quality directly affect how the property performs. A technician who can knock out a tight punch list, handle a turn efficiently, and still respond professionally to a resident after hours is genuinely valuable here.

On-call coverage is part of the job. You'll rotate emergency response with the support of a Service Manager, so you're not fielding calls alone without backup, but you do need to be available and reliable when it's your rotation. That's an honest part of this role worth knowing going in.

A CPO certification isn't required but it's a real advantage if you have it. Valid driver's license is required. Physical demands are significant: lifting up to 50 lbs, working from ladders and scaffolding, and spending most of your shift on your feet or crouching in mechanical spaces.

What Cottonwood is looking for

  • EPA Type II or Universal certification (required)
  • Considerable HVAC troubleshooting experience
  • Working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and general carpentry repairs
  • Ability to work evenings, weekends, and on-call shifts
  • Valid driver's license
  • Strong written and verbal communication in English for work orders, safety procedures, and resident interaction
  • CPO certification (a plus, not a dealbreaker)

Cottonwood offers health, vision, dental, life, and disability coverage with a quick eligibility window. You'd also get a 401k with company match, HSA match, three weeks of PTO in your first year, and 10.5 paid holidays. For maintenance professionals who've dealt with employers that treat benefits as an afterthought, that package is worth noting.

Technicians who grow in this type of role often move into lead tech or service manager positions within a few years, particularly at companies like Cottonwood that promote from within and manage multiple communities. If you're looking to build toward that track, the breadth of work here gives you the reps to get there.

greenhouse_8534456002.title: Maintenance Supervisor
greenhouse_8534456002.rewritten_description:
It's 7:15 a.m. and your team is already pulling work orders from overnight while you're walking the grounds, noting a gate sensor that's lagging and a pool chemical reading that needs adjusting before residents start their day. That's the rhythm of this role. Reactive and proactive, often in the same hour.

The Role at Waypoint

Waypoint Management Services is hiring a Maintenance Supervisor for a high-end luxury community in Miami, FL. This isn't a wrench-turning position with a title attached. You're running the full maintenance operation: budgets, vendors, make-ready timelines, preventive maintenance schedules, and a team that needs real leadership, not just direction.

Day-to-day, you'll own the make-ready process, coordinating with contractors to ensure units are genuinely move-in ready, not just checked off a list. You'll keep HVAC units on a preventive maintenance schedule, manage pool service and chemical readings, maintain the trash compactor, and make sure gated entrances and security equipment stay operational. Work orders get a 24-hour response, full stop. When service emergencies hit, you're the one building the corrective action plan and executing it.

The community manager is your primary partner. Strong communication between maintenance and leasing leadership is what keeps occupancy goals and service standards aligned. You'll need to hold that relationship well.

What You're Bringing

  • Prior supervisory experience leading both employees and outside vendors or contractors
  • EPA Section 608 certification (Types I and II), required at hire
  • CPO certification, or a commitment to earn it within 90 days
  • CAMT certification is preferred and signals a serious maintenance professional
  • Hands-on proficiency in plumbing, carpentry, general repairs, and HVAC troubleshooting
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards and a genuine commitment to safe operations
  • High school diploma or GED, plus at least two years of relevant maintenance experience
  • Bilingual in Spanish and English, strongly preferred given Miami's resident population
  • Willingness to carry an on-call schedule when the property needs it

Waypoint offers medical, dental, and vision coverage starting day one, a 401k with matching, an employee referral and recognition program, bonus opportunities, and an onsite housing discount for qualified employees.

One thing worth noting about this role specifically: the candidates who stand out aren't necessarily the most technically skilled. They're the ones who think about the property's physical condition the way an owner would. They catch the deferred maintenance item before it becomes a capex problem. They build a team that doesn't need to be micromanaged on turnovers because the standard is already understood. If that's how you operate, this is a role worth your attention.

From here, strong Maintenance Supervisors at luxury communities often move into Regional Maintenance Director roles or transition into facilities management at larger portfolios. The leadership and budget accountability you build in this seat transfers directly.
greenhouse_8534456002.description_text: What we offer: Generous employee discount for onsite housing Competitive salary Medical/Dental/Vision benefits on day one 401k (matching) Employee Referral Program Employee Recognition Program Incentive/bonus opportunities High end luxury community Waypoint Management is growing! CAMT Certification preferred Ready to lead a community where people are proud to call home? Apply today and grow your career with Waypoint. We are an equal opportunity employer. greenhouse_4238675009.title: Part-Time Exterior Porter
greenhouse_4238675009.rewritten_description:
It's 7 a.m. on a Saturday. An event wrapped up the night before, and the first tenants and visitors are already moving through the lobby. Your job is to make sure they don't see a trace of what happened the evening before.

Comstock is hiring a part-time Exterior Porter for its ParkX operations in Herndon, VA. This role sits at the front line of the property experience. You're responsible for the condition of common areas across assigned sites: sidewalks, lobbies, restrooms, and public-facing spaces that tenants and guests interact with every day. That means pressure washing, window cleaning, trash removal, and routine janitorial tasks completed on schedule and to standard.

What the Work Actually Involves

You'll report to your assigned location in uniform before your shift starts, follow established cleaning checklists, and document anything that needs management attention. Safety compliance is part of the job, not an afterthought. That includes proper chemical handling, keeping storage areas organized and hazard-free, and flagging maintenance issues or damage when you spot them.

Event support is a real part of this role. Setup and breakdown for events or public gatherings will come up, and availability for weekends and after-hours during peak periods is expected. When a coworker is out, you may be called on to cover. The Senior Operations Manager, Janitorial, directs day-to-day priorities, and you'll attend team meetings that affect janitorial operations.

What Comstock Is Looking For

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Valid driver's license with a clean record
  • Availability for weekend and after-hours work, especially around events
  • Ability to lift up to 50 pounds and handle the physical demands of sustained outdoor and indoor work across variable conditions
  • Strong attention to detail and the ability to self-direct with minimal supervision
  • Professional demeanor with tenants, visitors, and property staff
  • Comfort working independently and as part of a team

Benefits are available and include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) match, and life insurance, though specifics vary by role. Comstock also offers annual professional development funds, free parking, EV charging, and a parental leave program.

One honest note on what separates strong candidates here: it's not just physical ability. The people who do well in exterior porter roles long-term are the ones who communicate proactively. If a piece of equipment is showing wear, if a supply is running low, if something looks like it could become a bigger problem, saying something early matters more than most job descriptions let on. Property teams rely on porters as eyes on the ground, and that situational awareness compounds over time into trust and real opportunity within an organization.
greenhouse_4238675009.description_text: Job Overview The ParkX Porter is responsible for maintaining cleanliness in the common areas at assigned sites. Duties include general upkeep such as pressure washing, window cleaning, trash removal, and routine cleaning tasks. Porter also support event setup and cleanup when necessary and respond to cleanliness concerns as they arise. The position requires reliability, attention to detail, and the ability to work professionally and courteously with property staff, tenants, and visitors. Key Responsibilities Ensures all common areas, including sidewalks, lobbies, restrooms, and public spaces are kept clean and presentable. Completes daily cleaning and janitorial tasks as assigned by the Senior Operations Manager, Janitorial. Pressure washes areas as assigned by management. Reports to assigned location in the approved ParkX Porter uniform. Reports to assigned location before the start of the work shift. Ensures compliance with all safety regulations and cleaning standards. Exhibits a professional and courteous demeanor with all site visitors. Acknowledges, greets, and interacts professionally with property staff and guests, using appropriate protocols. Assists management teams with special cleaning or maintenance requests as needed. Always maintains a neat and orderly janitorial closet and storage areas, free of debris and hazards. Completes any required end-of-shift documentation and ensures proper transfer of responsibilities to the next shift. Helps with setup and breakdown for events or public gatherings when required. Monitors cleaning supply inventory and ensures safe and proper use of cleaning chemicals. Notifies management of any situation that may require their attention. Follows all cleaning schedules and checklists accurately. Understands and is compliant with cleaning procedures and schedules. Documents and reports any safety hazards, maintenance issues, or damages. Maintains a safe, clean, and neat work environment by following standards and procedures. Attends team meetings that impact janitorial operations. Completes maintenance or cleaning tasks according to the company's maintenance checklists. Fills in for coworkers during their absences. Promptly addresses and resolves cleanliness concerns in a first-class manner that is consistent with guidelines established by the ParkX Management Executive Team. Maintains equipment through regular maintenance and preventative care. Reports equipment damage to management. Is available for weekend and after-hours work, particularly during events or peak periods. Understands and promotes corporate goals, core values, and departmental responsibilities detailed in the Company org chart and Employee Handbook. Understands and complies with ParkX’s policies on calls outs, time off, and timeliness. Performs other duties as assigned. Qualifications High School Diploma or Equivalent Valid driver's license without major violations Flexibility to work after hours and weekends for events Outgoing personality with ability to relate to diverse group of people and build/maintain strong relationships Strong problem-solving, time management and communication skills Must be highly credible and trustworthy, and operate with high degree of integrity Must hold oneself and others accountable and strive for a high level of excellence Must have a positive, can-do attitude and be able to fuel growth and innovation Must be customer-focused and results-oriented Must want to continuously learn and develop Exceptional oral communication, active listening, and organizational skills Ability to establish strong working relationships with others in team setting Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks with a strong attention to detail in a fast-paced environment Self-directed and able to work independently, with minimal supervision Physical Demands & Work Environment Required to stand for long periods and walk, climb stairs, balance, stoop, kneel, crouch, bend, stretch, twist, and/or reach Push, pull or lift up to 50 pounds Continuous repetitive motions Work under variable temperature conditions (or extreme heat or cold), under variable noise levels, outdoors/indoors, around fumes and/or hazards, around dust and/or mite hazards, around chemicals and bio-hazards Benefits Compensation: Benefit Package: Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K Match, Life Insurance Career Development - Opportunities for Advancement within our expanding portfolio - Annual Professional Development Funds to fuel your growth Employee Perks (to name a few): - Free Parking and EV charging - Parental Leave Program - Enjoy a Friendly Work Environment that values collaboration *Note benefits vary depending on the function of your role

greenhouse_4238547009.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_4238547009.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a hands-on maintenance technician role at a Berkshire Residential community in Palm Valley. Your day moves fast and rarely follows a script. You're handling service requests across plumbing, electrical, appliances, and carpentry, while also keeping an eye on curb appeal and making sure the property stays in shape between turns. You'll support the shop, manage inventory, and keep equipment and keys where they belong. Berkshire owns and manages its own assets, so there's no third-party buffer between the team and accountability. That matters in how this role actually functions day to day.

Curb appeal is real here. A well-maintained exterior signals to prospects and residents that the property is managed with care, and that starts with the tech who walks the grounds and notices what others miss. You're also a point of contact for residents, so your attitude on a work order visit carries weight. A quick, professional interaction goes a long way toward reducing delinquency headaches down the line by keeping residents satisfied and renewing.

What You Bring

  • At least one year of hands-on maintenance experience in a residential community setting
  • Working knowledge across the core trades: appliances, plumbing, electrical, carpentry, and general building maintenance
  • Ability to communicate clearly in English, written and verbal
  • Comfort with computers and basic property management software for work order tracking
  • A general awareness of safety protocols and willingness to enforce them on site

A Few Things Worth Knowing

Berkshire operates its own portfolio rather than managing for outside owners, which typically means more consistent standards and less of the budget uncertainty that comes with fee management arrangements. The trade-off is that expectations are high and there's real ownership over outcomes. You're not just executing work orders. You're contributing to the physical condition of an asset that Berkshire has a direct stake in.

What separates a strong maintenance tech from an average one at a community like this isn't just technical skill. It's reliability, follow-through on punch list items, and the ability to read urgency correctly. A flooded unit and a dripping faucet are not the same emergency. Techs who triage well and communicate clearly with the office team make everyone's job easier and tend to move up. This kind of role can lead naturally into a lead tech or maintenance supervisor position as you build experience across more complex systems and start mentoring newer staff.

Berkshire does offer a 25% rent discount, three weeks of vacation, and personal development plans, which is worth noting because that package is above average for a maintenance-level role in this industry.

greenhouse_4238544009.title: Sales & Resident Experience Consultant
greenhouse_4238544009.rewritten_description:
The Skills That Drive This Role

Leasing is a numbers game, and the best consultants know it. At Estancia Townhomes, you're expected to close at least 20% of your traffic. That means every tour, every follow-up call, every email thread has a measurable outcome attached to it. If you naturally track your own ratios and ask yourself why a prospect didn't convert, you already think the way this role demands.

The other skill that runs through everything here is communication. Not just being friendly, though that matters. It's the ability to shift your approach depending on who's in front of you, write a follow-up that actually gets a response, and handle a resident concern without letting it spiral. Berkshire calls themselves people-first, and at the leasing desk, that philosophy shows up in real time, every single day.

What You're Actually Doing Day to Day

Your mornings might start with walking your tour route and model units before the first appointment. Punch-list thinking applies here: scuffed baseboard, burned-out bulb, a door that sticks. These details read as signals to prospects about how the property is run, and a sharp leasing consultant treats the model like a product on a shelf.

From there, the day fills in around people. Prospective residents coming in or calling, current residents with questions or concerns, your property manager needing input on a marketing idea. You're also expected to stay current on what competing communities are doing with pricing and concessions, because local market awareness directly shapes how you position Estancia in a conversation.

  • Guide prospects through tours and apply genuine sales technique to move them toward a decision
  • Maintain a closing ratio of at least 20% on qualified traffic
  • Keep leasing office and tour routes professionally presented at all times
  • Handle resident communications with accuracy and a consistent, professional tone
  • Monitor local market conditions and contribute ideas that support occupancy and resident satisfaction
  • Bring two or more years of customer service or sales experience to the role

What Makes Someone Stand Out Here

Two candidates can both be warm and personable. What separates a good leasing consultant from a great one is follow-through. The prospect who toured on Saturday and hasn't responded to your email by Tuesday needs a second touch, a different angle, something that earns a reply. The candidates who thrive at Berkshire are the ones who treat their pipeline with the same attention a sales rep would, not just waiting for the next walk-in but actively working the ones they already have.

Berkshire owns and manages its own properties, which means the leasing team operates with a level of accountability that third-party management setups sometimes dilute. You're not leasing for a client. You're leasing for the owner, which tends to sharpen the feedback loop between your performance and what actually matters to the business.

greenhouse_5209066008.title: Assistant General Manager
greenhouse_5209066008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto's Assistant General Manager role in Cambridge runs on a specific blend of skills: sales instinct, financial discipline, and the kind of interpersonal range that lets you close a lease in the afternoon and coach a leasing consultant through a difficult conversation an hour later. This isn't a back-office position. It's a front-line leadership role where your output shows up in occupancy numbers, delinquency reports, and resident retention rates every single month.

The Skills This Role Leans On Hard

Leasing ability comes first. You're conducting tours, building rapport with prospective residents, and converting traffic into signed leases. That requires genuine curiosity about people, not a scripted pitch. Alongside that, you're handling rent collection, posting receipts, and managing delinquency, so comfort with accounts receivable and basic financial workflows is non-negotiable. When the General Manager is out, you step into that seat. That means the team looks to you for direction, problem resolution, and keeping the property's standards intact, both physically and culturally.

Bozzuto places real weight on community presentation. The interior and exterior condition of the property reflects directly on your work, and you're expected to hold that standard consistently, not just when leadership is watching. Yardi proficiency helps here since tracking financials, work orders, and lease activity all flow through the platform. Social media content creation is also part of the job, specifically Facebook and Instagram, so an eye for what resonates with a residential audience matters more than it might in other AGM roles.

What the Day-to-Day Actually Looks Like

Expect weekend availability one to two weekends per month, with weekday days off as the tradeoff. Prospective residents shop apartments on weekends, and being present for that traffic is part of how you hit leasing goals. On a typical day you might review the delinquency list in the morning, lead a tour or two midday, handle a resident concern that escalated from the leasing team, and circle back with a maintenance team member on a punch list item before end of shift. The variety is real. So is the accountability.

What separates strong AGM candidates at a company like Bozzuto from average ones isn't just leasing skill. It's the ability to hold a high-quality resident experience standard while simultaneously managing the operational details that protect NOI. Operators who treat those two things as competing priorities tend to struggle. The ones who see them as connected tend to move up quickly. The AGM seat is a natural stepping stone to General Manager, and Bozzuto's scale across the mid-Atlantic and Northeast means those opportunities surface regularly.

What You'll Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a leasing or property management environment
  • Hands-on exposure to rent collection, budgets, and accounts receivable
  • Proficiency with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Active presence on social media platforms with an ability to create content that connects
  • Sales aptitude and a track record of following through on commitments to residents and teammates

The salary range for this role is $65,000 to $70,000, with additional bonus potential. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5209232008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5209232008.rewritten_description:
Most maintenance roles at apartment communities are either purely technical or purely supervisory. This one sits at the intersection. Bozzuto is looking for someone who can fix a boiler and then turn around and coach a tech on why the work order documentation matters just as much as the repair itself.

The Assistant Maintenance Manager role at their Cambridge, MA property is built around two things: keeping the asset in excellent condition and developing the habits in yourself and your team that make that consistency possible. You'll handle HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair directly, but you'll also own the make-ready process, manage preventative maintenance schedules, and hold the line on safety codes and OSHA standards. Cambridge winters are real, so snow removal and grounds upkeep are part of the rotation too.

What This Role Actually Requires

Bozzuto wants five or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance. Supervisory experience is preferred, and certifications matter here. An EPA 608, HVAC certification, or NAPE credential will carry weight in the hiring process. Beyond that, comfort with complex mechanical systems like boilers, chillers, central plant, and split systems puts you well ahead of candidates who only know standard in-unit work. Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting round out the skill set.

  • 5+ years in trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • EPA, HVAC, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Experience with complex mechanical systems (boilers, chillers, central plant)
  • Supervisory or team lead experience, even informal
  • Availability for on-call rotation and weekend coverage
  • Ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather conditions

Where This Role Can Take You

The assistant manager seat in maintenance is one of the cleaner stepping stones in property operations. You're building the two things that open doors in this industry: technical credibility and people management experience. Those two together are harder to find than most companies admit. From here, the natural progression is Maintenance Manager, then Director of Facilities or Regional Maintenance Director at a portfolio level. At a company like Bozzuto, which operates across multiple markets and asset classes, internal movement is a real path rather than a talking point.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this specific role is the ability to treat the property like they own it. That means catching problems before they become work orders, not just responding to them. It also means understanding that a slow turn directly affects lease-up momentum and NOI, even if that's not language you use every day on the shop floor. The candidates who get promoted out of this seat are the ones who already think that way.

The salary for this position is $70,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_7896060.title: Housekeeper
greenhouse_7896060.rewritten_description:
What This Role Actually Looks Like Day to Day

Housekeeping at a residential apartment community is different from hotel or commercial cleaning work. You're not just tidying up. You're a visible part of the resident experience, and the condition of the clubhouse, hallways, amenities, and model units directly shapes how people feel about living there. At Weinstein Properties' Bexley Westshore community in Cumming, GA, this role carries real weight.

Weinstein is a family-owned company with more than 70 years of operating history and over 60 communities across the Southeast and mid-Atlantic. That kind of longevity doesn't happen by accident. It comes from investing in the people doing the daily work, including the housekeeping team.

The Work Itself

Your Monday through Friday schedule runs 8am to 5pm, which means predictable hours and no weekend surprises. The work is physical and recurring. You'll clean and maintain common areas, the leasing office, the clubhouse, and amenity spaces. You'll stock and clean the coffee station, handle vacant unit cleaning, and keep model units sharp and fresh for prospective residents. "Freshens" on model homes are a regular part of the rotation, making sure the unit always shows well during traffic hours.

You should know going in: repetition is a real part of this job. The clubhouse gets cleaned every day. Certain tasks cycle on a daily or weekly basis. If you find satisfaction in keeping a space consistently excellent rather than constantly chasing variety, you'll fit here. If you need novelty to stay engaged, this one might not be the right match.

The role also requires genuine physical stamina. Prolonged standing and walking throughout the shift are the norm, not the exception.

What Strong Candidates Bring

  • Reliability. Showing up on time, every day, matters more than almost anything else in this role.
  • Self-direction. A lot of the work happens independently, so you need to manage your own time and priorities without constant oversight.
  • Attention to detail. Residents and prospective residents notice when things are off. You should notice before they do.
  • Willingness to collaborate. Maintenance and leasing teams share the same goal: a well-run, presentable community. When those teams need a hand, being willing to step in matters.
  • A practical attitude toward unpleasant tasks. Dirty work is part of property maintenance. The candidates who thrive here don't flinch at it.

Compensation and Fit

The position starts at $17 per hour with quarterly bonuses built into the structure. There's also a $1,000 stay-on bonus available after 90 days in good standing, which is a straightforward signal that Weinstein wants to keep good people once they find them. Details on the bonus structure are worth discussing directly with the recruiter.

Weinstein's stated approach is to train talent from the ground up rather than hire only for what someone already knows. For someone early in a property management career, that's worth taking seriously. Housekeeping and groundskeeping roles are frequently the entry point into broader maintenance and facilities work. The people who pay attention, build relationships with the maintenance team, and demonstrate reliability often find doors open to them over time.
